-0.0035472,51.541289.name: Stratford
-0.0035472,51.541289.publicNote:
Stratford Station and Stratford International Station are within 8 minutes walk of one another. Trains to Green measures your travel-time from whichever is quickest.
Fun fact: Stratford International is not international and the name makes no sense. It was named such in the hope of summoning the Eurostar there, but the word-magic failed to take effect.

Sights: Cook's Pond Viaduct, [Lingfield Park Racecourse](https://www.lingfieldpark.co.uk), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Lingfield Old Town conservation area, Old Cage, Lingfield Oak, Community Orchard, and Lingfield College. -0.0045745,51.2578327.name: Oxted
-0.0045745,51.2578327.ramblerNote:
**[Oxted Circular via Yalden Spring](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-circular/)**: Wooded Greensand Ridge, heathland common, open parkland, valleys, escarpment views to North Downs and High Weald, and historic market town. Sights: [Limpsfield Common](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/limpsfield-common), [Titsey Estate](https://www.titsey.org), [St Mary's Church](https://www.stmarysheritagetrust.org.uk), [Quebec House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/quebec-house), Squerryes Court, General James Wolfe statue, Sir Winston Churchill statue, Yalden Spring, and Tenchleys Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Grasshopper on the Green](https://grasshopperongreen.com) in Westerham, the [Kings Arms Hotel](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/kent/kings-arms) in Westerham, or the [George & Dragon](https://georgeanddragonwesterham.co.uk) in Westerham. 19 km. 5 hours.

**[Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/)**: Meadows, wooded Greensand Ridge, gently rolling Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old farmsteads and manor houses, flat plain, and nature reserve. Sights: [St Peter's Church](https://www.stpeterslimpsfield.org/history-of-st-peters.html), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Tenchleys Manor, St George's Church, Crowhurst Place, Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Community Orchard, and Lingfield Oak. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Oxted Circular via Titsey Place**: A shorter variant of [Woldingham Circular via Titsey Place](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-circular-via-titsey-place/). North Downs escarpments, plantation paths, open downland, River Eden meadows, and Limpsfield village streets. Sights: [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, St Mary's Church Oxted, and St Peter's Church Limpsfield. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk) in Botley Hill, the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities) in Titsey Place, or the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnlimpsfield.com) in Limpsfield. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**Oxted Circular via Moat Farm**: A shorter variant of [Oxted Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-circular/). Wooded Greensand common, heathland, farmland, Limpsfield Chart village green, and gentler hillside paths. Sights: [Limpsfield Common](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/limpsfield-common), [Titsey Estate](https://www.titsey.org), Moat Farm, and Tenchleys Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Carpenters Arms](https://www.carpentersarmslimpsfield.com) in Limpsfield Chart, or the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnlimpsfield.com) in Limpsfield. 11 km. 3 hours.

**Oxted to Godstone**: An alternative variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Meadows, wooded Greensand Ridge, gently rolling Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, and flat fields alongside railway. Sights: [St Peter's Church](https://www.stpeterslimpsfield.org/history-of-st-peters.html), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), Tenchleys Manor, and St George's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 16 km.

**Oxted Circular via Chartwell**: A longer variant of [Oxted Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-circular/). Wooded Greensand Ridge, heathland common, open parkland, Mariners Hill viewpoints, Chartwell estate woodland, and valleys. Sights: [Limpsfield Common](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/limpsfield-common), [Titsey Estate](https://www.titsey.org), [St Mary's Church](https://www.stmarysheritagetrust.org.uk), [Quebec House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/quebec-house), [Chartwell](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chartwell), [Landemare café](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chartwell/eating-and-shopping-at-chartwell), Squerryes Court, General James Wolfe statue, Sir Winston Churchill statue, and Mariners Hill memorial. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Grasshopper on the Green](https://grasshopperongreen.com) in Westerham, the [Kings Arms Hotel](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/kent/kings-arms) in Westerham, or the [George & Dragon](https://georgeanddragonwesterham.co.uk) in Westerham. 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/)**: Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, farmland fields, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_(town)), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Broxbourne Waterside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broxbourne), [Harold Godwinson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harold_Godwinson), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Waltham Abbey Gardens, Epping Forest District Museum, St Augustine's Church, Waltham Abbey Town Hall, 2012 Olympic Shield, Ancestor sculpture, and Broxbourne Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel) in Waltham Abbey, the Sun Inn in Waltham Abbey, the Crown in Waltham Abbey, the Kalik Coffee House in Waltham Abbey, or the Bake You Happy in Waltham Abbey. 19 km. 4 hours.

**[Broxbourne Circular via New River](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/)**: Canal towpath, parkland, Spital Brook valley, ancient diverse woodland, streams, boardwalks, and green lanes. Sights: New River, Broxbourne Woods National Nature Reserve, Broxbourne Pumping Station, Wormley Wood, and Cowheath Wood. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/) in Wormley West End. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**Broxbourne to Cheshunt**: A shorter variant of [Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/). Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and Epping Forest District Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel) in Waltham Abbey, the Sun Inn in Waltham Abbey, the Crown in Waltham Abbey, the Kalik Coffee House in Waltham Abbey, or the Bake You Happy in Waltham Abbey. 15 km. 3 hours.

**Broxbourne to Bayford**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular/). Lunch at the [Woodman & Olive](http://www.thewoodmanandolive.com/) in Wormley West End. 18 km.

-0.0178578,51.1264031.name: East Grinstead
-0.0178578,51.1264031.ramblerNote:
**[East Grinstead Circular via Tablehurst Farm](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/)**: Hilly Wealden countryside, pasture, woods, ancient houses, and quaint hilltop old town. Sights: Standen, Brambletye House, and Tablehurst Farm. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–Nov. Lunch at the Chequers Inn Hotel in Forest Row, the Java and Jazz Cafe and Pizzeria in Forest Row, the Brambletye Hotel in Forest Row, the Swan in Forest Row, the Hop Yard Brewery in Forest Row, or the Tablehurst Farm Shop in Forest Row. 17 km.

**[East Grinstead to Wivelsfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-to-wivelsfield/)**: Hills, streams, ponds, heaths, mixed woodland rich in bluebells and wood anemones, pastures, and heathery commons with South Downs views. Sights: Weir Wood Reservoir, Broadhurst Manor, Chailey Common, Chailey Windmill, and Bluebell Railway. Mud and flood prone. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk) in Horsted Keynes, the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk) in Horsted Keynes, or the [Sloop Inn](https://thesloopinn.com/) in Scaynes Hill. 31 km. 7.5 hours.

**East Grinstead Circular via Standen**: A variant of [East Grinstead Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/). Short circular through Wealden countryside to National Trust house and garden. Sights: Standen. Can be muddy. Lunch at the National Trust tea room in Standen. 7 km.

**East Grinstead Circular via Brambletye House & Stone Farm Rocks**: A variant of [East Grinstead Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/). Combines shorter morning through historic town and fields with longer afternoon along reservoir and past Standen. Sights: Brambletye House, Weir Wood Reservoir, Stone Farm Rocks, and Standen. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–Nov. Lunch at the Chequers Inn Hotel in Forest Row, the Java and Jazz Cafe and Pizzeria in Forest Row, the Hop Yard Brewery in Forest Row, or the Tablehurst Farm Shop in Forest Row. 18 km.

**East Grinstead Circular via Weir Wood Reservoir**: A longer variant of [East Grinstead Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/east-grinstead-circular/). Weir Wood Reservoir edge, road section, viewpoints, woods, and Wealden countryside. Sights: Weir Wood Reservoir, Stone Farm Rocks, and Standen. Road section with traffic. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Chequers Inn Hotel in Forest Row, the Java and Jazz Cafe and Pizzeria in Forest Row, the Brambletye Hotel in Forest Row, the Swan in Forest Row, the Hop Yard Brewery in Forest Row, or the Tablehurst Farm Shop in Forest Row. 20 km.

**[Forest Row Circular extension](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/forest-row-circular/)**: a one-way walk back to East Grinstead from the Forest Row Circular walk — bus to Forest Row from East Grinstead, then walk back via the main route plus a 5.5 km extension.

**[Cheshunt Circular via Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheshunt-circular/)**: Flat river floodplain, gravel-pit lakes, water meadows, country park sculptures, farmland, and a market town. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Lea Valley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lea_Valley), [Waltham Abbey (town)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_(town)), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Epping Forest District Museum, Abbey Gateway, Waltham Abbey Gardens, Greenwich Meridian Trail, Waltham Abbey Town Hall, and Gunpowder Railway. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Crown in Waltham Abbey, the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel) in Waltham Abbey, the Sun Inn in Waltham Abbey, the Kalik Coffee House in Waltham Abbey, or the Bake You Happy in Waltham Abbey. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Cheshunt Circular via Waltham Abbey**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/). Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, and Waltham Abbey Gardens. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel) in Waltham Abbey, the Sun Inn in Waltham Abbey, the Crown in Waltham Abbey, the Kalik Coffee House in Waltham Abbey, or the Bake You Happy in Waltham Abbey. 10 km. 2 hours.

**Cheshunt Circular via River Lee Country Park**: A shorter variant of [Cheshunt Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cheshunt-circular/). Flat river floodplain, gravel-pit lakes, water meadows, country park, and a market town — omitting the afternoon lake loops. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, Epping Forest District Museum, Abbey Gateway, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre). Can be muddy. **Cheshunt to Broxbourne**: An alternative variant of [Broxbourne Circular via Waltham Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/broxbourne-circular-via-waltham-abbey/). Flat river floodplain, towpaths, water meadows, lakeside paths, woodland strips, orchid area boardwalk, and open parkland. Sights: [River Lee Country Park](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/river-lee-country-park), [Waltham Abbey Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waltham_Abbey_Church), [Church of the Holy Cross](http://www.walthamabbeychurch.co.uk), [Royal Gunpowder Mills](https://www.royalgunpowdermills.com), [Lee Valley White Water Centre](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/lee-valley-white-water-centre), [Broxbourne Waterside](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Broxbourne), Wildlife Discovery Centre, Cornmill Meadows Dragonfly Sanctuary, North Met Pit Orchid Area, Waltham Abbey Gardens, and Epping Forest District Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Angel](https://www.mcmullens.co.uk/local-pub/angel) in Waltham Abbey, the Sun Inn in Waltham Abbey, the Crown in Waltham Abbey, the Kalik Coffee House in Waltham Abbey, or the Bake You Happy in Waltham Abbey. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Godstone to Oxted via Tandridge Yew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/godstone-to-oxted/)**: Low-lying farmland, river valley, woodland, low ridges, golf course, ancient churchyards, and old coaching village. Sights: [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), St George's Church Crowhurst, St Peter's Church Tandridge, Lagham Manor, Greenwich Meridian Trail, Greensand Way, Oxted Mill, and St Silvan's House. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 17 km. 4.5 hours.

-0.0516667,51.2896975.name: Woldingham
-0.0516667,51.2896975.ramblerNote:
**[Woldingham to Oxted via Godstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-to-oxted/)**: Rambler favourite! North Downs woodland ridge, open chalk downland, M25 crossing, village green, Greensand Way farmland, small settlements, and ancient churchyards. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Godstone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godstone), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), [Godstone Vineyard](https://www.godstonevineyards.com), St Mary's Homes, and St Nicholas Godstone. Chalk paths can be slippery. Lunch at the [Hare & Hounds](https://www.hareandhoundsgodstone.co.uk) in Godstone, the [White Hart](https://www.thewhitehartongodstonegreen.com) in Godstone, or the [Bell Inn](https://www.thebellgodstone.co.uk) in Godstone. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Woldingham Circular via Titsey Place](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-circular-via-titsey-place/)**: Rural valleys, North Downs escarpments, beech woods, plantation paths, open downland, hilltop village, and historic garden village. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), Woldingham School, North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, Woldingham Garden Village, Peace Centre, St Paul's Church, and Madeira Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk) in Botley Hill, or the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities) in Titsey Place. 17 km. 5 hours.

**Woldingham to Oxted via Great Church Wood**: A shorter variant of [Woldingham to Oxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-to-oxted/). Rambler favourite! North Downs woodland ridge, South Hawke bridleway, M25 footbridge, open farmland, Greensand Way, ancient churchyard, and golf course. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Tandridge Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/tandridge-churchyard-765), Great Church Wood, and St Peter Tandridge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Old Bell](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/surrey/old-bell) in Old Oxted, the [Haycutter](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/haycutter) in Broadham Green, or the [Barley Mow](https://www.barleymowtandridge.co.uk) in Tandridge. 11 km. 3 hours.

**Woldingham to Oxted via Titsey Place**: A shorter variant of [Woldingham Circular via Titsey Place](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/woldingham-circular-via-titsey-place/). Rural valleys, North Downs escarpments, plantation paths, open downland, River Eden meadows, and market town streets. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, and St Peter's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk) in Botley Hill, the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities) in Titsey Place, or the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnlimpsfield.com) in Limpsfield. 13 km. 3.5 hours.

Rural valleys, North Downs escarpments, plantation paths, open downland, River Eden meadows, and market town streets. Sights: [Marden Park Woods](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/marden-park), [Titsey Place](https://www.titsey.org), North Downs Way, Oxted Downs, Titsey Plantation, Greenwich Meridian, and St Peter's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Botley Hill Farmhouse](http://www.botleyhill-farmhouse.co.uk) in Botley Hill, the [Titsey Place Tea Room](https://www.titsey.org/tea-rooms-and-facilities) in Titsey Place, or the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnlimpsfield.com) in Limpsfield. 13 km. 3.5 hours.

Sights: [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), [Selsdon Wood Nature Reserve](http://www.friendsofselsdonwood.co.uk), [Ledgers Wood](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/ledgers-wood), King's Wood, St Mary the Virgin, Farleigh, Sanderstead to Whyteleafe Countryside Area, South London Downs National Nature Reserve, and Hamsey Green Pond. -0.09193,51.51586.name: City of London
-0.09193,51.51586.publicNote:
If you stand at the Guildhall — where the historic ‘City of London’ is still governed — you’re within 20 minutes walk of seven London terminals: Cannon Street, Fenchurch Street, Liverpool Street, Moorgate, Blackfriars, London Bridge, and Farringdon (well, Farringdon isn’t a terminal, but it is the best-connected station in Greater London).
By default, Trains to Green assumes you’ll start your journey at one of these stations. Switch to your preferred home station in the filter menu.

Lunch at the Fox in Coulsdon Common, or the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/) in Kenley Common. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Riddlesdown to Kingswood**: A longer variant of [Riddlesdown to Coulsdon South](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/riddlesdown-to-coulsdon-south/). Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and extended after-lunch route to Kingswood. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch at the Fox in Coulsdon Common, or the [Wattenden Arms](http://www.thewattendenarmskenley.co.uk/) in Kenley Common.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via the Ouse valley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe/)**: Rolling High Weald hills, woodland, farmland, river valley, golf course, level crossing, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Ouse Valley Viaduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ouse_Valley_Viaduct), [Sussex Ouse Valley Way](http://www.sussexousevalleyway.co.uk/index.htm), and [High Weald Landscape Trail](http://www.highweald.org/home/news/1850-new-online-guide-for-landscape-trail-.html). Can be muddy. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Haywards Heath to Balcombe via Ardingly](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe-via-ardingly/)**: Undulating High Weald farmland, woodland, golf course, reservoir dam wall, fields, and a private school grounds. Sights: Ardingly Reservoir, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly College, Ouse Valley Viaduct, Stone Hall, Balcombe Place, St Peter, Ardingly, Balcombe Lake, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, and High Weald Landscape Trail. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Ardingly Activity Centre Café](https://www.ardinglyactivitycentre.co.uk/our-cafe) in Ardingly Reservoir, the [Ardingly Inn](https://theardinglyinn.co.uk) in Ardingly, the Fellows Bakery in Ardingly, or the Ardingly Café in Ardingly. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Haywards Heath Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-circular-via-lindfield/)**: Low hills, Ouse Valley, woodland, nature reserves, fields, hedgerows, historic villages, and canal-era river crossings. Sights: Scrase Valley Local Nature Reserve, Eastern Road Local Nature Reserve, Sussex Ouse Valley Way, [Bluebell Railway](https://www.bluebell-railway.com), Bluebell Vineyard, Freshfield Lane Brickworks, [Lindfield](http://www.westsussex.info/lindfield.shtml), All Saints Lindfield, Church House, Toll House, and Ouse Navigation. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Sloop Inn](https://www.thesloopinn.com) in Freshfield Bridges. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Finsbury Park to Tottenham Hale**: A variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Urban park, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, riverside path, and open marshland. Sights: [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Springfield Park, Coppermill Tower, and Engine House. Lunch at the Coal House Café in Woodberry Wetlands. 8 km.

**Finsbury Park to Hampstead**: A longer variant of [Highgate to Hampstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/highgate-to-hampstead/). Disused railway trackbed nature reserve, urban green spaces, woods, heathland, parkland, ponds, garden suburb streets, and panoramic hilltops. Sights: [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), [Hampstead Garden Suburb](http://www.hgs.org.uk/history/index.html), [Free Church](https://www.hgsfreechurch.org.uk), [St Jude-on-the-Hill](https://www.stjudeonthehill.com), [Hampstead Heath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hampstead_Heath), [Golders Hill Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golders_Hill_Park), [The Pergola](https://www.historic-uk.com/HistoryMagazine/DestinationsUK/Hampstead-Pergola-Hill-Gardens), [Kenwood House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kenwood), [Burgh House](https://www.burghhouse.org.uk), [Fenton House and Garden](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/fenton-house-and-garden), [St John-at-Hampstead](https://hampsteadparishchurch.org.uk), Highgate Wood, The Archer, Hill Garden, and Parliament Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Old Bull & Bush](https://www.thebullandbush.co.uk) in North End Road, or the Refreshment House in Golders Hill Park. 19 km. 5 hours.

**[Wivelsfield to Haywards Heath via Cuckfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivelsfield-to-haywards-heath/)**: Woods, wildflower meadows, farmland, High Weald ridge, golf course, and suburban road sections. Sights: London Eye, Southbank Centre, National Theatre, Tate Modern, Millennium Bridge, Globe Theatre, Southwark Cathedral, Golden Hinde, Hays Galleria, [HMS Belfast](http://www.iwm.org.uk/visits/hms-belfast), City Hall, [Tower Bridge](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tower_Bridge), Design Museum, [Brunel Museum](http://www.brunel-museum.org.uk/), Surrey Docks Farm, Statue of Peter the Great, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Cutty Sark](http://www.rmg.co.uk/cuttysark), [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), Royal Observatory, Winchester Palace, and Tower of London. Sights: Palace of Westminster, Tate Britain, Battersea Power Station, Battersea Park, London Peace Pagoda, and Fulham Palace. Lunch at the Morpeth Arms in Millbank, the Ship in Wandsworth, or the Rocket in Putney. 7 km.

-0.1269,51.5196.name: London
-0.1269,51.5196.publicNote:
By default, Trains to Green assumes you’ll start your journey at one of London’s 18 termini (Charing Cross, Kings Cross, St Pancras, Euston, Liverpool Street, London Bridge, Waterloo, Waterloo East, Victoria, Paddington, Marylebone, Blackfriars, Moorgate, Cannon St, or Fenchurch St). When you select a rural destination station, the travel-time you see is from whichever London terminus provides the quickest journey. Over time, alternative London starting stations will be added to give you a more tailored experience.

Wherever you start from, journey times are always based on the regular Saturday schedule. If it's Sunday, bank holiday or weekday, or there are works going on, travel times may differ.

Send any comments or questions to [nicolas@niczap.design](mailto:nicolas@niczap.design).
-0.1269,51.5196.privateNote: Obviously your real journey depends on where you live! Choose your closest London National Rail station for a more tailored experience.

-0.137032,51.0555957.name: Balcombe
-0.137032,51.0555957.ramblerNote:
**[Balcombe Circular via Wakehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-wakehurst/)**: Undulating High Weald countryside, wooded hillsides, farm fields, covered footbridge over reservoir valley, permissive parkland paths, and botanic gardens. Sights: [Wakehurst](https://www.kew.org/wakehurst), [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Loder Valley Nature Reserve, Ardingly Reservoir, Millennium Seed Bank, Wakehurst Mansion, and Balcombe Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Gardeners Arms](https://www.gardenersarmsardingly.co.uk) in Little London. 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Balcombe Circular via Ardingly Reservoir](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-ardingly-reservoir/)**: Wealden hills, woods, pasture, hidden farms, quiet lanes, dry tracks, and reservoir edge. Sights: Ouse Valley Viaduct and Ardingly Reservoir. Can be muddy. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch at the Ardingly Activity Centre in Ardingly, the Ardingly Inn in Ardingly, the Ardingly Cafe in Ardingly, or the Gardeners Arms in Ardingly. 16 km.

**[Balcombe to East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-east-grinstead/)**: Gentle wooded hills, muddy farm tracks, landscaped gardens, reservoir shoreline, open fields, and woodland paths. Sights: [Wakehurst Place](http://www.wakehurst.org/visitor/visitwp.html), [Priest House Museum](http://www.sussexpast.co.uk/property/site.php?site_id=17), [St Margaret's Church](http://www.westhoathly.org.uk/), Stone Farm Rocks, Weir Wood Reservoir, Standen House, and Balcombe House. Can be very muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Cat Inn](http://www.catinn.co.uk/) in West Hoathly. 17 km. 5 hours.

**[Balcombe Circular via Nymans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular/)**: Fields, woods, stream valleys, grassy hillsides, estate parkland, and a fine old village. Sights: [Nymans](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-nymansgarden2), Lord Snowdon's lake with Japanese pavilion, St Mary's Parish Church, Slaugham Place, Arch with Roman pillars, River Ouse, and Old Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Red Lion in Handcross, the Welcome Cafe in Nymans, the Riding House in Nymans, the Heritage in Slaugham, the Victory Inn in Staplefield, or the Jolly Tanners in Staplefield. 19 km. 5 hours.

**[Balcombe to Horsham via Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/)**: Wealden woods, farmland fields, streams, ponds, riverside nature reserve, and old market town. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest), [Chesworth Farm Riverside Fields](https://friendsofchesworthfarm.com/), [River Arun](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Arun), [Horsham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horsham), and [Black Jug](http://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/blackjug/). Can be very overgrown. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/) in Plummers Plain. 27 km.

**Balcombe Circular via Wakehurst & St Peter, Ardingly**: A shorter variant of [Balcombe Circular via Wakehurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-wakehurst/). Reservoir perimeter path, scrubland, farm fields, church lane, showground perimeter, permissive parkland paths, and botanic gardens. Sights: [Wakehurst](https://www.kew.org/wakehurst), Ardingly Reservoir, St Peter, Ardingly, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly Showground, Millennium Seed Bank, Wakehurst Mansion, Balcombe Lake, and Queen's Green Canopy. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Gardeners Arms](https://www.gardenersarmsardingly.co.uk) in Little London. 12 km. 3 hours.

**Balcombe Circular via Ardingly**: A variant of [Haywards Heath to Balcombe via Ardingly](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haywards-heath-to-balcombe-via-ardingly/). Undulating High Weald farmland, woodland, reservoir perimeter path, fields, and estate grounds. Sights: Ardingly Reservoir, [South of England Event Centre](https://southofenglandeventcentre.co.uk/events), Ardingly College, Ouse Valley Viaduct, Stone Hall, Balcombe Place, St Peter, Ardingly, and Balcombe Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Ardingly Activity Centre Café](https://www.ardinglyactivitycentre.co.uk/our-cafe) in Ardingly Reservoir, the [Ardingly Inn](https://theardinglyinn.co.uk) in Ardingly, the Fellows Bakery in Ardingly, or the Ardingly Café in Ardingly. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Balcombe Circular via Ardingly Reservoir & Millennium Seedbank**: A longer variant of [Balcombe Circular via Ardingly Reservoir](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-circular-via-ardingly-reservoir/). Hilly woodland paths, farm tracks, bluebell woods, fields, reservoir views, and High Weald valleys. Sights: Ouse Valley Viaduct, Ardingly Reservoir, Wakehurst Place, and Millennium Seedbank. Steep climbs and descents. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Ardingly Activity Centre in Ardingly, the Ardingly Inn in Ardingly, the Ardingly Cafe in Ardingly, or the Gardeners Arms in Ardingly. 19 km.

**Balcombe to Horsham via High Weald**: A shorter variant of [Balcombe to Horsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/balcombe-to-horsham/). Wealden woods, farmland, streams, and direct route to Horsham station. Sights: [High Weald](http://www.highweald.org/), [Nymans Estate](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/nymans), and [St Leonard's Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Leonard%27s_Forest). Can be very overgrown. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf](http://www.thewheatsheafplummersplain.co.uk/) in Plummers Plain. 21 km.

-0.137974,51.315543.name: Coulsdon South
-0.137974,51.315543.ramblerNote:
**Coulsdon South Circular**: An alternative variant of [Riddlesdown to Coulsdon South](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/riddlesdown-to-coulsdon-south/). Ancient commons, open grazing land, bluebell woods, chalk downland, and quiet suburban fringe. Sights: Chaldon Church, Farthing Down, Kenley Common, Coulsdon Common, and Happy Valley. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch at the Fox in Coulsdon Common. 14 km.

**Coulsdon South to Whyteleafe**: An alternative variant of [Whyteleafe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whyteleafe-circular/). Chalk downland, commons, wooded valleys, Happy Valley, airfield perimeter, and restored grassland. Sights: [Farthing Downs](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/farthing-downs), [Kenley Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/kenley-common), [Kenley Airfield](https://www.kenleyrevival.org/content/category/visiting), [Coulsdon Common](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/coulsdon-common), [Ss Peter and Paul, Chaldon](https://www.exploringsurreyspast.org.uk/themes/places/surrey/tandridge/chaldon/chaldon_church_mural), [Riddlesdown](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/the-coulsdon-commons/riddlesdown), Happy Valley, Burial Ground, and Riddlesdown Quarry. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fox](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thefoxcoulsdoncommon#) in Coulsdon Common, or the [Boundary on the Green](https://www.boundaryonthegreenpub.co.uk) in Caterham Barracks. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

-0.1457168,50.9247179.name: Hassocks
-0.1457168,50.9247179.ramblerNote:
**[Hassocks to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-lewes/)**: South Downs chalk grassland ridge, panoramic views to sea and inland, dew ponds, Iron Age fort, wooded climbs, riverside embankment, and historic town. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/), [Old Hamsey Church](http://www.friendsofhamsey.org/history/), Anne of Cleves House, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Lewes Priory, St John Sub Castro, and Bull House. Can be slippery. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Hassocks to Brighton via St Margaret's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/)**: South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, university campus, suburban streets, and seafront. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, Amex Stadium, Kemp Town Racecourse, Brighton Pier, Royal Pavilion, The Lanes, and Brighton College. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Stanmer House in Stanmer, the Stanmer Tea Rooms in Stanmer, the Swan Inn in Falmer, the Falmer Cafe in Falmer South, or the Park Farm Shop in Falmer South. 22 km. 6 hours.

**Hassocks to Falmer**: A shorter variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). South Downs valleys, open chalk downland, ancient woods, parkland, and university campus. Sights: St Margaret's Church, Ditchling Beacon, Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve, Stanmer House, Stanmer Church, University of Sussex, Swan Inn, and St Laurence Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Stanmer House in Stanmer, the Stanmer Tea Rooms in Stanmer, the Swan Inn in Falmer, the Falmer Cafe in Falmer South, or the Park Farm Shop in Falmer South. 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hassocks to Plumpton**: A shorter variant of [Hassocks to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-lewes/). South Downs chalk grassland ridge, dew ponds, wooded descent, farmland paths, and racecourse. Sights: Church of St John the Baptist, [Jill Windmill](http://www.jillwindmill.org.uk), Jack Windmill, Ditchling Beacon, and [Chattri Memorial](http://www.chattri.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton. 13 km.

**Hassocks to Shoreham-by-Sea**: A longer variant of [Hassocks to Upper Beeding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-upper-beeding/). Chalk downland hills, escarpment ridge, quiet road with valley views, farm tracks, nature reserve, and Adur river path. Sights: Wolstonbury Hill, Devil's Dyke, Edburton Hill, Mill Hill Nature Reserve, and Bramber Castle. Lunch at the Wildflower Cafe in Devil's Dyke area, or the Devil's Dyke pub in Devil's Dyke. 20 km.

**Hassocks to Brighton via Clayton Windmills**: An alternative variant of [Hassocks to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hassocks-to-brighton/). Residential paths, woodland, open downland ridge, chalk track along South Downs Way, and windmills. Sights: Clayton Windmills, Jack Windmill, Jill Windmill, Church of St John the Baptist, Butcher's Wood, Keymer Post, and Ditchling Beacon Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Stanmer House in Stanmer, the Stanmer Tea Rooms in Stanmer, or the Swan Inn in Falmer.

**[Highgate to Hampstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/highgate-to-hampstead/)**: Urban green spaces, woods, heathland, parkland, ponds, playing fields, garden suburb streets, and panoramic hilltops. Sights: [Hampstead Garden Suburb](http://www.hgs.org.uk/history/index.html), [Free Church](https://www.hgsfreechurch.org.uk), [St Jude-on-the-Hill](https://www.stjudeonthehill.com), [Hampstead Heath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hampstead_Heath), [Golders Hill Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Golders_Hill_Park), [The Pergola](https://www.historic-uk.com/HistoryMagazine/DestinationsUK/Hampstead-Pergola-Hill-Gardens), [Kenwood House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/kenwood), [Burgh House](https://www.burghhouse.org.uk), [Fenton House and Garden](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/london/fenton-house-and-garden), [St John-at-Hampstead](https://hampsteadparishchurch.org.uk), Highgate Wood, Hill Garden, Parliament Hill, The Archer, and Hampstead Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Old Bull & Bush](https://www.thebullandbush.co.uk) in North End Road, or the Refreshment House in Golders Hill Park. 15 km. 4 hours.

**Highgate to Tottenham Hale**: A shorter variant of [Alexandra Palace to Tottenham Hale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alexandra-palace-to-tottenham-hale/). Disused railway trackbed nature reserve, urban parks, reservoir wetlands, residential streets, and riverside path. Sights: [Parkland Walk](https://www.parkland-walk.org.uk), [Woodberry Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/woodberry-wetlands-nature-reserve), [Walthamstow Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.visitleevalley.org.uk/walthamstow-marshes), [Walthamstow Wetlands](https://www.wildlondon.org.uk/walthamstow-wetlands-nature-reserve), Finsbury Park, Springfield Park, and Coppermill Tower. Lunch at the Finsbury Park Café in Finsbury Park, or the Coal House Café in Woodberry Wetlands. 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Merstham to Tattenham Corner via First World War Centenary Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/)**: Rambler favourite! Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [First World War Centenary Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), Millennium Stones, Inglis Folly, and Langley Vale Wood. Race days restrict access. Lunch at the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com) in Mogador. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Merstham to Tadworth**: A shorter variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). Rambler favourite! Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), and [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets). Race days restrict access. Lunch at the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com) in Mogador. 17 km.

**Merstham to Epsom**: A variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). Rambler favourite! North Downs ridge, parkland, open heathland, racecourse downland, and residential streets into Epsom town centre. Sights: [Gatton Park](https://gattonpark.co.uk), [Reigate Fort](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/reigate-hill-and-gatton-park/features/reigate-fort-at-reigate-hill-and-gatton-park), [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk), [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), The Durdans, and Rosebery Park. Race days restrict access. Lunch at the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com) in Mogador. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Merstham to Redhill via Ss Peter and Paul**: A variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Grassy embankment, wooded open spaces, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, restored grassland, wooded paths, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: Merstham Bund, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, Ss Peter and Paul, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Road noise near M25. Lunch at the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk) in Nutfield Marsh, or the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpublichouse.co.uk) in Nutfield. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Merstham to Redhill via St Mary the Virgin**: A variant of [Redhill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/redhill-circular/). Grassy embankment, wooded open spaces, wetland lakes, restored sand quarries, Greensand Ridge, farm fields, wooded commons, and suburban streets. Sights: Merstham Bund, Spynes Mere Nature Reserve, Mercers Country Park, St Mary the Virgin, Castle Hill, Nutfield Priory, Royal Earlswood Park, Earlswood Common, Upper Earlswood Lake, Redhill Common, and King George VI Memorial. Road noise near M25. Lunch at the [Inn on the Pond](https://www.innonthepond.co.uk) in Nutfield Marsh, the [Whyte Harte Hotel](https://whytehartehotel.co.uk) in Bletchingley, the Lamingtons Tea Room in Bletchingley, or the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionbletchingley.co.uk) in Bletchingley. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Mitcham Junction to Colliers Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mitcham-junction-to-colliers-wood/)**: Flat urban riverside parks, nature reserves, chalk stream, industrial heritage, and south London commons and green corridors. Lunch at the Monk Pub in Hadley Green. 8 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone to Cockfosters via Hadley Green**: A shorter variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, and wooded parkland. Sights: Hadley Green, Monken Hadley Common, Cockfosters tube station, and Trent Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Monk Pub in Hadley Green. 8 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone Circular via Darlands Lake Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, former hayfields, young oak woodland, wooded wetland, village greens, and suburban streets on London's Northern Heights. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve and Darlands Lake. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Three Hammers in Mill Hill, the Summer House in Mill Hill, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com) in Mill Hill. 13 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone Circular via Totteridge Green**: A variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, woodland, Totteridge village, Totteridge Green, and suburban roads. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve and Totteridge Green. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Three Hammers in Mill Hill, the Summer House in Mill Hill, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com) in Mill Hill. 14 km.

**Totteridge & Whetstone to High Barnet**: A longer variant of [Totteridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/totteridge-circular/). Greenbelt meadows, woodland, suburban streets, Hadley Green, and fine houses around a historic battlefield site. Sights: Darlands Lake Nature Reserve, Hadley Green, and Battle of Barnet site. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Three Hammers in Mill Hill, the Summer House in Mill Hill, or the [Rising Sun](http://www.therisingsunmillhill.com) in Mill Hill. 18 km.

**[Wimbledon Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-circular/)**: Urban streets, open common, wooded paths, golf fairways, parkland, and quiet roads. Sights: Wimbledon Common, Cannizaro Park, Wimbledon Park, All England Lawn Tennis Club, and Queen's Mere. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the Windmill Cafe in Wimbledon Common. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Wimbledon to Putney](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-putney/)**: Suburban streets, open common, landscaped park, woodland riverside path, Royal Park grassland, suburban roads, and riverside Thames path. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](https://cannizaropark.com/about-cannizaro-park), [Wimbledon Common](https://www.wpcc.org.uk), [Richmond Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/richmond-park), [Barnes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barnes,_London), [Barnes Common](https://barnescommon.org.uk), [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london), [Oxford and Cambridge Boat Race](https://www.theboatrace.org/the-course), [Beverley Brook Walk](https://www.merton.gov.uk/system/files?file=beverley_brook_walk_web.pdf), Millennium Fountain, Cannizaro Park Aviary, Sunken Garden, Mediterranean Garden, Italian Garden, Rose Garden, Diana and the Fawn statue, Caesar's Camp Iron Age hill fort, Barnes Common Old Cemetery, Craven Cottage, St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Putney, and Gustav Holst blue plaque. Can be muddy. **[Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/)**: Common heathland, woodland, royal deer park, formal gardens, Thames riverside path, and open grassland. Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Cannizaro House](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cannizaro_Park), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), [Southside House](http://www.southsidehouse.com/), [Caesar's Camp](http://www.themodernantiquarian.com/site/2393/caesars_camp_wimbledon.html), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, King Henry VIII Mound, Ham House, Teddington Lock, and Hampton Court Palace. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the Fox and Grapes in Wimbledon Common, the Pembroke Lodge in Richmond Park, or the Roebuck in Richmond Hill. 18 km.

Can be muddy. **Wimbledon to Twickenham**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). Sights: [Cannizaro Park](http://www.cannizaropark.org.uk/index.htm), [Richmond Park](http://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/richmond_park/), Isabella Plantation, Pembroke Lodge, [Marble Hill House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/marble-hill-house/), [Orleans House Art Gallery](https://www.orleanshousegallery.org/), [York House Riverside Garden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Naked_Ladies), and [Eel Pie Island](http://www.eelpieislandartists.co.uk). Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the Fox and Grapes in Wimbledon Common, the Pembroke Lodge in Richmond Park, or the White Swan in Twickenham riverside.

**Wimbledon to Kew Bridge**: An alternative variant of [Wimbledon to Kingston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wimbledon-to-kingston/). -0.2153478,51.4609339.name: Putney
-0.2153478,51.4609339.ramblerNote: **[Putney to Richmond](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/putney-to-richmond/)**: Flat, all-weather Thames riverside paths, tree-lined embankments, wealthy suburbs, semi-rural stretches, historic bridges, and urban parks. Sights: St Mary's Church Putney, All Saints Church Fulham, [Fulham Palace](http://www.fulhampalace.org/), [Hammersmith Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hammersmith_Bridge), [William Morris Society](http://www.williammorrissociety.org/), [Emery Walker Trust](https://www.emerywalker.org.uk/), [Water and Steam Museum](http://www.waterandsteam.org.uk/), [Kew Gardens](https://www.kew.org/), [Syon Park](http://www.syonpark.co.uk/), [Richmond Lock and Footbridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Lock_and_Footbridge), [Richmond Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Richmond_Bridge,_London), and London Wetlands Centre. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch at the [Putney Pantry](http://www.putneypantry.com/) in Putney, or the [White Cross](http://thewhitecrossrichmond.com/) in Richmond. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

-0.2358629,51.2914245.name: Tadworth
-0.2358629,51.2914245.ramblerNote:
**[Tadworth Circular via Headley Heath and Box Hill Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tadworth-via-headley-heath-and-box-hill-circular/)**: Open chalk downland, heathland, mixed woodland, forested hills, escarpment edges, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Headley Heath](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/headley-heath/), [Box Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Box_Hill,_Surrey), White Hill, Juniper Top, and Stane Street. Easy to disorientate. Lunch at the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnheadley.co.uk/) in Headley, the [National Trust Box Hill](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/box-hill) in Box Hill, the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/) in Walton on the Hill, the [Chequers](https://www.chequerspub.co.uk/) in Tadworth, the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/) in Tadworth, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk/) in Tadworth. 19 km.

**Tadworth Circular via Banstead Commons**: A shorter variant of [Merstham to Tattenham Corner](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/merstham-to-tattenham-corner/). Rambler favourite! Open heathland, wooded bridleways, Banstead Commons, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Banstead Commons](https://bansteadcommons.org.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com) in Mogador. 8 km. 2 hours.

**Tadworth to Merstham**: A shorter variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). Heathland, North Downs escarpment with fine views, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Banstead Heath, Tadworth Windmill, Margery Wood, North Downs Way, Colley Hill, Inglis Folly, Memorial Glade, Reigate Fort, Gatton Park, Millennium Stones, Quality Street, and Merstham Village Garden. M25 noise audible. Lunch at the [Sportsman](https://www.thesportsmanmogador.com) in Mogador. 11 km. 2.5 hours.

Village green, Thames riverfront, historic riverside houses, landscape garden, modern riverside developments, riverside park, and palace grounds. Sights: [Chiswick House & Gardens](https://chiswickhouseandgardens.org.uk), Hogarth Tomb-Monument, Craven Cottage, [Fulham Palace](https://www.fulhampalace.org), and [London Wetland Centre](https://www.wwt.org.uk/wetland-centres/london). **Barnes to Putney Bridge via Barnes Common**: A shorter variant of [Beverley Brook](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/beverley-brook/). Commons, brookside paths, and Thames-side esplanade. Sights: Barnes Common and Barn Elms. Lunch at the Café Loris in Barnes, or the [Stag's Head](https://www.stagsheadbarnes.co.uk/) in Barnes. 4 km.

**[Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/)**: Downland, racecourse gallops, WWI memorial woodland and wildflower meadows, heathland, riding stables, and undulating farm tracks. Sights: [Epsom Downs Racecourse](https://www.thejockeyclub.co.uk/epsom/events-tickets), [Langley Vale Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/langley-vale-wood), First World War Centenary Wood, Regiment of Trees, Witness, Jutland Wood, Tadworth Camp, Coal Tax post, Banstead Heath, Tadworth Windmill, and Emily Davison commemorative plaque. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Blue Ball](https://theblueballtadworth.co.uk/food-drink) in Walton on the Hill, the Village Café in Walton on the Hill, or the [Bell](http://www.thebell-therat.co.uk) in Walton on the Hill. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Tattenham Corner to Tadworth**: A shorter variant of [Tattenham Corner Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tattenham-corner-circular/). **Biggleswade to Arlesey**: A shorter variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). **Sandy to Biggleswade**: A shorter variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, woods, riverside paths, and conservation site perimeter. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site](https://www.birdingplaces.eu/en/birdingplaces/united-kingdom/broom-lakes), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Ivel Mill, Biggleswade Common, and Galley Hill hill fort. Can be boggy. Lunch at the [Jordans Mill Riverside Café](https://jordansmill.com/plan-your-visit/whats-on#riverside-cafe) in Jordans Mill. 14 km. 3 hours.

**Sandy to Arlesey via Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site**: A longer variant of [Sandy to Arlesey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandy-to-arlesey/). Flat river meadows, commons, heathland, woods, riverside paths, and market town streets. Sights: [The Lodge](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/the-lodge), [Broom East Wildlife Conservation Site](https://www.birdingplaces.eu/en/birdingplaces/united-kingdom/broom-lakes), [Jordans Mill](https://jordansmill.com), [Henlow Grange](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Henlow_Grange), [Kingfisher Way](https://enjoybiggleswade.co.uk/local-walking-routes), [River Ivel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Ivel), Ivel Mill, Holme Mills, Biggleswade Common, Galley Hill hill fort, Crown Hotel, and River Hiz. Can be boggy. Lunch at the [Crown Hotel](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-crown-hotel-biggleswade) in Biggleswade, the [White Hart](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/bedfordshire/white-hart) in Biggleswade, the [Rosso Lounge](https://thelounges.co.uk/rosso) in Biggleswade, or the [Surfin Cafe](https://www.surfincafe.co.uk) in Biggleswade. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

Sights: [Hogsmill Local Nature Reserve](https://www.epsom-ewell.gov.uk/sites/default/files/documents/residents/venues-open-space-hire/parks-playgrounds/local-nature-reserves/Hogsmill%20Local%20Nature%20Reserve%20information%20June%202016_0.pdf), [Warren Farm](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/warren-farm/), [Nonsuch Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nonsuch_Park), [St. Mary The Virgin](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1277324), [Nonsuch Palace Gallery](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Service Wing Museum](http://www.friendsofnonsuch.co.uk/museum-and-gallery.html), [Whitehall](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/), [St Dunstan's neo-Gothic lychgate](https://historicengland.org.uk/listing/the-list/list-entry/1065676), [Lumley Chapel](http://friendsofwhitehallcheam.co.uk/index.php/136-2/), Nonsuch Mansion House, Packhorse Bridge, and Ewell Spring. Flat royal parkland, ornamental ponds, woodland plantations, and suburban streets; bypasses Home Park. Sights: [Bushy Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park), Diana Fountain, Chestnut Avenue, Water Gardens, Woodland Gardens, and Canadian Totem Pole. Lunch at the [Pheasantry café](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park/pheasantry-cafe) in Bushy Park, the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthoteluk.co.uk) in Hampton Wick, the [Swan](https://www.theswanhamptonwick.co.uk) in Hampton Wick, or the [Foresters Arms](https://www.the-foresters.com) in Hampton Wick. 9 km. 2 hours.

-0.320874,51.1811282.name: Holmwood
-0.320874,51.1811282.ramblerNote:
**[Holmwood to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-reigate/)**: Gently undulating Low Weald countryside, ancient semi-natural woodland, village green, river crossing, and Greensand ridge with views of North Downs and South Downs. Sights: Hammond's Copse Nature Reserve and Reigate Priory. Lunch at the Plough in Leigh. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Holmwood to Gomshall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-gomshall/)**: Broadleaf woods, heathland, sandy bridleways, open valley, fields, and a picturesque Surrey Hills village. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, [Leith Hill Rhododendron Wood](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/leith-hill/features/the-rhododendron-wood-at-leith-hill), Leith Hill Place, Church of St John the Evangelist, Wotton House, Deerleap Wood, Monument to Samuel Wilberforce, Gomshall Mill, and Piney Copse. Can be muddy. Best in May, Oct and Nov. Lunch at the Plough Inn in Coldharbour, or the Wotton Hatch in Wotton. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Leith Hill Tower**: A longer variant of [Holmwood to Shamley Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-shamley-green/). Sandy Greensand Ridge, beech and sweet chestnut woods, heathland, several big climbs and viewpoints, green valleys, and extended route to Guildford. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, Holmbury Hill, and Pitch Hill. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the Heartwork Coffee in Holmbury St Mary, the King's Head in Holmbury St Mary, or the Royal Oak in Holmbury St Mary. 28 km.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Church of St James**: A longer variant of [Gomshall to Guildford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/gomshall-to-guildford/). Extended route combining two walks through Surrey hills, woods, heathland, and riverside paths. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Chinthurst Hill Tower, and Yvonne Arnaud Theatre. Can be muddy. Lunch at the William IV in Albury Heath. 48 km.
-0.320874,51.1811282.publicRamblerNote:
**[Holmwood to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-reigate/)**: Gently undulating Low Weald countryside, ancient semi-natural woodland, village green, river crossing, and Greensand ridge with views of North Downs and South Downs. Sights: Hammond's Copse Nature Reserve and Reigate Priory. Lunch at the Plough in Leigh. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Holmwood to Gomshall](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-gomshall/)**: Broadleaf woods, heathland, sandy bridleways, open valley, fields, and a picturesque Surrey Hills village. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, [Leith Hill Rhododendron Wood](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/leith-hill/features/the-rhododendron-wood-at-leith-hill), Leith Hill Place, Church of St John the Evangelist, Wotton House, Deerleap Wood, Monument to Samuel Wilberforce, Gomshall Mill, and Piney Copse. Can be muddy. Best in May, Oct and Nov. Lunch at the Plough Inn in Coldharbour, or the Wotton Hatch in Wotton. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Leith Hill Tower**: A longer variant of [Holmwood to Shamley Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/holmwood-to-shamley-green/). Sandy Greensand Ridge, beech and sweet chestnut woods, heathland, several big climbs and viewpoints, green valleys, and extended route to Guildford. Sights: Leith Hill Tower, Holmbury Hill, and Pitch Hill. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the Heartwork Coffee in Holmbury St Mary, the King's Head in Holmbury St Mary, or the Royal Oak in Holmbury St Mary. 28 km.

**Holmwood to Guildford via Church of St James**: A longer variant of [Gomshall to Guildford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/gomshall-to-guildford/). Extended route combining two walks through Surrey hills, woods, heathland, and riverside paths. Sights: Church of St James, Albury Park, Chinthurst Hill Tower, and Yvonne Arnaud Theatre. Can be muddy. Lunch at the William IV in Albury Heath. 48 km.

-0.3241359,51.2411653.name: Dorking
-0.3241359,51.2411653.ramblerNote:
**[Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/)**: Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com) in Mickleham, or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham. 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Dorking to Reigate](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-reigate/)**: Mainly level riverside path, sandstone Greensand Way, picturesque Surrey villages, and parkland grounds. Sights: Betchworth Castle, St Michael's Church, and Reigate Priory. Lunch at the [Dolphin Inn](http://www.youngs.co.uk/pub-detail.asp?PubID=387) in Betchworth, the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloak-brockham.co.uk/) in Brockham, or the [Skimmington Castle](http://www.skimmingtoncastle.co.uk/) in Reigate Heath. 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Dorking Circular via Brockham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/deepdene-trail/)**: Restored landscaped gardens, rhododendron woods, surfaced parkland paths, open fields with Downs views, wooded valley trails, village green, and town centre. Sights: [Deepdene Gardens](https://deepdenetrail.co.uk/), Hope Mausoleum, Betchworth Castle, Cotmandene Common, Chat Park, Glory Woods, and Brockham village green. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Inn on the Green in Brockham, the Reading Room Coffee and Cake House in Brockham, or the Taphouse in Brockham. 16 km.

**[Dorking Circular via Denbies Hillside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/)**: North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, wooded ridges, open downland, river valleys, semi-natural woodland reserve, and ponds. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), St John the Evangelist, Hammer Pond, Friday Street, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, St Martin's Church, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Wotton Hatch](https://www.wottonhatch.co.uk) in Wotton. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Norbury Park and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Dorking to Box Hill via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/). Rambler favourite! Vineyard, wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, riverside path along the Mole, and North Downs. Sights: [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Broadwood's Tower, Box Hill Fort, and Swiss Cottage. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com) in Mickleham, or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham. 15 km. 4 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, Norbury Park woodland edge, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, and Stane Street. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham. 16 km.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Betchworth**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, and village greens. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham) in Brockham, or the Royal Oak in Brockham. 16 km.

**Dorking to Box Hill & Westhumble via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: A variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham, or the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com) in Mickleham. 16 km.

**Dorking Circular via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Mole Valley riverside meadows, chalk escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. Sights: Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Christ Church, Brockham, [Deepdene Trail](https://visitmolevalley.co.uk/the-deepdene-trail), Deepdene Terrace, Deepdene Gardens, Hope Mausoleum, St Martin, Dorking, and Mullins Coffee Shop. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham) in Brockham, or the Royal Oak in Brockham. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Dorking Circular via Abinger Roughs**: A longer variant of [Dorking Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-circular/). North Downs chalk escarpment, Greensand Hills, Abinger Roughs mixed woodland and grassland, open hillside, river valleys, and wooded ridges. Sights: [Denbies Hillside](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/denbies-hillside), [Abinger Roughs](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/abinger-roughs-and-netley-park), Wilberforce Memorial, Witch's Broom Tree, Abinger Manor, St James, Abinger, Friday Street, Hammer Pond, Bury Hill Fisheries, The Nower, Greensand Way, Rose Hill Arch, and St Martin's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Abinger Hatch](https://theabingerhatch.co.uk) in Abinger Common. 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble to Leatherhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-leatherhead/)**: Steep chalk hills, open downland, yew and box woodland, grassy ridges with panoramic views, gentle river valley fields, and historic parkland. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Thorncroft Manor, Leopold Salomons Memorial, Norbury Park, Denbies Wine Estate, Box Hill & Westhumble Railway Station, Town Bridge, and King George V Memorial Gardens. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch at the Running Horses in Mickleham, or the King William IV in Mickleham. 11 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble to Merstham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/)**: North Downs ridge, wooded escarpment, open hilltops, undulating ridge paths, rural farmland, parkland, and golf course. Sights: Box Hill, Reigate Hill, Colley Hill, Gatton Park, and Reigate Fort. Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/) in Reigate Hill, or the Feathers in Merstham. 16 km.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/)**: Sustained chalk downland climb, escarpment, disused quarries, Mole Valley riverside meadows, bluebell woods, parkland, and village greens. Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, Swiss Cottage, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham) in Brockham, or the Royal Oak in Brockham. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/)**: North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. **Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham & Norbury Park**: A shorter variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, and Stane Street. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham. 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Polesden Lacey**: A shorter variant of [Effingham Junction to Westhumble](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/effingham-junction-to-westhumble/). Lunch at the Granary Cafe, Polesden Lacey in Polesden Lacey. 13 km.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Headley Heath and Mickleham**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/). North Downs hills, open downland, heathland, woodland edge of Norbury Park, vineyard, and valley paths. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Norbury Park](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Denbies Wine Estate](https://www.denbies.co.uk), St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and Mole Gap Trail. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble to Dorking via Betchworth & Deepdene Trail**: A longer variant of [Box Hill Circular via Betchworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-betchworth/). Chalk downland, escarpment, disused quarries, parkland, Deepdene estate woodland, terraced gardens, and town. **Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Stepping Stones pub**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill to Leatherhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-leatherhead/). Steep chalk hills, box and yew woodland, grassy ridges, rural woods, riverside fields, and a valley return through Norbury Park. Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Norbury Park, and Stepping Stones pub. Steep descents, can be slippery. Lunch at the Running Horses in Mickleham, or the King William IV in Mickleham.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Norbury Park House**: An alternative variant of [Box Hill to Leatherhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-leatherhead/). Sights: Box Hill, Stepping Stones, Juniper Top, White Hill, St Michael & All Angels Church, Norbury Park, Norbury Park House, and Denbies Wine Estate. Steep descents, can be slippery. **Box Hill & Westhumble to Caterham**: A longer variant of [Box Hill to Merstham and Caterham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-to-merstham/). Hilly, multiple ascents. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the [Chalk Hare](http://www.chalkhare.co.uk/) in Reigate Hill.

Sights: [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, Betchworth Quarry and Lime Works, [St Michael, Betchworth](https://www.nationalchurchestrust.org/church/betchworth-st-michael), [Betchworth Castle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Betchworth_Castle), Stepping Stones, Swiss Cottage, and Christ Church, Brockham. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Dolphin](https://www.dolphinbetchworth.com) in Betchworth, the [Inn on the Green](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/brockham) in Brockham, or the Royal Oak in Brockham. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Headley Heath and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/box-hill-circular-via-headley-heath-and-mickleham/)**: North Downs hills, open downland, wooded heathland, grassy spurs, chalk paths, beech woodland, and valley views. Sights: [Headley Heath](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/headley-heath), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Salomons Memorial, Box Hill Fort, St Michael and All Angels, The Gallops, Stane Street, Brockham Quarry and Lime Works, and North Downs Way. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham, or the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com) in Mickleham. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Box Hill & Westhumble Circular via Norbury Park and Mickleham**: A shorter variant of [Dorking to Box Hill via Norbury Park and Mickleham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorking-to-box-hill-via-norbury-park-and-mickleham/). Rambler favourite! Wooded hills, chalk downland, open grassland, Mole valley views, and North Downs. Sights: [Norbury Park Nature Reserve](https://www.surreywildlifetrust.org/nature-reserves/norbury-park), [Box Hill](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/surrey/box-hill), Westhumble Chapel, Broadwood's Tower, and Box Hill Fort. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [King William IV](https://www.thekingwilliamiv.com) in Mickleham, or the [Running Horses](https://www.therunninghorses.co.uk) in Mickleham. 11 km. 3 hours.

Lunch at the London Apprentice in Isleworth. 11 km.

Sights: [Hampton Court Palace](https://www.hrp.org.uk/hampton-court-palace), [Bushy Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park), Tijou Screen, Privy Garden, Long Water, Diana Fountain, Chestnut Avenue, Water Gardens, Lion Gates, Woodland Gardens, Maze, Great Vine, Banqueting House, Great Fountain Garden, Royal Tennis Court, Pond Gardens, and Canadian Totem Pole. Lunch at the [Pheasantry café](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/visit/parks/bushy-park/pheasantry-cafe) in Bushy Park. 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Hampton Court to Hampton Wick**: An alternative variant of [Hampton Court Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampton-court-circular/). Flat royal parkland, lime tree avenues, deer park, ornamental ponds, woodland plantations, and suburban streets. Sights: Oxshott Heath, War Memorial, Black Pond, [Cobham Mill](https://www.cobhammill.org.uk), [Painshill](https://www.painshill.co.uk), St Andrew's Church, Church Stile House, Cobham Park Estate, and St Michael's Chapel. Sights: Oxshott Heath, Black Pond, The Ledges, Esher Common, Arbrook Common, [Prince's Coverts](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Prince%27s_Coverts), [Ashtead Common](http://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/city-commons/ashtead-common/Pages/default.aspx), and [Epsom Common](http://www.epsomcommon.org.uk/Epsom_Common/epsom_common.html). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Star](http://www.baronspubs.com/star/) in Kingston Road, Leatherhead. 18 km.
