-0.8001257,51.0712216.name: Liphook
-0.8001257,51.0712216.ramblerNote: **[Liphook to Haslemere](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/liphook-to-haslemere/)**: Mixed oak, beech and chestnut woods, bridleways, streams, open countryside, horse paddocks, and a final climb into a market town. Sights: Shulbrede Priory, Haslemere Museum, Valewood Park, and Black Down. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Red Lion in Fernhurst, or the Fern (Cafe) at the Pavilion in Fernhurst. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**Sandhurst to Crowthorne**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, gravel-pit lakes, nature reserves, country lanes, mixed woodland, heathland, and pine woods. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, and St James's Church. Lunch at the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com) in Finchampstead, or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk) in Finchampstead. 14 km.

**Sandhurst to Wokingham via Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre**: A variant of [Blackwater to Wokingham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackwater-to-wokingham/). Roads from station, Horseshoe Lake circuit, fields, pine woodland, heathland, golf course, soft fruit farms, and market town. Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Heathlake Nature Reserve, and [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html). Lunch at the [Lake View Café](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk/lakeviewcafe) in Horseshoe Lake. 14 km.

Sights: [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), Ambarrow Court Local Nature Reserve, Wellington College, Heathlake Nature Reserve, [Wokingham](https://www.wokinghamsociety.org.uk/history.html), St James's Church, Wellingtonia Avenue, Wokingham Town Hall, Tudor House, and Rose Street. Lunch at the [Queen's Oak](https://www.thequeensoak.com) in Finchampstead, or the [Greyhound](https://greyhoundfinchampstead.co.uk) in Finchampstead. 22 km.

-0.8085107,51.7522425.ramblerNote: **[Little Kimble to Saunderton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/little-kimble-to-saunderton/)**: Gentle Chilterns hills, beech woods, open stretches, fine views, and undulating but not taxing gradients. Bring a compass. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch at the [Pink and Lily](http://pink-lily.com/) in Parslow's Hillock, the [Plough](http://theplough.pub/) in Lower Cadsden, or the [Whip Inn](http://thewhipinn.co.uk/) in Lacey Green. 17 km. 5 hours.

**[Crowthorne to Blackwater](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/)**: Pine woods, heathland, lakes and flooded gravel pits, riverside meadows, and green belt nature reserves. Sights: [Simon's Wood and Finchampstead Ridges](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/oxfordshire-buckinghamshire-berkshire/simons-wood-and-finchampstead-ridges), [Moor Green Lakes Nature Reserve](https://www.mglg.org.uk), [Horseshoe Lake Activity Centre](https://horseshoelakeactivitycentre.co.uk), Shepherd Meadows Nature Reserve, Wellingtonia Avenue, Martineau Cottages, Devil's Highway, Three Castles Path, and Blackwater Valley Path. Flood risk in places. Lunch at the [Frog and Wicket](https://www.thefrogandwicket.co.uk) in Eversley Cross, or the [Chequers](https://www.thechequerseversleycross.co.uk) in Eversley Cross. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Crowthorne to Sandhurst**: A shorter variant of [Crowthorne to Blackwater via Eversley Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/crowthorne-to-blackwater-via-eversley-cross/). -0.8253668,51.6758974.name: Saunderton
-0.8253668,51.6758974.ramblerNote:
**[Saunderton Circular via Bledlow Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/saunderton-via-bledlow-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Rolling Chiltern Hills, open ridge, secluded valleys, gentle gradients with one or two steep hills, and fine valley views. Sights: Holy Trinity Church, Lyde Gardens, and St Mary's Church. Lunch at the [Lions](http://www.lionsofbledlow.co.uk) in Bledlow, or the [Boot](http://theboot-bledlowridge.co.uk/) in Bledlow Ridge. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Saunderton Circular via West Wycombe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/saunderton-via-west-wycombe-circular/)**: Chiltern woodland, sloping meadows, commons, and a short ridge walk. Sights: [Hughenden Manor](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hughenden), [West Wycombe Caves](https://www.hellfirecaves.co.uk), West Wycombe Park, Dashwood Mausoleum, St Lawrence Church, and Iron Age hill fort. Very muddy in winter. Lunch at the Tea Room at Hughenden Manor in Hughenden, the [Le De Spencer's Arms](http://www.ledespencersarms.co.uk/) in Downley Common, or the [George and Dragon Hotel](http://www.georgeanddragonhotel.com) in West Wycombe. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Saunderton to Princes Risborough**: An alternative variant of [Saunderton via Bledlow Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/saunderton-via-bledlow-circular/). Rambler favourite! Rolling Chiltern Hills, open ridge, and secluded valleys. Sights: Holy Trinity Church and Lyde Gardens. Lunch at the [Lions](http://www.lionsofbledlow.co.uk) in Bledlow. 13 km.

-0.8313789,51.2906158.name: Fleet
-0.8313789,51.2906158.ramblerNote:
**[Fleet Circular via Tweseldown Racecourse](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fleet-circular/)**: Heathland, woodland, military training lands, levada water channel, canal towpath, residential outskirts, and nature reserves. Sights: [Fleet Pond Nature Reserve](http://fleetpond.org.uk/history-of-the-pond), [Basingstoke Canal](https://basingstoke-canal.org.uk), Tweseldown Racecourse, Beacon Hill levada, Zebon Copse Local Nature Reserve, Naishes Lane SANGS, and Basingbourne Heath. MoD closures apply. Lunch at the [Foresters](https://foresters-dining.co.uk) in Fleet, the [Tweseldown](https://www.thetweseldown.co.uk/index) in Tweseldown, the Windmill in Ewshot, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhoundscc.co.uk) in Church Crookham. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Fleet Circular via Fleet Pond Nature Reserve**: An alternative variant of [Fleet Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fleet-circular/). Canal towpath, woodland edge, residential streets, nature reserve, and housing development. Sights: [Fleet Pond Nature Reserve](http://fleetpond.org.uk/history-of-the-pond), [Basingstoke Canal](https://basingstoke-canal.org.uk), Zebon Copse Local Nature Reserve, and Basingbourne Heath. MoD closures apply. Lunch at the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxandhoundscc.co.uk) in Church Crookham. 15 km.

-0.8440858,51.7180454.name: Princes Risborough
-0.8440858,51.7180454.ramblerNote:
**[Princes Risborough to Wendover via Whiteleaf Cross](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/princes-risborough-to-wendover/)**: Rambler favourite! Beech woods, chalk downlands, high ridgeline, nature reserves, escarpments with wide views, and a pleasant old market town. Sights: Whiteleaf Cross, Chequers, Coombe Hill, and Boer War Monument. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Swan in Smokey Row (Great Kimble), or the Plough in Lower Cadsden. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Princes Risborough to Great Missenden](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/princes-risborough-to-great-missenden/)**: Sloping fields, beech woods, hamlets, upmarket farms, cottages, and two short steep hills with fine views. Sights: [Lacey Green Windmill](http://www.laceygreenwindmill.org.uk/), [Roald Dahl Museum and Story Centre](http://www.roalddahlmuseum.org/), and Prestwood Local Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch at the Gate Inn in Bryant's Bottom, or the Polecat Inn in Prestwood. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

-0.859831,51.8312778.name: Aylesbury Vale Parkway
-0.859831,51.8312778.ramblerNote: **[Aylesbury Vale Parkway to Aylesbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aylesbury-vale-parkway-to-aylesbury/)**: Rolling hills, pastures, hay meadows, river valleys, quiet hilltop villages, and a long canal towpath stretch into a historic town centre. Sights: Quarrendon deserted medieval village, Bolebec castle mound, St. Osyth's Well, Grand Union Canal, and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Can be muddy. Avoid midsummer. Lunch at the [Five Elms](http://fiveelmsweedon.com/) in Weedon, the [Bell at Hardwick](http://www.thebellathardwick.co.uk/) in Hardwick, the [White Swan](http://whiteswanwhitchurch.co.uk/) in Whitchurch, or the Royal Oak Inn in Aston Abbotts. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

-0.8681985,51.1812254.name: Bentley
-0.8681985,51.1812254.ramblerNote:
**[Bentley to Farnham via Devil's Jumps](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/)**: Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland ridges, Scots pine, heather, large ponds with swimming, remote commons, and quiet woodland paths and lanes. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, Devil's Jumps, Hankley Common D-Day training site, and Waverley Abbey. MOD closures apply. Lunch at the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/) in Churt, or the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/) in Tilford. 26 km. 6 hours.

**Bentley to Farnham via Frensham Little Pond**: A shorter variant of [Bentley to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/). Rambler favourite! Ancient forests, heathland, Scots pine, large ponds with swimming, woodland paths and lanes, and skipping Devil's Jumps and Hankley Common. Sights: Alice Holt Forest, Frensham Great Pond, and Frensham Little Pond. Lunch at the [Bel and the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-churt.co.uk/) in Churt, or the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/) in Tilford. 19 km.

**Bentley to Farnham via Waverley Abbey**: An alternative variant of [Bentley to Farnham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bentley-to-farnham/). Rambler favourite! Greensand Way, River Wey, optional out-and-back to Waverley Abbey, and cuts steep ascents near Farnham. Sights: Waverley Abbey. Lunch at the [Barley Mow](http://www.thebarleymowtilford.com/) in Tilford.

-0.8824136,51.5111653.name: Shiplake
-0.8824136,51.5111653.ramblerNote:
**[Shiplake to Henley-on-Thames via Shiplake Lock](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shiplake-to-henley/)**: Riverside Thames path, bluebell and beech woods, open agricultural fields, gentle hills, golf course, historic village, and market town riverfront. Sights: Shiplake Lock, Shiplake Church, Shiplake College, St Nicholas Church, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, Friar Park's lodge house, St Mary the Virgin, and Red Lion Hotel. Can flood after rain. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Maltsters Arms in Rotherfield Greys, or the Bottle & Glass Inn in Binfield Heath. 17 km. 5 hours.

**Shiplake to Henley-on-Thames via High Wood**: A variant of [Shiplake to Henley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shiplake-to-henley/). Bluebell woods, quiet lanes, open fields, beech woods, golf course, and historic town riverfront. Sights: High Wood, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, and St Mary the Virgin. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Bottle & Glass Inn in Binfield Heath, or the Maltsters Arms in Rotherfield Greys. 14 km.

**Shiplake Circular**: A variant of [Shiplake to Henley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shiplake-to-henley/). Riverside Thames path, bluebell and beech woods, open agricultural fields, gentle hills, market town, and return riverside towpath. Sights: Shiplake Lock, Shiplake Church, Greys Court, Lambridge Wood, Friar Park, and St Mary the Virgin. Footbridge may be closed. Lunch at the Maltsters Arms in Rotherfield Greys. 21 km.

-0.899422,51.5338451.name: Henley-on-Thames
-0.899422,51.5338451.privateNote:
Trainline says
London Paddington > Twyford > Henley-on-Thames: 49m
CLJ > Victoria > Paddington > Twyford > Henley-on-Thames: 1h46m
-0.899422,51.5338451.ramblerNote:
**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Great Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-great-wood/)**: Rambler favourite! Thames towpath, open riverside meadows, quaint brick-and-flint hamlet, ancient woodland, wooded hillside terraces, parkland estate, and Georgian market town. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, Hambleden Mill, St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden, St Mary the Virgin Church, Fawley, and Fawley Mausoleum. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Stag & Huntsman in Hambleden. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Henley-on-Thames to Pangbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-to-pangbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Thames riverside, ancient streets, broad valleys, beech woods, fields, small villages, alpaca farm, and toll bridge. Sights: St Nicholas' Church, All Saints Church, Bozedown Alpaca Farm, Whitchurch Toll Bridge, and Parish Church of Whitchurch-on-Thames. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Maltster's Arms](http://www.maltstersarmsrotherfield.co.uk/) in Rotherfield Greys, the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionpub.uk/) in Peppard Common, or the [Unicorn](http://www.unicornkingwood.co.uk/) in Kingwood. 19 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Hambleden Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-via-hambleden-circular/)**: Thames riverbank, beech woods, valley farmland, watermeadows, arable fields, and gentle hills. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden, Hambleden Mill, Hambleden Weir, Hambleden Lock, Remenham Hill, and Henley bridge. Best in Nov. Lunch at the Stag and Huntsman in Hambleden, the Hambleden Village Stores in Hambleden, or the Flower Pot Hotel in Aston. 14 km.

**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Rotherfield Greys](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-rotherfield-greys/)**: Pleasant valleys, undulating woodland, bluebell woods, open fields, Thames riverbank, and weir crossing. Sights: Marsh Lock and [Bottle & Glass Inn](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/). Marsh Lock bridge closed. Lunch at the [Bottle & Glass](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/) in Binfield Heath. 15 km.

**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Stonor Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-via-stonor-circular/)**: Rolling grassy hills, hidden valleys, mixed woodland, beech woods, farmland, and panoramic views. Sights: [Stonor Park](http://www.stonor.com), Abandoned Church at Bix Bottom, and George Harrison's former house. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the White Hart in Nettlebed. 23 km.

**[Henley-on-Thames Circular via Turville Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-via-turville-circular/)**: Thames Valley and Chilterns, open valley countryside, attractive woodland, Thames path, and picturesque villages and hamlets. Sights: Stonor House, St Bartholomew's Church, St Mary and the Virgin Church, and Turville windmill. Best in Jun–Aug. 24 km.

**Henley-on-Thames Circular via Great Wood & St Nicholas Church, Remenham**: A variant of [Henley Circular via Great Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-great-wood/). Rambler favourite! Parkland estate, ancient woodland, wooded hillside terraces, quaint brick-and-flint hamlet, weir crossings, and Thames towpath return. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, Hambleden Mill, St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden, St Mary the Virgin Church, Fawley, and St Nicholas Church, Remenham. Can be muddy. Best in Dec–Feb. Lunch at the Stag & Huntsman in Hambleden. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**Henley-on-Thames to Shiplake via Great Wood**: A longer variant of [Henley Circular via Great Wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-great-wood/). Rambler favourite! Thames riverside path extension from Henley to Shiplake station. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden Lock, and St Mary the Virgin Church, Hambleden. Lunch at the Stag & Huntsman in Hambleden.

**Henley-on-Thames to Shiplake via Rotherfield Greys**: A shorter variant of [Henley Circular via Rotherfield Greys](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-circular-via-rotherfield-greys/). Pleasant valleys, undulating woodland, bluebell woods, open fields, and ending at Shiplake station. Sights: [Bottle & Glass Inn](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/) and Baskerville. Lunch at the [Bottle & Glass](https://www.bottleandglassinn.com/) in Binfield Heath. 13 km.

**Henley-on-Thames to Marlow**: An alternative variant of [Henley via Hambleden Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-via-hambleden-circular/). Thames riverbank, beech woods, wooded hills, and valley farmland. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden, Hambleden Mill, and Hambleden Weir. Best in Nov. Lunch at the Stag and Huntsman in Hambleden, or the Hambleden Village Stores in Hambleden. 16 km.

**Henley-on-Thames Circular via Hambleden Circular & Marsh Lock**: A longer variant of [Henley via Hambleden Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/henley-via-hambleden-circular/). Thames riverbank, beech woods, valley farmland, watermeadows, arable fields, gentle hills, and riverside tarmac path. Sights: Temple Island, Hambleden, Hambleden Mill, Hambleden Weir, and Marsh Lock. Best in Nov. Lunch at the Stag and Huntsman in Hambleden, the Hambleden Village Stores in Hambleden, or the Flower Pot Hotel in Aston. 17 km.

-0.9070163,51.2847339.name: Winchfield
-0.9070163,51.2847339.ramblerNote: **Winchfield Circular**: A variant of [Hook to Winchfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hook-to-winchfield/). Canal towpath, parkland estate, rural fields, village lanes, and woodland. Sights: [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Tundry Pond, All Saints Church Dogmersfield, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Exchequer](https://www.exchequercrookham.co.uk) in Crookham Wharf, or the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheadpub.co.uk) in Dogmersfield. 13 km. 3 hours.

-0.9071791,50.8481981.name: Southbourne
-0.9071791,50.8481981.ramblerNote:
**[Southbourne to Emsworth](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southbourne-to-emsworth-via-thorney-island/)**: Flat coastal sea wall, tidal harbour, salt marsh, sand dunes, beach, mudflats, tidal lake, and historic village high street. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Pilsey Island RSPB Reserve, Brook Meadow Local Nature Reserve, and [Chichester Harbour](http://www.conservancy.co.uk). Check tide timings. Lunch at the [Traveller's Joy](http://www.travellersjoyemsworth.co.uk/) in Southbourne, the [Lord Raglan](http://www.lord-raglan-emsworth.co.uk/) in Emsworth, the [Ship Inn](http://www.shipinnemsworth.co.uk/) in Emsworth, the [Driftwood Cafe](http://www.driftwood-cafe.co.uk/) in Emsworth, the [Greenhouse Cafe](http://greenhousecafe.co.uk/) in Emsworth, the [Coal Exchange](http://www.thecoalexchange.co.uk/) in Emsworth, the [Blue Bell](http://www.bluebellinnemsworth.co.uk/) in Emsworth, the [Crown Inn](http://www.thecrownemsworth.com/) in Emsworth, or the [Railway Inn](http://www.therailwayemsworth.co.uk/) in Emsworth. 15 km.

**[Southbourne to Chichester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southbourne-to-chichester/)**: Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, light woodland, duck pond, and pedestrianised historic city centre. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham, Holy Trinity Church, Fishbourne Roman Palace, Bishop's Palace Gardens, Chichester Cathedral, Pallant House Gallery, Market Cross, and Festival Theatre. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch at the Old House at Home in Chidham, the Anchor Bleu in Bosham, the Shoreside Cafe in Bosham, the Bosham Walk Arts & Crafts Centre café in Bosham, or the Cumberland Gallery coffee shop in Bosham. 18 km. 5 hours.

**Southbourne to Bosham**: A shorter variant of [Southbourne to Chichester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southbourne-to-chichester/). Flat tidal harbour shoreline, raised sea walls, mudflats, fields, and quaint harbourside village. Sights: Parish Church of St Mary, Chidham and Holy Trinity Church. Can be muddy. Best at high tide. Lunch at the Old House at Home in Chidham, the Anchor Bleu in Bosham, or the Shoreside Cafe in Bosham.

-0.9417209,51.0065237.ramblerNote:
**[Petersfield to Rowlands Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-rowlands-castle/)**: Undulating ramble through western South Downs, gentle hills, hilltop views, and farmland. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Five Bells in Buriton, or the Red Lion in Chalton. 16 km. 5 hours.

**[Petersfield to Liss via Edward Thomas Memorial Stone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-liss/)**: Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, steep hillside ascent, unspoilt farmland, and quiet villages. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill, Edward Thomas Memorial Stone, All Saints' Church, Steep, Petersfield Market Square, and Equestrian Statue of King William III. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch at the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/) in Hawkley. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Petersfield Circular**: A shorter variant of [Petersfield to Liss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-liss/). Steep hillside ascent of Shoulder of Mutton Hill, gentle gradients, one short steep downhill section, and wooded hangers. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill and Edward Thomas Memorial Stone. Steep descent, slippery winter. Lunch at the [Harrow Inn](http://www.harrow-inn.co.uk/) in Steep. 10 km. 3 hours.

**Petersfield to Liss via Shoulder of Mutton Hill**: A shorter variant of [Petersfield to Liss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-liss/). Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, and shortcut via Pococks Lane and Upland Lane. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill. Steep ascent, slippery winter. Lunch at the [Hawkley Inn](http://hawkleyinn.co.uk/) in Hawkley. 11 km.

**Petersfield to Alton**: A longer variant of [Petersfield to Liss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petersfield-to-liss/). Rolling Hampshire countryside, wooded hangers, villages, open farmland, and the Alton Circular afternoon route. Sights: Shoulder of Mutton Hill. Very long walk. 23 km.

-0.957274,50.8924696.name: Rowlands Castle
-0.957274,50.8924696.ramblerNote:
**[Rowlands Castle Circular via Stansted House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-circular/)**: Park- and woodland, steep lonely valleys, open fields, hamlets, beech avenue, and South Downs escarpment views. Sights: Stansted House, Stansted Forest, and South Downs Way. Steep climbs and descents. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Victoria Inn](https://victoriainnwestmarden.com/) in West Marden, or the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakhooksway.co.uk/) in Hooksway. 25 km. 6 hours.

**[Rowlands Castle Circular via Stansted House and Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-grand-circular/)**: South Downs ridge, steep climbs, yew tree woods, open hilltops, escarpments, ancient burial mounds, farmland, hedged lanes, and quiet hamlet tracks. Sights: [Stansted House and Park](https://www.stanstedpark.co.uk/), St Mary Stoughton, [Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingley_Vale_National_Nature_Reserve), [Devil's Humps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil%27s_Humps,_Stoughton), [Beacon Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beacon_Hill,_West_Sussex), [Uppark House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/uppark-house-and-garden), [Harris Well](http://www.smuggling.co.uk/gazetteer_se_19.html), [Red Lion Inn](http://redlionchalton.co.uk/), St Michael's Church Chalton, and [St Hubert's Church](http://www.hampshire-history.com/st-huberts-church-idsworth/). Steep climbs throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](http://royaloakhooksway.co.uk/) in Hooksway, or the [White Horse](https://www.thewhitehorse.co.uk/) in South Harting. 37 km.

**Rowlands Castle Circular via Pen Hill**: A longer variant of [Rowlands Castle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-circular/). Narrow wood-fringed valley, main South Downs escarpment, undulating South Downs Way, and far views from Pen Hill and Beacon Hill. Sights: Pen Hill, Beacon Hill, South Downs Way, Stansted House, and Stansted Forest. Steep climbs and descents. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Victoria Inn](https://victoriainnwestmarden.com/) in West Marden, or the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakhooksway.co.uk/) in Hooksway. 29 km.

**Rowlands Castle to Petersfield**: A shorter variant of [Rowlands Castle Grand Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rowlands-castle-grand-circular/). South Downs ridge, steep climbs, yew tree woods, open hilltops, escarpments, farmland, and heath near Petersfield. Sights: [Stansted House and Park](https://www.stanstedpark.co.uk/), St Mary Stoughton, [Kingley Vale National Nature Reserve](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kingley_Vale_National_Nature_Reserve), [Devil's Humps](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devil%27s_Humps,_Stoughton), [Beacon Hill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Beacon_Hill,_West_Sussex), and [Uppark House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/uppark-house-and-garden). Steep climbs throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](http://royaloakhooksway.co.uk/) in Hooksway, or the [White Horse](https://www.thewhitehorse.co.uk/) in South Harting. 33 km.

Sights: [Bartley Heath](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/hook-common-and-bartley-heath), [Basingstoke Canal](https://www.canalguide.co.uk/canals/basingstokcanal.html), [Odiham Castle](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/odihamcastle), [Odiham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Odiham), [Dogmersfield Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dogmersfield_Park), Greywell Tunnel, St Mary the Virgin, Greywell Mill, Pest House, All Saints Church, Odiham High Street, Tundry Pond, Hunting Lodge, Old Thatch Cottage, and St Mary the Virgin Winchfield. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Newlyns Farm Shop & Café](https://www.newlyns-farmshop.co.uk/visit-us) in Lodge Farm, the [Mill House](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/millhouse) in North Warnborough, the Fox & Goose in Greywell, the [Bel & the Dragon](https://belandthedragon-odiham.co.uk) in Odiham, the [Red Lion](https://www.redlionodiham.co.uk) in Odiham, or the [Waterwitch](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hampshire/waterwitch) in Odiham. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

-0.9671299,51.1518972.name: Alton
-0.9671299,51.1518972.ramblerNote:
**[Alton Circular via Church St Mary, East Worldham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alton-circular/)**: Wide upland fields, beech hangers, hidden pastures, wooded commons, gentle downland ridges, and quiet Hampshire villages. Sights: Church St Mary, East Worldham, Gilbert White's House, Selborne Common, Zigzag Path, Chawton House, Jane Austen's House, St Nicholas's Church, All Saints Church, Upper Farringdon, and Massey's Folly. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Three Horseshoes in East Worldham, the Selborne Arms in Selborne, the White's Cafe in Selborne, the Rose & Crown in Upper Farringdon, the Cassandra's Cup in Chawton, or the Greyfriar in Chawton. 20 km.

**Alton Circular via Jane Austen's House**: A shorter variant of [Alton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/alton-circular/). Direct route via Upper Farringdon, gentle downland, quiet villages, and Jane Austen's Chawton. Sights: Jane Austen's House, Chawton House, All Saints Church, Upper Farringdon, and Massey's Folly. Lunch at the Rose & Crown in Upper Farringdon, the Cassandra's Cup in Chawton, or the Greyfriar in Chawton. 12 km.

-1.0356438,51.3720595.name: Mortimer
-1.0356438,51.3720595.publicNote: [Mortimer to Bramley, via Roman Silchester](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902544251): Perhaps an idiosyncratic love of mine, as this isn't a popular hiking spot, but I find it exceptionally charming. This route takes you through what little remains of the once-thriving Roman city of Calleva Atrebatum, including the original walls, and a large amphitheatre where gladiatorial combats entertained up to 7,000 people at a time. The Cavella Arms in Silchester is a perfect stop for lunch, but note that the menu is limited on Sundays. Adapted from *The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East*, 3rd edition.
-1.0356438,51.3720595.privateNote:
Trainline says 47m — Paddington via Reading
Previous hikes: 17 may 2025
-1.0356438,51.3720595.ramblerNote:
**[Mortimer to Aldermaston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mortimer-to-aldermaston/)**: Gentle farmland, clear stream, Roman earthworks and walls, Scots pine woods, gorse and birch commons, and river weirs. Sights: St Mary's Church, Stratfield Mortimer, Calleva Amphitheatre, Roman Walls of Calleva, St Mary the Virgin Church, Silchester, Iron Age Settlement, Kennet & Avon Canal Trust Information Centre, and Padworth Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Calleva Arms in Silchester Common. 15 km. 4 hours.

**Mortimer to Midgham**: A shorter variant of [Mortimer to Aldermaston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mortimer-to-aldermaston/). Gentle farmland, Roman earthworks, pine woods, commons, river weirs, and short canalside towpath. **[Pangbourne Circular via Church of St John the Baptist](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-circular/)**: River Thames valley, wooded hillsides, chalk nature reserve, open fields, quiet lanes, and pretty villages. Sights: Whitchurch Toll Bridge, St Mary's Church, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Great Chalk Wood, Church of St John the Baptist, Modern Artists Gallery, George Hotel, and Elephant Hotel. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Swan in Pangbourne, or the Greyhound in Whitchurch. 13 km. 4 hours.

**[Pangbourne Circular via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/)**: Part-wooded ridges, open pastures, wildflower nature reserve, Thames river meadows, beech woods, and Thames-side villages. Sights: Whitchurch Bridge, Hartslock Nature Reserve, Hartslock Wood, Goring Gap, Mapledurham Estate, and [YHA Streatley](http://www.yha.org.uk/hostel/streatley). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Sun Inn](https://www.thesunwhitchurchhill.co.uk/) in Whitchurch Hill, the [Catherine Wheel](http://www.tcwgoring.co.uk/index) in Goring-on-Thames, the [Chocolate Café](https://www.thechocolatecafeclub.co.uk/goring/) in Goring-on-Thames, the [Miller of Mansfield](https://themillerofmansfield.com/) in Goring-on-Thames, the [Pierreponts Café](http://pierreponts.co.uk/) in Goring-on-Thames, or the [Swan at Streatley](https://www.coppaclub.co.uk/theswanatstreatley/) in Streatley. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Pangbourne Circular via Whitchurch Toll Bridge**: A shorter variant of [Pangbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-circular/). **Pangbourne to Tilehurst**: A longer variant of [Pangbourne via Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pangbourne-via-goring-circular/). -1.1087807,50.7967035.name: Portsmouth Harbour
-1.1087807,50.7967035.ramblerNote: **[Portsmouth Harbour to Havant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/portsmouth-to-havant-via-hayling-island/)**: Flat coastal walk, seafront promenade, sandy beaches, short ferry crossing, disused railway path, and harbourside shore path. Sights: Spinnaker Tower, Southsea Castle, South Parade Pier, Lumps Fort, Eastney Batteries, Fort Cumberland, [Hayling Island Branch Line](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hayling_Island_Branch_Line), and St Nicholas Chapel. Lunch at the [Spice Island](https://www.greeneking.co.uk/pubs/hampshire/spice-island) in Old Portsmouth, the [Ferry Boat Inn](http://www.theferryboatinnhaylingisland.co.uk) in Hayling Island, the [Inn on the Beach](http://www.innonthebeach.co.uk) in Hayling Island, the [Ship Inn](http://www.shiplangstone.co.uk) in Langstone, or the Royal Oak in Langstone. 20 km.

-1.1331195,51.5211885.name: Goring & Streatley
-1.1331195,51.5211885.ramblerNote:
**[Goring & Streatley Circular via Bell Inn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular/)**: Undulating downs, Thames-side views, Ridgeway path, chalk hills, and quaint villages. Sights: [St Mary's Church Aldworth](http://www.aldworthvillage.org/index.php/st-marys-church/the-giants/), [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/), and Streatley Warren. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/) in Aldworth, or the [Four Points Inn](https://www.thefourpointsinn.co.uk/) in Aldworth. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Goring & Streatley Circular via Hill Bottom](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular-via-hill-bottom/)**: Rolling Chilterns countryside, beech forest trails, quiet country lanes, and three pubs en route. Sights: Pierreponts. Muddy after rain. Best in Apr–May. 18 km.

**Goring & Streatley Circular via St Mary's Church Aldworth**: A shorter variant of [Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular/). Undulating downs, hillier start over Lough Down, shorter afternoon return, and Thames-side views. Sights: [St Mary's Church Aldworth](http://www.aldworthvillage.org/index.php/st-marys-church/the-giants/). Steep climbs. Lunch at the [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/) in Aldworth. 12 km.

**Goring & Streatley Circular via Streatley Warren**: A longer variant of [Goring Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/goring-circular/). Ridgeway path extended to Streatley Warren, chalk downland, nature reserve, and Thames views. Sights: Streatley Warren and [St Mary's Church Aldworth](http://www.aldworthvillage.org/index.php/st-marys-church/the-giants/). Seasonal path closure. Best in Oct–Mar. Lunch at the [Bell Inn](https://www.facebook.com/The-Bell-Inn-Aldworth-102844626417649/) in Aldworth. 19 km.

**Goring & Streatley Circular via Beetle and Wedge**: A variant of [Cholsey to Goring](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cholsey-to-goring/). Circular route via Moulsford and finishing with a southerly stretch along the Thames. Sights: [Beetle and Wedge](http://www.beetleandwedge.co.uk/). 20 km.

-1.1367025,51.4022099.name: Aldermaston
-1.1367025,51.4022099.ramblerNote:
**[Aldermaston to Midgham via Stanford Dingley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aldermaston-to-woolhampton-via-stanford-dingley/)**: Undulating West Berkshire Downs, ancient woodlands, commons, fields, chalk stream valleys, heathland, canal towpath, and landscaped parkland. Sights: Bucklebury Common, Bucklebury Estate, Avenue of Oaks, Kennet & Avon Canal, and Midgham Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Six Bells](http://www.thesixbells.co.uk/) in Beenham, the [Blackbird Cafe](http://www.theblackbirdcafe.co.uk/) in Chapel Row, Bucklebury, the [Bladebone at Bucklebury](http://www.thebladebone.com/) in Chapel Row, Bucklebury, the [Old Boot](http://www.oldbootinnstanforddingley.co.uk/) in Stanford Dingley, the [Bull Inn](https://thebullinnpub.com/) in Stanford Dingley, the [Bucklebury Farm Café](https://buckleburyfarm.co.uk/cafe) in Bucklebury, or the [Cottage Inn](http://www.thecottageinnupperbucklebury.co.uk/) in Upper Bucklebury. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Aldermaston to Midgham via Frilsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aldermaston-to-woolhampton-via-frilsham/)**: Mildly undulating West Berkshire Downs, ancient woodlands, commons, chalk stream valleys, water meadows, heathland, landscaped park, and canal towpath. Sights: Bucklebury Common, Avenue of Oaks, Kennet & Avon Canal, and Midgham Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Six Bells](http://www.thesixbells.co.uk/) in Beenham, the [Blackbird Cafe](http://www.theblackbirdcafe.co.uk/) in Chapel Row, Bucklebury, the [Bladebone at Bucklebury](http://www.thebladebone.com/) in Chapel Row, Bucklebury, or the [Pot Kiln](http://www.potkiln.org/) in Frilsham. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Aldermaston to Midgham via Frilsham & Grimsbury Castle**: A longer variant of [Aldermaston to Woolhampton via Frilsham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/aldermaston-to-woolhampton-via-frilsham/). Undulating ancient woodlands, heathland, chalk stream valleys, and Iron Age hillfort. Sights: Grimsbury Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fox Inn](http://www.hermitage-fox.co.uk/) in Hermitage, or the White Horse of Hermitage in Hermitage. 27 km.

**[Nottingham to East Midlands Parkway via Trent Bridge](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/car-free-walks/120-car-free-train-routes.html)**: Canal, river, wooded track, and roadside footpath. Sights: Trent Bridge, West Bridgford, Clifton Hall, Ratcliffe Power Station, and River Trent. Short stretch on busy A453. Lunch at the Barton in Fabis farm shop and cafe. 17 km. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/204%20Nottingham%20to%20Parkway%20South%20Bank.gpx).

Sights: Nottingham Canal, Beeston Canal, River Trent, Trentlock, and Sawley Cut. Lunch at the refreshments in Trentlock and Sawley Cut. 22 km. [GPX file](https://ramblers-leicester.org.uk/images/CF%20Train%20Routes/101%20Nottingham%20to%20Parkway%20Station.gpx).

**Botley to Netley (reverse walk)**: An alternative variant of [Netley to Botley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/netley-to-botley/). Farmland, woodland, riverside causeway with marinas and mudflats, stony beach, and coastal country park. Sights: Manor Farm and Country Park, St Bartholomew's Church, Strawberry Trail, Warsash Ferry, Universal Marina and Boatyard, Banana Wharf, Mermaids at Universal, Hamble Common, and Netley Chapel. Check tide timings. Lunch at the Harper's Steakhouse (formerly The Navigator) in Lower Swanwick, the Boat House Cafe and Restaurant in Lower Swanwick, the Banana Wharf in Lower Swanwick, or the Mermaids at Universal in Lower Swanwick. 15 km.

**Botley to Sholing (reverse walk with extension)**: A longer variant of [Netley to Botley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/netley-to-botley/). Farmland, woodland, riverside causeway, stony beach, coastal country park, shoreline tarmac path, and wooded valley following the Itchen Way. Sights: Manor Farm and Country Park, St Bartholomew's Church, Warsash Ferry, Hamble Common, Netley Chapel, Netley Castle, Westwood Woodland Park, Cruise Liner Terminal, and Itchen Way. Check tide timings. Lunch at the Harper's Steakhouse (formerly The Navigator) in Lower Swanwick, the Boat House Cafe and Restaurant in Lower Swanwick, the Banana Wharf in Lower Swanwick, or the Mermaids at Universal in Lower Swanwick. 20 km.

Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), [St Nicholas, Freefolk](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-nicholas-freefolk.html), Bere Mill, Laverstoke Mill, Laverstoke House, Laverstoke Park, Overton Mill, Southington Mill, St Mary, Overton, Harroway, Town Mill, Fulling Mill, St Mary, Tufton, Whitchurch Town Hall, Voters Cottage, Berehill House, and Manor Cottages. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Watership Down Inn](https://www.watershipdowninn.com) in Freefolk, the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk) in Whitchurch, or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com) in Whitchurch. 17 km. 4 hours.

Lunch at the Palm Brasserie in Deane, the [Fox](http://www.thefox.org) in North Waltham, or the Wheatsheaf in North Waltham. 18 km.

-1.3277563,52.0605712.name: Banbury
-1.3277563,52.0605712.ramblerNote:
**[Banbury Circular via Milton Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/banbury-circular/)**: Gentle farmland, fields, wooded boundaries, villages, quiet lanes, and canal. Sights: St Mary's Church Bloxham and Milton Church. Map reading required. Lunch at the Horse & Jockey in Bodicote, the Plough in Bodicote, the [Marlstone Tavern](https://piepub.co.uk) in Milton, the [Elephant & Castle](https://www.hooky.co.uk/our_pubs/elephant-and-castle-in-bloxham/) in Bloxham, the [Joiners Arms](https://thejoinersarmsbloxham.co.uk/) in Bloxham, or the [Red Lion](https://www.the-red-lion-at-bloxham.co.uk) in Bloxham. 17 km.

**Banbury to King's Sutton**: A longer variant of [Kings Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/). Open fields, Cotswold villages, long views, and canal towpath from Banbury. Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch at the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/) in Aynho, the [Fox Inn](http://www.thefoxatsouldern.co.uk/) in Souldern, the [Blackbird Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/NTH/153/blackbird-croughton) in Croughton, or the [Rose & Crown](http://theroseandcrowncharlton.co.uk/) in Charlton. 25 km.

**Banbury Circular via St Michael's Church Barford St. Michael**: A longer variant of [Banbury Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/banbury-circular/). Gentle farmland, fields, wooded boundaries, market town, villages, quiet lanes, and canal. Sights: St Michael's Church Barford St. Michael, St Mary's Church Bloxham, and Market Place Deddington. Map reading required. Lunch at the Horse & Jockey in Bodicote, the Plough in Bodicote, the [Deddington Arms Hotel](http://www.deddington-arms-hotel.co.uk/) in Deddington, the [Red Lion](https://redliondeddington.co.uk/) in Deddington, the [Unicorn Inn](https://www.unicorndeddington.co.uk/) in Deddington, the [Elephant & Castle](https://www.hooky.co.uk/our_pubs/elephant-and-castle-in-bloxham/) in Bloxham, the [Joiners Arms](https://thejoinersarmsbloxham.co.uk/) in Bloxham, or the [Red Lion](https://www.the-red-lion-at-bloxham.co.uk) in Bloxham. 29 km.

-1.3387418,51.2376461.name: Whitchurch
-1.3387418,51.2376461.ramblerNote:
**[Whitchurch to Andover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whitchurch-to-andover/)**: Open Hampshire countryside, dismantled railway embankment, riverside paths along River Test, thatched-cottage village, concreted forest tracks through Harewood Forest, farmland, and suburban approach into Andover. Sights: All Hallows Church, Whitchurch Silk Mill, St Mary's Church Tufton, Longparish Upper Mill, Church of St Nicholas, Britwell Priors, Harewood Forest, St Mary's Church Andover, Lower Guildhall, and Andover Museum. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Cricketers Inn in Longparish, or the Plough Inn in Longparish. 19 km. 5 hours.

**Whitchurch Circular via River Test**: A shorter variant of [Overton to Whitchurch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/overton-to-whitchurch/). Upper Test valley, water meadows, mill-streams, riverside paths, and quiet lanes. Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), Bere Mill, Fulling Mill, and Whitchurch Town Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk) in Whitchurch, or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com) in Whitchurch. 11 km. 2.5 hours.

**Whitchurch Circular via St Nicholas, Freefolk**: An alternative variant of [Overton to Whitchurch](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/overton-to-whitchurch/). Upper Test valley, water meadows, mill-streams, parkland, riverside paths, and quiet lanes. Sights: [River Test](https://www.fishingbreaks.co.uk/chalkstream/test.htm), [Whitchurch Silk Mill](https://whitchurchsilkmill.org.uk), [All Hallows Church](https://www.hantswhit.church/all-hallows), [St Nicholas, Freefolk](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-nicholas-freefolk.html), Bere Mill, Laverstoke Mill, Laverstoke House, Laverstoke Park, St Mary, Tufton, Fulling Mill, and Whitchurch Town Hall. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Watership Down Inn](https://www.watershipdowninn.com) in Freefolk, the [White Hart Hotel](https://www.whiteharthotelwhitchurch.co.uk) in Whitchurch, or the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarmswhitchurch.com) in Whitchurch. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

-1.3417907,50.8750016.name: Netley
-1.3417907,50.8750016.ramblerNote: **[Netley to Botley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/netley-to-botley/)**: Coastal country park, stony beach, light woodland, mudflats, riverside causeway with marinas, open farmland, and village lanes. Sights: Royal Victoria Country Park, Netley Chapel, Hamble Common, Warsash Ferry, Universal Marina and Boatyard, Manor Farm and Country Park, and St Bartholomew's Church. Check tide timings. Lunch at the Harper's Steakhouse (The Navigator) in Lower Swanwick, the Boat House Cafe in Lower Swanwick, the Banana Wharf in Lower Swanwick, or the Mermaids at Universal in Lower Swanwick. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

-1.3726319,51.8247081.name: Hanborough
-1.3726319,51.8247081.publicNote: It's an easy walk from the station to the beautiful, vast (and free) grounds of Marlborough House, the baroque pile gifted by Queen Anne to her favourite Sarah Churchill. For a somewhat extortionate fee you can also visit the house, but you'd probably want a whole day for that.
-1.3726319,51.8247081.ramblerNote: **[Hanborough to Charlbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hanborough-to-charlbury/)**: Rolling Oxfordshire farmland, landscaped parkland, lakeside paths, open fields, ancient hedgerows, Roman road, Cotswold stone villages, and deer park. Sights: Blenheim Palace, Column of Victory, Great Bridge, Cornbury Park, St Mary the Virgin Church, and Charlbury Railway Station. Can be muddy. Best in Sep. Lunch at the White Horse in Stonesfield, or the Plough Inn in Finstock. 22 km. 6 hours.

-1.4457051,51.4026132.name: Kintbury
-1.4457051,51.4026132.privateNote:
LON Trainline says: 1h5m — Paddington via Newbury
CLJ trainline: 1h57 CLJ>VIC>PAD>NBY>KIT
-1.4457051,51.4026132.ramblerNote:
**[Kintbury to Bedwyn via Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kintbury-to-great-bedwyn/)**: Quiet woods and pastures, long open ridge with panoramic views, forest walk, and canalside village. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, Kennet and Avon Canal, and Bedwyn Brail Roman Villa. Can be muddy. 21 km. 6.5 hours.

**Kintbury to Bedwyn via Inkpen Hill**: A shorter variant of [Kintbury to Great Bedwyn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kintbury-to-great-bedwyn/). Quiet woods and pastures and ridge walk shortened by descending to Shalbourne valley earlier. Sights: Inkpen Hill, St Mary's Great Bedwyn, and Kennet and Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Plough Inn](http://www.ploughshalbourne.co.uk/) in Shalbourne. 16 km.

**Kintbury Circular Walk with Shortcut**: A shorter variant of [Kintbury to Great Bedwyn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kintbury-to-great-bedwyn/). Ridge walk to Inkpen Common and shortened loop cutting out Hampstead Park and canal section. Sights: Inkpen Hill, Walbury Hill, and Inkpen Common. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown and Garter](https://www.crownandgarter.co.uk/) in Inkpen. 17 km.

**Kintbury Circular Walk**: A variant of [Kintbury to Great Bedwyn](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kintbury-to-great-bedwyn/). Ridge walk to Walbury Hill, descent via Inkpen Common, villages, parkland, and canal towpath. Sights: Inkpen Hill, Walbury Hill, Inkpen Common, Hampstead Park, and Kennet and Avon Canal. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown and Garter](https://www.crownandgarter.co.uk/) in Inkpen, or the [White Hart](https://www.saviourwhitehart.co.uk/) in Hampstead Marshall. 23 km.

-1.4621381,53.3783713.name: Sheffield
-1.4621381,53.3783713.ramblerNote:
**[Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/)**: Urban streets, landscaped Victorian cemetery, municipal parkland, narrow wooded valley, open moorland, rugged gritstone edges, and a scenic descent into lush valley. Sights: [Victorian General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), [Endcliffe Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endcliffe_Park), [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), Burbage Rocks, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/) in Fulwood, Sheffield, the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/) in Ringinglow, or the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw) in Longshaw. 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Sheffield to Dore and Totley**: A shorter variant of [Sheffield Circular via Porter and Gleadless](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-circular-via-porter-and-gleadless/). Ancient escarpment woodlands, wooded valleys, moorland fringe, panoramic views, and urban parks. Sights: [Sheffield General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), Endcliffe Park, [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), [Ecclesall Woods](http://www.friendsofecclesallwoods.org.uk/about/ecclesall), and Whirlow Brook Park. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Endcliffe Park Café in Sheffield, the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/) in Fulwood, Sheffield, the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/) in Ringinglow, Sheffield, the Shelter Café in Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield, or the [Summer House](http://www.thesummerhousesheffield.com/) in Dore, Sheffield.

**Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge & Carl Wark**: A variant of [Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/). Urban streets, wooded valley, open moorland, rocky tors, gritstone edges, and valley descent. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw) in Longshaw.

Lunch at the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/) in Fulwood, Sheffield, the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/) in Ringinglow, or the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw) in Longshaw. 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Sheffield to Dore and Totley**: A shorter variant of [Sheffield Circular via Porter and Gleadless](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-circular-via-porter-and-gleadless/). Ancient escarpment woodlands, wooded valleys, moorland fringe, panoramic views, and urban parks. Sights: [Sheffield General Cemetery](http://gencem.org/), Endcliffe Park, [Porter Valley](https://www.fopv.org.uk/), [Peak District](http://www.peakdistrict.gov.uk/), [Ecclesall Woods](http://www.friendsofecclesallwoods.org.uk/about/ecclesall), and Whirlow Brook Park. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Endcliffe Park Café in Sheffield, the [Forge Dam Café](https://forgedamcafe.co.uk/) in Fulwood, Sheffield, the [Norfolk Arms](http://norfolkarms.com/) in Ringinglow, Sheffield, the Shelter Café in Whirlow Brook Park, Sheffield, or the [Summer House](http://www.thesummerhousesheffield.com/) in Dore, Sheffield.

**Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge & Carl Wark**: A variant of [Sheffield to Bamford via Stanage Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sheffield-to-bamford-via-stanage-edge/). Urban streets, wooded valley, open moorland, rocky tors, gritstone edges, and valley descent. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, and Stanage Edge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw) in Longshaw.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Shirley Holms**: A shorter variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, heathland, and coastal saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch at the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com) in Chequers Green. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Lymington Pier to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: An alternative variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). River estuary path, wet woodland, Iron Age hill fort, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: [Walhampton Monument](https://burrardneale.wordpress.com/history-of-the-walhampton-monument), [Lymington Reedbeds](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/lymington-reedbeds-nature-reserve), [Buckland Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Buckland_Rings), [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Wet woodland sections. Lunch at the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk) in Keyhaven. 21 km. 4.5 hours.

Coastal path, salt marshes, nature reserve, esplanade, low eroding cliffs, and then inland road walking to station. Sights: Hurst Castle, [Hordle Cliff](https://wessexcoastgeology.soton.ac.uk/hordle.htm), and [Beachcomber Café](http://www.beachcomber-cafe.co.uk/). Eroding cliff edges. Lunch at the [Gun Inn](http://www.theguninn.com/) in Keyhaven, the [Marine](http://www.themarinemos.co.uk/) in Milford-on-Sea, or the Needles Eye Café in Milford-on-Sea. 18 km.

-1.5466785,51.0338154.name: Mottisfont & Dunbridge
-1.5466785,51.0338154.ramblerNote:
**[Mottisfont & Dunbridge to Romsey via War Memorial Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mottisfont-to-romsey/)**: Rolling chalk hills, ancient and bluebell woodlands, river meadows, managed farmland, and a picturesque market town. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrew's Church, Romsey Abbey, King John's House, Tudor Cottage, War Memorial Park, and Sadler's Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Old Kitchen Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) in Mottisfont, the [Coach House Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) in Mottisfont, the [Mill Arms](http://www.millarmsdunbridge.co.uk/) in Dunbridge, the [Kimbridge Tea Rooms and Café](https://www.idealcollection.co.uk/venues/kimbridge-barn/) in Kimbridge, or the [Bear and Ragged Staff](http://www.bear-and-ragged-staff.co.uk/) in Kimbridge. 22 km. 5 hours.

**Mottisfont & Dunbridge Circular**: A variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, river meadows, and Test Valley chalk stream. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) and St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/) in King's Somborne, or the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/) in Houghton. 25 km.

**[Mottisfont & Dunbridge to Romsey via War Memorial Park](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/mottisfont-to-romsey/)**: Rolling chalk hills, ancient and bluebell woodlands, river meadows, managed farmland, and a picturesque market town. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrew's Church, Romsey Abbey, King John's House, Tudor Cottage, War Memorial Park, and Sadler's Mill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Old Kitchen Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) in Mottisfont, the [Coach House Café](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) in Mottisfont, the [Mill Arms](http://www.millarmsdunbridge.co.uk/) in Dunbridge, the [Kimbridge Tea Rooms and Café](https://www.idealcollection.co.uk/venues/kimbridge-barn/) in Kimbridge, or the [Bear and Ragged Staff](http://www.bear-and-ragged-staff.co.uk/) in Kimbridge. 22 km. 5 hours.

**Mottisfont & Dunbridge Circular**: A variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, river meadows, and Test Valley chalk stream. Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont) and St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/) in King's Somborne, or the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/) in Houghton. 25 km.

**[Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/)**: Landscaped parkland, mixed woodland, open heathland, farm fields, coastal saltmarsh embankments, and sailing town. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), [Lymington](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lymington), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, Lymington Yacht Haven, and Royal Lymington Yacht Club. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequersinnlymington.com) in Chequers Green. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Brockenhurst to Lymington Town via Hurst Castle**: A longer variant of [Brockenhurst to Lymington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/brockenhurst-to-lymington/). Landscaped parkland, woodland, heathland, farm fields, coastal marshes, shingle spit, and saltmarsh embankments. Sights: New Zealand Cemetery, St Nicholas Church, Brockenhurst Park, [Roydon Woods Nature Reserve](https://www.hiwwt.org.uk/nature-reserves/roydon-woods-nature-reserve), Roydon Manor, Shirley Holms, [Hurst Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/hurst-castle), Hurst Point Lighthouse, Fort Albert, Keyhaven Bird Observatory, The Pans & Avon Water Wetlands, [Lymington–Keyhaven Marshes](https://www.hants.gov.uk/thingstodo/countryside/finder/lymingtonkeyhaven), Salt Boiling Houses, Moses Dock, [Lymington Sea Water Baths](https://www.lymingtonseawaterbaths.org.uk), and Lymington Yacht Haven. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Gun Inn](https://www.theguninn.co.uk) in Keyhaven. 25 km. 5.5 hours.

-1.5988642,51.379545.ramblerNote: **[Bedwyn Circular via Savernake Forest and Marlborough](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bedwyn-circular-via-savernake-forest-and-marlborough/)**: Ancient forest with veteran oaks and beeches, chalk plateau, rolling downland, dry and wet valleys, undulating pastures, landscaped parkland, river meadows, dismantled railway line, and historic market town. Sights: [Chatsworth House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), Curbar Edge, Birchen Edge, Hunting Tower, Nelson's Monument, and Chatsworth Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Jolly's Coffee & Cake in Curbar, the [Robin Hood Inn](https://www.robinhoodbaslow.co.uk/) in Baslow, the [Carriage House](https://www.chatsworth.org/) in Chatsworth Estate, the [Parlour](https://www.chatsworth.org/) in Chatsworth Estate, or the [Cavendish](https://www.chatsworth.org/) in Chatsworth Estate. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

Sights: [Chatsworth House](https://www.chatsworth.org/), Curbar Edge, White Edge, Big Moor, and Chatsworth Park. More exposed, hilly. Lunch at the Jolly's Coffee & Cake in Curbar, the [Robin Hood Inn](https://www.robinhoodbaslow.co.uk/) in Baslow, the [Carriage House](https://www.chatsworth.org/) in Chatsworth Estate, the [Parlour](https://www.chatsworth.org/) in Chatsworth Estate, or the [Cavendish](https://www.chatsworth.org/) in Chatsworth Estate. 26 km.

-1.628532,51.9024116.name: Kingham
-1.628532,51.9024116.ramblerNote:
**[Kingham to Charlbury via Bruern Abbey](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury/)**: Gentle rolling hills, riverside meadows, woods, fields, Cotswold stone villages, and bridleways shared with horse riders. Sights: Bruern Abbey, Shipton under Wychwood, and Ascott under Wychwood. Can be very muddy. Best in Apr, Jun, Jul and Aug. 17 km.

**[Kingham to Moreton-in-Marsh via Adelstrop](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-moreton-in-marsh-via-adelstrop/)**: Fields near river Evenlode, quiet tarmac lanes, flower-filled meadows, gentle hills, and picturesque Cotswolds villages and churches. Sights: St Nicholas Church, Adelstrop village and station sign, [St Mary Magdalene Church](http://www.britainexpress.com/counties/glouces/churches/Adlestrop.htm), [Chastleton House](http://www.oxfordshirecotswolds.org/things-to-do/attractions/chastleton-house-p51773), and St Mary's Church, Chastleton. Can be boggy. Best in Jun and Aug. Lunch at the [Fox Inn](https://thefoxatoddington.com/) in Lower Oddington, or the [Horse and Groom](https://www.horseandgroomcotswolds.co.uk/) in Upper Oddington. 17 km. 5 hours.

**[Kingham to Charlbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kingham-to-charlbury-long/)**: Peaceful linear walk through Cotswold countryside, fields, lanes, and villages. Lunch at the Various in Burford, or the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswanswinbrook.co.uk/) in Swinbrook. 34 km.

Sights: [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), St. Andrews Church, and Broughton Down Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Black Horse](http://theblackhorsewesttytherley.com/) in West Tytherley, the [Greyhound Inn](https://greyhoundbroughton.co.uk/) in Broughton, the Tally Ho! Inn in Broughton, the [Boot Inn](http://thebootinn-houghton.co.uk/) in Houghton, or the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownks.co.uk/) in King's Somborne. 27 km. 6.5 hours.

-1.6417904,52.3960838.ramblerNote: **[Berkswell Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-853)**: Rural countryside, farmland, fields, farm driveways, quiet lanes, and village green. -1.6521354,53.3259212.name: Hathersage
-1.6521354,53.3259212.ramblerNote:
**[Hathersage Circular via Stanage Edge Higger Tor and Padley Gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular-via-stanage-edge-higger-tor-and-padley-gorge/)**: Green valley, gritstone escarpment edge, open moorland plateaus, ancient hill fort, narrow wooded gorge with tumbling waterfalls, and quiet river pastures. Sights: Stanage Edge, North Lees Hall, Brontë Cottage, Camp Green, Higger Tor, Carl Wark, Longshaw Estate, Padley Gorge, and Padley Chapel. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw) in Longshaw, or the [Longshaw Tea Room](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/longshaw-burbage-and-the-eastern-moors/features/eating-and-shopping-at-longshaw) in Longshaw. 20 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Hathersage Circular via Bretton Clough and Eyam](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular/)**: Rolling hills, drystone walls, meadows, deep wooded valleys, open moorland, heathery blanket peat bog, fast-flowing streams, and mining heritage spoil heaps. Sights: Carl Wark, Higger Tor, Highlow Brook, Bretton Clough, Eyam, Eyam Moor, Wet Withens Bronze Age stone circle, Eyam Moor Barrow, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/shops/hathersage). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Barrel Inn](https://barrelinn.co.uk/) in Bretton, the [Queen Anne Inn](http://queenanneinn.co.uk/) in Great Hucklow, or the [Bulls Head Inn](https://thebullsheadfoolow.co.uk/) in Foolow. 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Hathersage to Bamford via Derwent Edge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/)**: Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, plantation woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Derwent Edge, Hordron Edge Stone Circle, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com) in Bamford. 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Hathersage to Grindleford**: A shorter variant of [Hathersage Circular via Stanage Edge Higger Tor and Padley Gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-circular-via-stanage-edge-higger-tor-and-padley-gorge/). Green valley, gritstone escarpment edge, open moorland plateaus, ancient hill fort, and narrow wooded gorge with tumbling waterfalls. Sights: Stanage Edge, North Lees Hall, Brontë Cottage, Higger Tor, Carl Wark, Longshaw Estate, Padley Gorge, Padley Chapel, and Grindleford Station Café. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fox House Inn](http://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/yorkshire/thefoxhouselongshaw) in Longshaw, or the [Longshaw Tea Room](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/longshaw-burbage-and-the-eastern-moors/features/eating-and-shopping-at-longshaw) in Longshaw. 14 km.

**Hathersage to Bamford via Stanage Edge**: A shorter variant of [Hathersage to Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/). Gritstone edges, open moorland, pastures with views, ancient oak woods, reservoir shore, and Hope Valley. Sights: Stanage Edge, Ladybower Reservoir, Bamford Edge, and Ashopton Viaduct. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com) in Bamford. 20 km.

**Hathersage to Bamford via Hordron Edge Stone Circle**: A variant of [Hathersage to Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hathersage-to-bamford-via-bamford-and-hordron-edges/). Open moorland, prehistoric cairns, stone circles, standing stones, pathless sections, and Moscar and Bamford Moors. Sights: Hordron Edge Stone Circle and Ladybower Reservoir. Avoid Ground Nesting Season. Avoid Mar–Jul. Lunch at the [Ladybower Inn](https://www.ladybowerinn.com) in Bamford.

-1.6729068,52.2952126.name: Hatton
-1.6729068,52.2952126.ramblerNote: **[Hatton Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1238)**: Canal towpath, tunnel sections, narrow path sections, low bridges, and Warwickshire countryside. Sights: [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), Church of St. Mary the Virgin, [Baddesley Clinton](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/baddesley-clinton), [The Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hay Wood](https://www.forestryengland.uk/hay-wood), and Shakespeare Hall. Narrow path sections. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Shrewley Village Store in Shrewley, the Canal Shop and Moorings in Kingswood Junction, the Lapworth Village Shop in Lapworth, the Navigation Inn in Kingswood, the Punchbowl in Kingswood, the Boot Inn in Kingswood, the [Tom O' the Wood](https://www.tomothewood.co.uk) in Rowington, or the [Durham Ox](https://www.durhamoxshrewley.co.uk) in Shrewley. 8 km. 2.5 hours.

-1.6894024,53.3391287.name: Bamford
-1.6894024,53.3391287.ramblerNote:
**[Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/)**: Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, upland peat bog, and scenic ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Upper Derwent Valley reservoirs, Lose Hill, and [Old Nag's Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/). Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/) in Hope. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge & Hollins Cross**: A shorter variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, and shorter ridgeline descent to Edale. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Lose Hill, and Hollins Cross. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/) in Hope. 13 km.

**Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge & Brown Knoll**: A longer variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, upland peat bog on Brown Knoll, gritstone escarpments, and descent via Jacob's Ladder. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Brown Knoll, and Jacob's Ladder. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/) in Hope. 25 km.

-1.6963369,52.2772189.ramblerNote: **[Claverdon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-816)**: Gently rolling farmland, small pockets of woodland, Grand Union Canal towpath, and motorway bridge crossing. Sights: [St Michaels Church](https://www.churchesofarden.org.uk/claverdon), Pinley Abbey, [Grand Union Canal](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), [Hatton Locks](https://www.canalrivertrust.org.uk), and [Hatton Estate](https://www.hattonworld.com). Nettles and brambles. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownclaverdon.co.uk) in Claverdon, or the [Durham Ox](https://www.durhamoxshrewley.co.uk) in Shrewley. 4 km. 1.5 hours.

-1.6997231,52.4286358.ramblerNote: **[Hampton-in-Arden Circular Walk](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-850)**: Ancient village, tarmacked lanes, River Blythe crossing, nature reserve pools and hides, packhorse bridge, and open countryside. Sights: [Marsh Lane Nature Reserve](https://www.packingtonestate.co.uk), [St. Mary & St. Bartholomew](https://www.hamptoninardenchurch.org.uk), and [Hampton Manor](https://www.hamptonmanor.com). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [White Lion](https://www.thewhitelioninn.com) in Hampton-in-Arden, the [Hampton Corner Shop](https://www.hamptoncornershop.com) in Hampton-in-Arden, the [Sandwiches @ No.6](https://www.facebook.com/sandwichesatnumbersix) in Hampton-in-Arden, or the [Peel's at Hampton Manor](https://www.hamptonmanor.com) in Hampton-in-Arden. 5 km. 1.5 hours.

-1.7002657,51.9921819.name: Moreton-in-Marsh
-1.7002657,51.9921819.ramblerNote:
**[Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Longborough Opera House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/)**: Gentle Cotswold hills, open fields, ridge with fine views, and honey-coloured villages. Sights: Longborough Opera House and Blockley. Lunch at the [Coach and Horses Inn](http://coachnhorseslongborough.co.uk) in Longborough. 20 km.

**[Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Chipping Campden](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/)**: Gentle rolling Cotswold hills, sloping fields, honey-coloured villages, picturesque valleys, and dramatic dips. Sights: Chipping Campden and Blockley. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://www.eightbellsinn.co.uk/) in Chipping Campden. 25 km.

**[Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via the Escarpement](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular-via-the-escarpment/)**: Cotswold hills, escarpment, open fields, bucolic villages, racehorse training facility, Cotswold stone quarry, and long-grass paths in summer. Sights: Redesdale Market Hall, Curfew Tower, Stanway Watermill, Stanway House, Snowshill Manor, Monarch's Way, and Cotswold Way. Can be overgrown. Lunch at the [Plough Inn](http://theploughinnford.co.uk) in Ford, or the [Mount Inn](http://themountinn.co.uk) in Stanton. 42 km.

**Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Broad Campden**: A shorter variant of [Moreton-in-Marsh Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/). Gentle Cotswold hills, rolling fields, and honey-coloured villages including Paxford and Broad Campden. Sights: Broad Campden and Blockley. Lunch at the [Churchill Arms](https://www.churchillarms.co/) in Paxford, or the [Bakers Arms](https://www.bakersarmscampden.com/) in Broad Campden. 20 km.

**Moreton-in-Marsh Circular via Dover's Hill**: A longer variant of [Moreton-in-Marsh Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/moreton-in-marsh-circular/). Gentle rolling Cotswold hills, sloping fields, honey-coloured villages, escarpment views, and Dover's Hill. Sights: Dover's Hill, Chipping Campden, and Blockley. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://www.eightbellsinn.co.uk/) in Chipping Campden. 28 km.

-1.7163038,52.1949449.name: Stratford-upon-Avon
-1.7163038,52.1949449.ramblerNote: **[Stratford-upon-Avon Circular](https://api.warwickshire.gov.uk/documents/WCCC-1615347118-1237)**: Medieval market town, canal basin, riverside path, parks and gardens, and historic streets. Sights: [Shakespeare's Birthplace](https://www.shakespeare.org.uk), New Place, [Shakespeare's Schoolroom & Guildhall](https://www.shakespearesschoolroom.org), Royal Shakespeare Theatre, Swan Theatre, [Holy Trinity Church](https://www.stratford-upon-avon.org), [Bancroft Gardens](https://www.visitstratforduponavon.co.uk), Gower Memorial, Shakespeare Memorial Fountain, Hall's Croft, Guild Chapel, and Nash's House. Lunch at the [Dirty Duck](https://www.greeneking-pubs.co.uk/pubs/warwickshire/dirty-duck/) in Stratford-upon-Avon, or the [Shakespaw Cat Cafe](https://www.shakespawcatcafe.com) in Stratford-upon-Avon. 4 km. 1.5 hours.

-1.7296279,53.3459832.name: Hope

**[Hope to Hathersage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/)**: Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and scenic river descent. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Speedwell Cavern, Treak Cliff Cavern, Peak Cavern, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, Odin Mine, Hope Cement Works, and [David Mellor Design Museum](https://www.davidmellordesign.com/design-museum-cafe). Can be muddy. Lunch at the Castleton Fish & Chips in Castleton, the Dolly Tearooms in Castleton, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café in Castleton, the Three Roofs Café in Castleton, the Castle in Castleton, the Bull's Head in Castleton, the Castleton Maid Ice Cream in Castleton, the Peveril Tea Rooms in Castleton, the George Inn in Castleton, the Rose Cottage Café in Castleton, the 1530 The Restaurant in Castleton, the Tilly's of Castleton in Castleton, the Ye Olde Nag's Head in Castleton, the Baytree Tea Room & Gifts in Castleton, the Rambler's Rest in Castleton, the Peak Hotel in Castleton, the Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese in Castleton, the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/) in Bradwell, the White Hart Inn in Bradwell, the Brook Café in Bradwell, or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/) in Bradwell. 27 km. 7 hours.

**Hope to Edale**: An alternative variant of [Bamford to Edale via Win Hill and Great Ridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bamford-to-edale-via-win-hill-and-great-ridge/). Steep ascent to heathery ridge, exposed moorland, gritstone escarpments, and ridgeline with panoramic valley views. Sights: Win Hill Pike, The Great Ridge, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, and Lose Hill. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the [Cheshire Cheese Inn](http://www.thecheshirecheeseinn.co.uk/) in Hope. 15 km.

**Hope to Bamford**: An alternative variant of [Hope to Hathersage or Bamford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hope-to-hathersage-or-bamford-via-castleton/). Limestone and gritstone uplands, pastures with panoramic views, landslip area, dry limestone gorge, collapsed cave dale, upland moor, and descent to Bamford. Sights: Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Cave Dale, Peveril Castle, and Hope Cement Works. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Castleton Fish & Chips in Castleton, the Dolly Tearooms in Castleton, the Peak District National Park Visitor Centre Café in Castleton, the Three Roofs Café in Castleton, the Castle in Castleton, the Bull's Head in Castleton, the [Ye Olde Bowling Green Inn](https://www.yeoldebowlinggreenbradwell.co.uk/) in Bradwell, or the [Shoulder of Mutton Inn](https://www.shoulderbradwell.pub/) in Bradwell.

-1.7706911,51.3422347.name: Pewsey
-1.7706911,51.3422347.ramblerNote:
**[Pewsey Circular via Giant's Grave](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular/)**: Chalk downland escarpments, open ridges, steep serrated downs, Vale of Pewsey farmland, canal towpath, prehistoric earthworks, and hillforts. Sights: Wansdyke, Milk Hill, Alton Barnes White Horse, Kennet & Avon Canal, Giant's Grave, and Pewsey Downs. Lunch at the [Honeystreet Mill Café](https://www.honeystreetmill.co.uk/about-3) in Honeystreet, or the [Barge Inn](https://thebargeinnhoneystreet.uk/) in Honeystreet. 27 km. 6 hours.

**[Pewsey Circular via Avebury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular-via-avebury/)**: Open Wiltshire downland, Marlborough Downs ridges, bluebell woods, sarsen stone fields, ancient earthworks, Vale of Pewsey, and canal towpath. Sights: Fyfield Down Sarsen Stones Field, Avebury Standing Stones, Avebury Bank & Ditch Earthworks, Silbury Hill, West Kennett Long Barrow, The Sanctuary, Windmill Hill, West Woods, Wansdyke, Milk Hill, and Kennet & Avon Canal. Lunch at the [Who'd A Thought It](http://www.thewhodathoughtit.co.uk/index) in Lockeridge, the [Circles Restaurant (at Avebury Museum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/avebury) in Avebury, or the [Red Lion](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/wiltshire/red-lion) in Avebury. 36 km. 8.5 hours.

**Pewsey Circular via Wansdyke**: A shorter variant of [Pewsey Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pewsey-circular/). Chalk downland escarpments, open ridges, Vale of Pewsey farmland, canal towpath, and prehistoric earthworks. Sights: Wansdyke, Milk Hill, Alton Barnes White Horse, and Kennet & Avon Canal. Lunch at the [Honeystreet Mill Café](https://www.honeystreetmill.co.uk/about-3) in Honeystreet, or the [Barge Inn](https://thebargeinnhoneystreet.uk/) in Honeystreet. 19 km.

Lunch at the Haven House Inn in Mudeford Quay. 12 km.
