-1.8064141,51.0705706.name: Salisbury
-1.8064141,51.0705706.publicNote: Combine your pilgrimage to Stonehenge with a beautiful hike.
-1.8064141,51.0705706.ramblerNote:
**[Salisbury Circular via Cranborne Chase Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/salisbury-circular/)**: Historic city, open fields, river valley, pretty villages, chalk downs, ancient drove tracks, steep hillside nature reserve, and water meadows. Sights: Salisbury Cathedral, [Old Mill at Harnham](http://oldmillhotelsalisbury.co.uk/), Harnham Slope Nature Reserve, and Cranborne Chase Area of Outstanding Natural Beauty. Strenuous afternoon climbs. Lunch at the [Fox & Goose](http://www.foxandgoose-coombebissett.co.uk/) in Coombe Bissett, the [Coombe Bissett Stores](http://www.coombebissettstores.co.uk/) in Coombe Bissett, the [White Hart](http://www.thewhitehart-bishopstone.co.uk/) in Bishopstone, the [Queen's Head](http://queensheadbroadchalke.co.uk/) in Broad Chalke, or the [Chalke Valley Stores](http://www.chalkevalleystores.co.uk/) in Broad Chalke. 32 km. 7.5 hours.

**Salisbury to Dean**: An alternative variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, and following the Clarendon Way. Sights: Clarendon Palace and [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/) in Pitton, or the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/) in Winterslow. 24 km.

**Salisbury to Mottisfont & Dunbridge**: An alternative variant of [Dean to Mottisfont](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dean-to-mottisfont/). Chalk downlands, rolling hills, varied woods, field boundaries, farm tracks, steep-sided chalk downs, river meadows, Test Valley chalk stream, and sculpture park. Sights: Clarendon Palace, [New Art Centre at Roche Court](http://sculpture.uk.com/), [Mottisfont Abbey & Gardens](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/mottisfont), and St. Andrews Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Silver Plough](http://silverplough-pitton.co.uk/) in Pitton, or the [Lord Nelson](http://www.the-lord-nelson.co.uk/) in Winterslow. 37 km.

-1.8172462,53.3648176.name: Edale
-1.8172462,53.3648176.ramblerNote:
**[Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/)**: Rugged moorland plateau, peat bog, gritstone outcrops and boulders, fertile valley floor, steep ridgeline, and Dark Peak and White Peak landscapes. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Jacob's Ladder, Mam Tor, Winnats Pass, Great Ridge, and Pennine Way. Can be very boggy. Lunch at the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/) in Edale, or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#) in Edale. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Edale Circular via Kinder Low](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kinder-scout-circuit-from-edale/)**: Moorland plateau, boggy ground, gritstone boulder fields, exposed edges, steep cloughs, heather slopes, and high-level ridge paths. Sights: Kinder Scout, Kinder Downfall, Grindslow Knoll, Kinder Low, The Woolpacks, Crowden Tower, Fairbrook Naze, and Ringing Roger. Serious navigation hazard. 27 km. 8 hours.

**Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor & Grindslow Knoll**: A shorter variant of [Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/). Moorland plateau, gritstone outcrops, valley floor, ridge, and with shortcut over Grindslow Knoll. Sights: Kinder Scout, Grindslow Knoll, and Mam Tor. Lunch at the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/) in Edale, or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#) in Edale. 18 km.

**Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor & Grindsbrook Clough**: A shorter variant of [Edale Circular via Kinder Scout and Mam Tor](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edale-circular-via-kinder-scout-and-mam-tor/). Moorland plateau, gritstone outcrops, scrambling descent through clough, and valley floor. Sights: Kinder Scout and Grindsbrook Clough. Light scrambling required. Lunch at the [Old Nags Head](http://www.the-old-nags-head.co.uk/) in Edale, or the [Rambler Inn](https://www.dorbiere.co.uk/the-rambler-inn/#) in Edale. 18 km.

-1.9131016,53.2607853.name: Buxton
-1.9131016,53.2607853.ramblerNote:
**[Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/)**: Quiet moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, green valleys, reservoirs, and hillfort promontory. Sights: Castle Naze, Black Edge, Combs Edge, Combs Reservoir, and Corbar Hill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/) in Combs. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Buxton to Macclesfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-to-macclesfield/)**: Moorland, exposed ridges, gritstone uplands, wooded valleys, rolling pastures, ancient packhorse routes, and quarried country park. Sights: Shining Tor, Buxton Crescent Hotel, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/the-bar-at-cat-and-fiddle/), Forest Chapel, Macclesfield Forest, and Tegg's Nose Country Park. Very exposed moorland. Lunch at the [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk) in Buxton New Road, the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest, or the Stanley Arms Hotel in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Chee Dale](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-or-to-monsal-dale-via-the-wye-valley/)**: Limestone gorges, stepping stones, overhanging rockfaces, dismantled railway line, limekilns, high pastures, dry valleys, and quiet villages. Sights: Chee Dale, Miller's Dale Viaducts, Five Wells Chambered Tomb, Deep Dale, Wye Valley, and Monsal Trail. Paths flood after rain. Lunch at the Angler's Rest in Miller's Dale, or the [Refreshment Room](https://www.blueberryfood.co.uk/the-refreshment-room) in Millers Dale. 26 km. 7.5 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via the Dragon's Back](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-the-dragons-back/)**: Limestone uplands, remote valleys, dramatic reef knoll ridges, dry valleys, disused quarries, and open moorland. Sights: Chrome Hill, Parkhouse Hill, Hitter Hill, High Wheeldon, High Edge, Dowel Dale, Harpur Hill Quarry, and Blue Lagoon. Steep scrambling sections. Lunch at the [Packhorse Inn](https://www.thepackhorseinn.net) in Crowdecote, the [Ye Olde Cheshire Cheese](https://www.robinsonsbrewery.com/pubs/ye-olde-cheshire-cheese-inn-longnor) in Longnor, the Longnor Fish and Chip Shop and Tea Room in Longnor, or the Cobbles Coffee Shop in Longnor. 26 km. 7 hours.

**[Buxton Circular via Axe Edge Moor and Three Shires Head](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-axe-edge-moor-and-three-shires-head/)**: Open upland moor, broad grassy and gravel paths, boggy moorland sections with groughs and peat hags, scenic river valleys, disused quarries, pastures, and long far views. Sights: Solomon's Temple, Three Shires Head, Panniers Bridge, and [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle). Can be boggy. Lunch at the [Flash Bar Café and Shop](http://www.flashbarstores.co.uk/) in Flash Bar, or the New Inn in Flash. 27 km. 7 hours.

**Buxton to Chapel-en-le-Frith**: A shorter variant of [Buxton Circular via Combs Moss](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxton-circular-via-combs-moss/). Moorland plateau, gritstone scarps, and descent via Castle Naze causeway to valley station. Sights: Castle Naze, Combs Edge, and Combs Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Beehive Inn & Farm Shop](http://thebeehiveinn.co.uk/) in Combs.

-1.9472858,52.0984969.name: Evesham
-1.9472858,52.0984969.ramblerNote: **[Evesham to Pershore via Dumbleton and Bredon Hills](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/evesham-to-pershore-via-dumbleton-and-bredon-hills/)**: Vale of Evesham market gardens, river meadows, pastures, minor hills, country estate, woods, and Iron Age hillfort summit with panoramic views. -2.0230749,51.6767954.name: Kemble
-2.0230749,51.6767954.publicNote: Your gateway to a particularly beautiful part of the Cotswalds. Hike all the way from here to Stroud Station if you're feeling ambitious.
-2.0230749,51.6767954.ramblerNote: **[Kemble Circular via Thames Head and Cirencester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kemble-circular/)**: Cotswolds farmland, disused canal towpath, ancient hillfort, beech woods, high watershed, grand parkland vistas, market town streets, quiet fields, and upper Thames riverbank. Sights: Thames Head, Lyd Well, Trewsbury Camp Hillfort, Thames & Severn Canal, Sapperton Canal Tunnel, Hailey Wood, Cirencester Park, Queen Anne Monument, Roman Amphitheatre, Roman Town Wall, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Can flood at Thames Head. Lunch at the [Thames Head Inn](https://www.arkells.com/pub/the-thames-head-inn-cirencester.htm) in Kemble, or the [Bell at Sapperton](https://www.bellsapperton.co.uk/) in Sapperton. 27 km. 6 hours.
-2.0230749,51.6767954.publicRamblerNote: **[Kemble Circular via Thames Head and Cirencester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kemble-circular/)**: Cotswolds farmland, disused canal towpath, ancient hillfort, beech woods, high watershed, grand parkland vistas, market town streets, quiet fields, and upper Thames riverbank. Sights: Thames Head, Lyd Well, Trewsbury Camp Hillfort, Thames & Severn Canal, Sapperton Canal Tunnel, Hailey Wood, Cirencester Park, Queen Anne Monument, Roman Amphitheatre, Roman Town Wall, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). -2.0787884,51.061005.name: Tisbury
-2.0787884,51.061005.ramblerNote:
**[Tisbury Circular via Hindon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-hindon/)**: Dry chalky U-shaped downland valleys, wooded ridges, grassy valleys, picturesque villages, rolling hills, and river valley. Sights: Fonthill Estate, Place Farm, and Fonthill House. Lunch at the [Pythouse Kitchen Garden Cafe](http://pythousekitchengarden.co.uk/) in West Hatch, the [Grosvenor Arms](http://www.grosvenorarmshindon.co.uk/) in Hindon, the [Lamb](http://www.lambhindon.co.uk/) in Hindon, or the [Beckford Arms](http://www.beckfordarms.com/) in Fonthill Gifford. 22 km. 5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Donhead St. Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-donhead-st-andrew/)**: Rolling downland, wooded estates, large country parks, open ridges, Neolithic hill fort, steep descents, and unspoilt village. Sights: [Old Wardour Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/old-wardour-castle/) and New Wardour Castle. Can be muddy. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-dinton-and-fovant/)**: Nadder Valley, undulating West Wiltshire Downs, grassy U-shaped valleys, bluebell woods, wildflower meadows, pastures, and chalk downland. Sights: Philipps House, Dinton Park, Dinton Mill, and Fovant Badges. Lunch at the Wyndham Arms in Dinton. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Alvediston](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-alvediston/)**: Chalky downland valleys, open hilltops, coombes, wooded Iron Age hill fort, undulating countryside, and expansive views. Plenty of ascent. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](http://www.royaloakswallowcliffe.com/) in Swallowcliffe, or the [Horseshoe Inn](http://thehorseshoe-inn.co.uk/) in Ebbesbourne Wake. 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Tisbury Circular via Ludwell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-ludwell/)**: Upper Nadder Valley, Wardour Park, rolling downland, steep chalk escarpments, ridge walking, woods, boggy brook crossings, and unspoilt village. Sights: New Wardour Castle, Old Wardour Castle, Win Green Hill, White Sheet Hill, and Castle Ditches Iron Age Hillfort. Steep rough descents. Lunch at the [Grove Arms](https://www.thegrovearms.co.uk/) in Ludwell, or the [Talbot Inn](http://www.talbotinnberwickstjohn.co.uk/) in Berwick St. John. 26 km. 6.5 hours.

**Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant & Chiselbury Iron Age hill fort**: A longer variant of [Tisbury Circular via Dinton and Fovant](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tisbury-circular-via-dinton-and-fovant/). Nadder Valley, undulating West Wiltshire Downs, grassy valleys, bluebell woods, wildflower meadows, chalk downland, and Fovant Down hilltop. Sights: Philipps House, Dinton Park, Dinton Mill, Fovant Badges, and Chiselbury Iron Age hill fort. Lunch at the Wyndham Arms in Dinton. 29 km.

-2.0947934,54.9735335.name: Hexham
-2.0947934,54.9735335.ramblerNote:
**Hexham Circular (HWP: Walwick to Planetrees)**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, quiet country lanes, North Tyne Valley, ridge, Wall stretches, and village of Acomb. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, and Carrowburgh Fort. Lunch at the Riverside Kitchen Café in Chollerford, the George Hotel in Chollerford, the Miners Arms in Acomb, the Sun Inn in Acomb, or the Queens Arms Hotel in Acomb. 20 km.

**Hexham to Corbridge**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). River Tyne, country lanes, North Tyne Valley views, Wall stretches, ridge, and historic town of Corbridge. Sights: Chesters Fort, Brunton Turret, St. Oswald's Church, Halton Chesters Fort, Aydon Castle, Halton Castle, and Halton Church. Lunch at the Hadrian Hotel in Wall, or the Errington Coffee House in near Halton Chesters. 21 km.

**[Macclesfield Circular via Teggs Nose and Kerridge Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-circular-via-teggs-nose-and-kerridge-hill/)**: Steep residential roads, rolling pastures, quarried hilltop, moorland edges, forest, isolated chapel, reservoir, long ridgeline, and canal towpath. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Lamaload Reservoir, White Nancy, Kerridge Ridge, and Macclesfield Canal. Steep ascents. Lunch at the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/) in Macclesfield Forest, the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, or the Robin Hood in Rainow. 24 km. 7 hours.

**[Macclesfield to Buxton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/macclesfield-to-buxton/)**: Residential roads, pastures, rolling hills, quarried hilltop, forest tracks, exposed moorland, lonely upland valleys, and riverside approach into a spa town. Sights: Tegg's Nose Country Park, Macclesfield Forest, Forest Chapel, Shining Tor, Goytsclough Quarry, Pavilion Gardens, [Cat & Fiddle](https://theforestdistillery.com/cat-fiddle/), and [Peak View Restaurant & Tea Room](http://www.peakviewtearooms.co.uk/). Exposed in bad weather. Lunch at the [Forest Distillery](https://theforestdistillery.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest, or the [Stanley Arms Hotel](https://www.stanleyarms.com/) in Bottom-of-the-Oven, Macclesfield Forest. 25 km. 7 hours.

Sights: [Clent Hills](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Clent_Hills), [Wychbury Hill](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wychbury_Hill), [Hagley Park](https://www.hagleypark.co.uk/), The Four Stones, Walton Hill, Waseley Hills Country Park, Beacon Hill Castle, Lickey Hills Country Park, Bilberry Hill, and Cofton Hill. Sights: Westbury White Horse, Bratton Camp, Scratchbury Camp, Battlesbury Camp, Imber Village, and St Giles, Imber. MOD closures apply. Sights: Pendragon Castle, Wild Boar Fell, Swarth Fell, and Mallerstang Edge. Can be boggy. 23 km. 6 hours.

-2.3607303,54.2056394.name: Ribblehead
-2.3607303,54.2056394.ramblerNote:
**[Ribblehead Circular via Whernside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whernside/)**: Moorland, mosses, heather, cotton grass, high plateau with tarns, elevated scarp path, steep descent on engineered path, farm tracks, limestone country with pavements, shake holes, sink holes, and dry streambeds. Sights: Whernside, Ribblehead Viaduct, Artengill Railway Viaduct, and Low Force Waterfall. Can be boggy. 20 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Ribblehead Circular via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/)**: High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, limestone pavement, erratics, shake holes, pot holes, farm tracks, wooded gorge option, and valley return under viaduct. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Ribblehead Limestone Quarry National Nature Reserve, Park Fell, Simon Fell, Scales Moor limestone plateau, Ingleton Waterfalls Trail, and Ribblehead Station. Very exposed summit. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the Village Kitchen in Ingleton, the Ingleton Chippy in Ingleton, the Ingleton Pizza in Ingleton, the G&T Café and Kitchen in Ingleton, or the Three Horse Shoes in Ingleton. 24 km. 7 hours.

**Ribblehead to Bentham**: An alternative variant of [Ingleborough via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/). High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, and largely flat finish from Ingleton to Bentham. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Park Fell, and Simon Fell. Very exposed summit. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the Village Kitchen in Ingleton, the Ingleton Chippy in Ingleton, the Ingleton Pizza in Ingleton, the G&T Café and Kitchen in Ingleton, or the Three Horse Shoes in Ingleton. 17 km.

**[Ribblehead Circular via Whernside](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/whernside/)**: Moorland, mosses, heather, cotton grass, high plateau with tarns, elevated scarp path, steep descent on engineered path, farm tracks, limestone country with pavements, shake holes, sink holes, and dry streambeds. Sights: Whernside, Ribblehead Viaduct, Artengill Railway Viaduct, and Low Force Waterfall. Can be boggy. 20 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Ribblehead Circular via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/)**: High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, limestone pavement, erratics, shake holes, pot holes, farm tracks, wooded gorge option, and valley return under viaduct. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Ribblehead Limestone Quarry National Nature Reserve, Park Fell, Simon Fell, Scales Moor limestone plateau, Ingleton Waterfalls Trail, and Ribblehead Station. Very exposed summit. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the Village Kitchen in Ingleton, the Ingleton Chippy in Ingleton, the Ingleton Pizza in Ingleton, the G&T Café and Kitchen in Ingleton, or the Three Horse Shoes in Ingleton. 24 km. 7 hours.

**Ribblehead to Bentham**: An alternative variant of [Ingleborough via Ingleton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-ingleton/). High moorland, steep mountain ascent, exposed plateau, and largely flat finish from Ingleton to Bentham. Sights: Ingleborough, Ribblehead Viaduct, Park Fell, and Simon Fell. Very exposed summit. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf Inn](https://www.thewheatsheaf-ingleton.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the [Peaks & Troughs Bar & Kitchen](https://www.peakstroughs.co.uk/) in Ingleton, the Village Kitchen in Ingleton, the Ingleton Chippy in Ingleton, the Ingleton Pizza in Ingleton, the G&T Café and Kitchen in Ingleton, or the Three Horse Shoes in Ingleton. 17 km.

-2.3636343,54.2825661.name: Dent
-2.3636343,54.2825661.ramblerNote: **[Dent to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-knoutberry-hill/)**: Grassy moorland, high fells, quiet roads, good tracks, open views, railway viaducts, becks, and pastures. Sights: Dent Head Viaduct, Ribblehead Viaduct, Great Knoutberry Hill, Blea Moor Railway Tunnel, [Station Inn](https://www.thestationinnribblehead.com/), and Ribblehead Station Visitor Centre and Tea Room. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.
-2.3636343,54.2825661.publicRamblerNote: **[Dent to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-knoutberry-hill/)**: Grassy moorland, high fells, quiet roads, good tracks, open views, railway viaducts, becks, and pastures. -2.368703,54.4549076.ramblerNote:
**[Kirkby Stephen Circular via Eden River gorge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/)**: Upland fell, exposed moorland, peat bog, limestone country with shake holes and swallow holes, river gorge, narrow woodland path, scenic valley, heathery commons, and pastures. Sights: Nine Standards, Eden River gorge, Ladthwaite Beck waterfall, Dukerdale, and Podgill Viaduct. Can be boggy. 18 km. 5 hours.

**Kirkby Stephen Circular via Nine Standards**: An alternative variant of [Nine Standards](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/). Upland fell, lanes and paths into town, and historic market town. Sights: Nine Standards. Can be boggy. 20 km.

**Kirkby Stephen to Garsdale**: A longer variant of [Nine Standards](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/nine-standards/). High exposed upland watershed, moorland, open fells, source of Eden River, and good track above Mallerstang Valley. Sights: Nine Standards, High Seat, Hugh Seat, and Hell Gill Bridge. Exposed high ground. 29 km. 8 hours.

-2.4118943,53.8244874.name: Whalley
-2.4118943,53.8244874.ramblerNote: **[Whalley to Clitheroe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pendle-hill/)**: Abbey precinct, town centre, riverside path, golf course, open pasture, old drove road, moorland ridge, plateau summit, steep scarp, lush valley, meandering river, and nature reserve. Sights: Whalley Abbey Precinct, Pendle Hill, Nick O' Pendle, Ogden Clough, Downham village, River Ribble, and St. Leonard's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Wellsprings](https://thewellsprings.co.uk/) in Sabden, the Downham Ice Cream Shop in Downham, or the [Assheton Arms](https://www.jamesplaces.com/assheton-arms/) in Downham. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

-2.4374501,50.7091366.name: Dorchester South
-2.4374501,50.7091366.ramblerNote:
**[Dorchester South Circular via Bronkham Hill barrows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/)**: Prehistoric downland, South Winterbourne Valley, heathery hilltops, barrow-studded ridgeway, and sweeping views to Jurassic Coast. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), and Bronkham Hill barrows. Lunch at the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/) in Martinstown. 24 km. 6 hours.

**Dorchester South Circular via Valley of (Sarsen) Stones**: A longer variant of [Dorchester Circular or to Portesham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dorchester-circular-or-to-portesham/). Prehistoric downland, Valley of Stones boulder train, stone circles, barrows, Bride Valley, and heathery hilltops. Sights: [Maumbury Rings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Maumbury_Rings), [Maiden Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/maiden-castle/), [Hardy Monument](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/hardy-monument), Valley of (Sarsen) Stones, Hell Stone Burial Chamber, and Bridehead Lake. Lunch at the [Brewer's Arms](http://www.thebrewersarms.com/) in Martinstown. 37 km. 9 hours.

**[Dorchester to Abbotsbury](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/osmington-or-dorchester-to-abbotsbury/)**: a linear walk from Dorchester South to Abbotsbury (bus return). Part of a longer Osmington-to-Abbotsbury walk.

-2.4471521,51.1116892.name: Bruton
-2.4471521,51.1116892.ramblerNote:
**[Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/)**: South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge forming the Wiltshire/Somerset boundary, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens with lakeside walks, grassy U-shaped valley, and honey-coloured stone-built town. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Bristol Cross, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, and Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch at the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/) in North Brewham, the Alpine Coffee Co in Kingsettle Hill, the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/) in Stourton, or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead) in Stourton. 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Bruton Circular via Alfred's Tower](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-hauser-wirth-somerset/)**: Narrow steep-sided valleys and coombes, fast-flowing streams, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone villages, and far views to Wiltshire and Dorset boundary. Sights: Alfred's Tower, [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset), and Bruton Dovecote. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thethreehorseshoesbatcombe.co.uk/) in Batcombe, the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/) in Bruton, the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/) in Bruton, the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/) in Bruton, or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/) in Bruton. 25 km. 6.5 hours.

**Bruton Circular via Hauser & Wirth Somerset**: A shorter variant of [Bruton Circular or from Castle Cary](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-hauser-wirth-somerset/). Valleys, coombes, rolling hilltops, honey-coloured stone village, and extended time at art gallery. Sights: [Hauser & Wirth Somerset](https://www.hauserwirth.com/locations/10068-hauser-wirth-somerset) and Bruton Dovecote. Lunch at the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/) in Bruton, the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/) in Bruton, the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/) in Bruton, or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/) in Bruton. 16 km.

**Bruton Circular via Stourhead & White Sheet Hill Iron Age Hillfort**: A longer variant of [Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/). South Somerset pastures, wooded ridge, hanging woods, landscaped estate gardens, and Iron Age hillfort and Neolithic camp on exposed downland hill. Sights: Alfred's Tower, Stourhead Estate, White Sheet Hill Iron Age Hillfort, White Sheet Hill Neolithic Camp, Palladian Bridge, Pantheon, Stourhead House, Bruton Dovecote, and Hauser & Wirth Somerset. Lunch at the [Old Red Lion](http://www.theoldredlion.org.uk/) in North Brewham, the Alpine Coffee Co in Kingsettle Hill, the [Spread Eagle Inn](https://www.spreadeagleinn.com/) in Stourton, or the [Stourhead Estate Restaurant](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/wiltshire/stourhead/eating-and-shopping-at-stourhead) in Stourton. 29 km.

-2.4549096,50.6159266.name: Weymouth
-2.4549096,50.6159266.ramblerNote: **Weymouth Circular**: A variant of [Weymouth via Isle of Portland Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/weymouth-via-isle-of-portland-circular/). Disused railway path, pebble causeway, steep hill climb, clifftop ridge with panoramic views, and South West Coast Path back to harbour. Sights: [Rodwell Trail](http://www.weymouth.gov.uk/home.asp?sv=169), Chesil Beach, The Verne, Verne Citadel, and Weymouth Harbour. Lunch at the [Jailhouse Cafe](http://jailhousecafe.co.uk/) in The Verne, Portland, or the Heights Hotel in Portland. 15 km.

-2.4627642,54.9679903.name: Haltwhistle
-2.4627642,54.9679903.ramblerNote: **Haltwhistle to Bardon Mill**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Burn gorge, upland plateau, Roman military zone, crags, Sycamore Gap, lough, and open moorland. Sights: Haltwhistle Burn Roman Fortlet, Great Chesters Fort, Sycamore Gap, Crag Lough, and Housesteads Fort. Lunch at the Roman Army Museum Tea Room in Walltown Quarry, the Milecastle Inn in near Haltwhistle, the Twice Brewed Inn in near Steel Rigg, the Once Brewed Coffee and Bakehouse at The Sill in near Steel Rigg, or the Housesteads Fort Café in Housesteads. 18 km.

**[Castle Cary Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/castle-cary-circular-via-camelot/)**: Somerset Levels fringes, hillock, quiet residential streets, charming market town, dry green valley, ancient holloway, pastures, shallow river valley, hillfort plateau, steep ascents, large WWII airfield remains, and broad plateau with views. Sights: Market House, Cadbury Castle, Lodge Hill, Glastonbury Tor, Mendip Hills, Parrock Hill, The Beacon, and Sigwells Plateau. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the Catash Inn in North Cadbury, the Chapel Cross Coffee Rooms in South Cadbury, or the Camelot Inn in South Cadbury. 30 km. 7 hours.

**Castle Cary to Bruton via Stourhead**: An alternative variant of [Bruton Circular via Stourhead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bruton-circular-via-stourhead/). Lunch at the [Three Horseshoes Inn](https://www.thethreehorseshoesbatcombe.co.uk/) in Batcombe, the [Matt's Kitchen](http://www.mattskitchen.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Prickly Pear](https://www.prickly-pear.com/) in Bruton, the [Sun Inn](https://thesuninnbruton.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [At the Chapel](http://www.atthechapel.co.uk/) in Bruton, the [Old Pharmacy](https://www.oldpharmacybruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Briar](https://numberonebruton.com/briar/) in Bruton, the [Blue Ball Bruton](https://www.blueballbruton.com/) in Bruton, the [Da Costa](https://da-costa.co.uk/) in Bruton, or the [Roth Bar](https://rothbar.co.uk/) in Bruton. 29 km.

-2.569663,50.7802255.name: Maiden Newton
-2.569663,50.7802255.ramblerNote:
**[Maiden Newton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maiden-newton-circular-or-to-dorchester/)**: Chalk downland, dry hanging valleys, rounded hills, broad ridges, chalk streams, picturesque villages, and historic churches. Sights: The Cerne Giant, Cerne Abbey, Hog Cliff National Nature Reserve, and Giant Hill. Six hilly ascents. Lunch at the [Greyhound Inn](https://dorsetgreyhound.co.uk) in Sydling St. Nicholas, the [Royal Oak](https://theroyaloakcerneabbas.co.uk) in Cerne Abbas, the [Giant Inn (aka The Red Lion)](https://www.thegiantcerneabbas.co.uk) in Cerne Abbas, the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinncerneabbas.co.uk/index) in Cerne Abbas, or the [Abbots Tearoom](https://www.abbotsbedandbreakfast.co.uk) in Cerne Abbas. 28 km. 7 hours.

**Maiden Newton to Dorchester South**: An alternative variant of [Maiden Newton Circular or to Dorchester](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/maiden-newton-circular-or-to-dorchester/). Chalk downland, Cerne Valley, historic Dorchester city centre, and two modest climbs with fine views. Sights: The Cerne Giant, Cerne Abbey, Hog Cliff National Nature Reserve, and Giant Hill. Hilly ascents. Lunch at the [Greyhound Inn](https://dorsetgreyhound.co.uk) in Sydling St. Nicholas, the [Royal Oak](https://theroyaloakcerneabbas.co.uk) in Cerne Abbas, the [Giant Inn (aka The Red Lion)](https://www.thegiantcerneabbas.co.uk) in Cerne Abbas, the [New Inn](https://www.thenewinncerneabbas.co.uk/index) in Cerne Abbas, or the [Abbots Tearoom](https://www.abbotsbedandbreakfast.co.uk) in Cerne Abbas.

-2.6635574,54.6943632.name: Langwathby
-2.6635574,54.6943632.ramblerNote:
**[Langwathby to Appleby via Little Dun Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cross-fell-and-great-dun-fell/)**: Moorland ridge, high plateau, boulder-filled fell tops, upland hay meadows, disused mine scars, rough pastures, and engineered Pennine Way paths. Sights: Cross Fell, Great Dun Fell, Little Dun Fell, Knock Fell, and High Cup Nick. Exposed, boggy, foggy. Avoid winter/early spring. 33 km. 8.5 hours.

**Langwathby to Appleby via Green Castle Crag**: A variant of [Cross Fell and Great Dun Fell](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/cross-fell-and-great-dun-fell/). Tarmac service track from radar station, beautiful gorge, rim of Knock Ore Gill, rough pastures, and paths to the Pennine Way. Sights: Cross Fell, Great Dun Fell, Green Castle Crag, and Knock Ore Gill. Exposed, boggy, foggy.
-2.6635574,54.6943632.publicRamblerNote:

-2.7038502,54.9321536.name: Brampton
-2.7038502,54.9321536.ramblerNote: **Brampton to Haltwhistle**: A variant of [Hadrian's Wall Path Core Section](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hadrians-wall-path-core-section/). Ridge, wooded gorge, river, Roman remains, quarry crags, upland moorland, and market town. Sights: Lanercost Priory, Birdoswald Fort, Willowford Bridge, Milecastle 48, Thirlwall Castle, Roman Army Museum, and Great Chesters Fort. Lunch at the Haytongate Farm Snack Hut in Haytongate, the Birdoswald Fort Café in Birdoswald, the House of Meg Café in Gilsland, the Samson Inn in Gilsland, the Bridge Inn in Gilsland, the Greenhead Hotel in Greenhead, the Greenhead Tea Room in Greenhead, or the Roman Army Museum Tea Room in Walltown Quarry. 28 km.

-3.0090126,51.8174352.name: Abergavenny
-3.0090126,51.8174352.ramblerNote:
**[Abergavenny Circular via Deri Fach oak wood](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sugar-loaf-abergavenny-circular/)**: Lush pastures, ancient oak woods, heathland, grassy ridges, heather and gorse, open mountain summit, and valley stream. Sights: Sugar Loaf, [Sugar Loaf Vineyard Coffee Shop and Tasting Room](https://sugarloafvineyards.co.uk/), Deri Fach oak wood, and Mynydd Llanwenarth. Very exposed summit. 19 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Abergavenny Circular via Abergavenny Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blorenge/)**: Steep ascent through woodland, tramroad contours, industrial ruins, heather moorland, bracken slopes, glacial bowl, canal towpath, and pastures with views. Sights: Abergavenny Castle, Castle Meadows, Llanfoist tunnel, Monmouthshire & Brecon Canal, Llanfoist Inclines, Garnddyrys Forge, Blorenge summit plateau, The Punchbowl, Llanfoist Wharf, and Blaenavon World Heritage Site. Steep ascent and descent. 19 km. 6 hours.

**Abergavenny Circular via Sugar Loaf**: A longer variant of [Sugar Loaf Circular from Abergavenny](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sugar-loaf-abergavenny-circular/). Lush pastures, ancient oak woods, heathland, grassy ridges, heather and gorse, open mountain summit, and extended descent flank. Sights: Sugar Loaf and [Sugar Loaf Vineyard Coffee Shop and Tasting Room](https://sugarloafvineyards.co.uk/). Very exposed summit.

-3.2848689,51.3969039.name: Barry
-3.2848689,51.3969039.ramblerNote:
**[Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/)**: Coastal headlands, pebble and sandy beaches, flat rock-shelf beaches, low cliffs, striated limestone cliffs, parkland, quiet cliff paths, wetland, and historic town centre. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), [St Illtyd's Church](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Illtyd's_Church,_Llantwit_Major), Cold Knap Point, Rhoose Point, and Summerhouse Point. Check tide timings. Best in Jun–Aug. Lunch at the [Blue Anchor](http://www.blueanchoraberthaw.com) in East Aberthaw. 21 km.

**Barry to Rhoose Cardiff International Airport**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). Coastal headlands, pebble beach, low cliffs, parkland, railway viaduct, and cliff tops above former quarries. Sights: [Barry Docks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Docks), [Barry Island](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Barry_Island), Cold Knap Point, [Porthkerry Country Park](http://www.valeofglamorgan.gov.uk/en/enjoying/Coast-and-Countryside/Porthkerry-Country-Park/Porthkerry-Country-Park.aspx), [Porthkerry Viaduct](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Porthkerry_Viaduct), [The Bulwarks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Bulwarks,_Porthkerry), and Rhoose Point. Check tide timings.

-3.3494457,51.3871547.ramblerNote: **Rhoose Cardiff International Airport to Llantwit Major**: A shorter variant of [Barry to Llantwit Major](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/barry-to-llantwit-major/). -3.9964221,50.7323333.name: Okehampton
-3.9964221,50.7323333.ramblerNote: **[Okehampton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ten-tors/)**: Remote, featureless moorland, boggy ground, river crossings, hilly terrain, and northern Dartmoor. Sights: [Dartmoor National Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dartmoor). MOD firing ranges apply. Best in Mar–Aug. 88 km.
-3.9964221,50.7323333.publicRamblerNote: **[Okehampton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ten-tors/)**: Remote, featureless moorland, boggy ground, river crossings, hilly terrain, and northern Dartmoor. 0.00375,51.4826479.name: Maze Hill
0.00375,51.4826479.ramblerNote: **Maze Hill to Canary Wharf**: An alternative variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Greenwich Park, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, eastern Isle of Dogs riverside path, and Docklands waterfront. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, Painted Hall, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Whale on the Wharf sculpture, and [Mudchute Park & Farm](https://www.mudchute.org). Lunch at the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com) in Greenwich. 12 km. 2.5 hours.
0.00375,51.4826479.publicRamblerNote: **Maze Hill to Canary Wharf**: An alternative variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). 0.0042319,51.2442817.name: Hurst Green
0.0042319,51.2442817.ramblerNote:
**[Hurst Green to Sevenoaks via Toy's Hill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand ridge, chart hills, undulating woodland, commons, deer park, historic estates, and suburban old town. Sights: Chartwell, Toy's Hill, Ide Hill, Knole Park, and Knole Palace. Steep climbs. Lunch at the NT restaurant at Chartwell in Chartwell, or the [Fox & Hounds](http://www.foxhoundstoyshill.co.uk/) in Toys Hill. 18 km.

**[Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/)**: Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the High Weald and South Downs. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, Henley Wood Enclosure, and Riddlesdown. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Old Ship in Tatsfield. 21 km. 5.5 hours.

**Hurst Green to Oxted**: A shorter variant of [Hurst Green to Chiddingstone Causeway](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-chiddingstone-causeway/). Greensand hills, woodland, open country, and village paths. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Carpenters Arms](http://carpenterslimpsfield.co.uk/) in Limpsfield Chart. 9 km. 3 hours.

**Hurst Green to Godstone**: A variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, and flat fields alongside railway. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), Tenchleys Manor, and St George's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 13 km.

**Hurst Green to Lingfield**: A shorter variant of [Oxted to Lingfield](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/oxted-to-lingfield/). Hillside paths, Low Weald farmland, ancient woodland, old manor houses, flat plain, and nature reserve. Sights: [Staffhurst Wood](https://www.surreycc.gov.uk/culture-and-leisure/countryside/sites/visitor-information/staffhurst-wood), [Crowhurst Yew](https://www.ancient-yew.org/yew_trees/crowhurst-surrey-385), [Lingfield Nature Reserve](https://www.lingfieldreserves.org.uk), Tenchleys Manor, St George's Church, Crowhurst Place, Ss Peter and Paul, New Place, Old Cage, Community Orchard, and Lingfield Oak. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Royal Oak](https://www.thegrumpymole.co.uk/oxted) in Staffhurst Wood. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Hurst Green to Upper Warlingham**: A shorter variant of [Hurst Green to Riddlesdown](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-riddlesdown/). Heathland common, golf courses, farmland, North Downs escarpment, open downland, woodland strips, and views across the Caterham Valley. Sights: Limpsfield Common, River Eden, Titsey church, Tatsfield church, and Henley Wood Enclosure. Steep descent. Lunch at the Old Ship in Tatsfield. 19 km.

**Hurst Green to Sevenoaks via Chartwell**: An alternative variant of [Hurst Green to Chiddingstone Causeway](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hurst-green-to-chiddingstone-causeway/). Easterly direction and mainly woodland walking. Sights: [Chartwell](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Ide Hill. Lunch at the [NT restaurant at Chartwell](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) in Chartwell, or the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/) in Ide Hill. 21 km.

0.0086166,51.5688027.name: Leytonstone
0.0086166,51.5688027.ramblerNote:
**[Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/)**: Open woodland, densely forested areas, grassy paths, earth tracks, ponds, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, and High Stone. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov, Apr and May. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 13 km.

**Leytonstone to Chingford**: A shorter variant of [Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/). Open woodland, earth tracks, grassy paths, ponds, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge) and Epping Forest. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov, Apr and May. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 9 km.

**Leytonstone to Epping**: A longer variant of [Leytonstone to Loughton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leytonstone-to-loughton/). Open woodland, earth tracks, forest trails, ponds, Iron Age earthworks, and gentle slopes. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/queen-elizabeths-hunting-lodge), Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, and Ambresbury Banks. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch at the [Butlers Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Chingford. 20 km.

0.0089218,51.4657989.name: Blackheath
0.0089218,51.4657989.ramblerNote:
**[Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Blackheath](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/)**: Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, Docklands waterfront, and financial district. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Blackheath, Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Meridian, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Crossrail Place Roof Garden, One Tree Hill, SS Great Eastern site, Discover Greenwich Visitor Centre, Millennium Sundial, Vanbrugh Castle, Morden College, The Paragon, Anglo-Saxon tumuli, and Blackheath Halls. Lunch at the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com) in Greenwich, the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk) in Greenwich, or the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk) in Ballast Quay, Greenwich. 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**[Blackheath to Deptford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-deptford/)**: Urban London, genteel residential streets, Georgian and Victorian estates, large open heathland, well-kept parks, river valleys, Victorian cemeteries, and green hilltops with panoramic city views. Sights: Blackheath Cator Estate, Morden College, The Paragon, All Saints' Blackheath, Greenwich Park, St. Margaret of Antioch Church, Boone's Almshouses, Lee Manor House, Manor House Gardens, Mountsfield Park, One Tree Hill, St. Augustine's Church, Camberwell New Cemetery, Honor Oak Crematorium, Rivoli Ballroom, Brockley and Ladywell Cemetery, Hilly Fields Park, Millennium Stone Circle, Blythe Hill Fields, Telegraph Hill Upper Park, Telegraph Hill Lower Park, Deptford Market Yard, Albany Theatre, and Ladywell Fields. Lunch at the Mountsfield Park Café in Catford, the Pistachios in the Park in Hilly Fields, or the Blythe Hill Tavern in Honor Oak Park. 22 km. 5 hours.

**Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Whale on the Wharf sculpture**: An alternative variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, eastern Isle of Dogs riverside path, and Docklands waterfront. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), and Whale on the Wharf sculpture. Lunch at the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com) in Greenwich, the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk) in Greenwich, or the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk) in Ballast Quay, Greenwich. 11 km.

**Blackheath to Canary Wharf via Mudchute Park & Farm**: A longer variant of [Blackheath to Canary Wharf](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/blackheath-to-canary-wharf/). Leafy Georgian avenues, open heath, parkland, Royal Observatory hill, riverside Thames Path, Docklands waterfront, Mudchute Park & Farm, and Isle of Dogs circuit. Sights: [Greenwich Park](https://www.royalparks.org.uk/parks/greenwich-park), [Old Royal Naval College](https://www.ornc.org), [Royal Museums Greenwich](https://www.rmg.co.uk), National Maritime Museum, Queen's House, Royal Observatory, Cutty Sark, [Ranger's House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/rangers-house-the-wernher-collection), Painted Hall, Flamsteed House, Greenwich Foot Tunnel, [Mudchute Park & Farm](https://www.mudchute.org), SS Great Eastern site, Crossrail Place Roof Garden, [Canary Wharf outdoor public art](https://canarywharf.com/the-estate/art-culture/art-on-the-estate), Millwall Railway Viaduct, and Isle of Dogs Memorial Garden. Lunch at the [Plume of Feathers](https://plumegreenwich.com) in Greenwich, the [Trafalgar Tavern](https://www.trafalgartavern.co.uk) in Greenwich, or the [Cutty Sark](https://www.cuttysarkse10.co.uk) in Ballast Quay, Greenwich. 18 km. 4 hours.

0.009471,51.6325345.name: Chingford
0.009471,51.6325345.ramblerNote: **Chingford to Epping**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Ancient woodland, forest rides, gravel tracks, and meadows. Sights: [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Connaught Water, and Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Rangers Road, the Original Tea Hut in High Beach, the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home) in High Beach, the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//) in Epping, or the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/) in Epping. 13 km.

0.0095073,51.3765959.name: Hayes
0.0095073,51.3765959.ramblerNote:
**[Hayes Circular Walk](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-circular/)**: Three commons, coppiced woodland, open heathland, gorse, ponds, riverside paths, and suburban road crossings. Sights: Hayes Common, Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Keston Common, West Wickham Common, Ravensbourne Lodge, and Iron Age mound and ditch. Busy road crossings. Lunch at the [Fox Inn](https://thefoxkeston.co.uk/) in Keston, the [Greyhound](https://www.greyhound.pub/) in Keston, the [Triple Two Cafe](https://tripletwocoffee.co.uk/keston/) in Keston, or the [Heathfields](https://heathfieldskeston.co.uk/) in Keston. 9 km.

**[Hayes to Knockholt](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hayes-to-knockholt/)**: Fields, woods, valleys, heathland, commons, and London Green Belt countryside. Sights: [Down House](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/server.php?show=nav.14922), Keston Ponds, Caesar's Well, Wilberforce Oak, Holwood House, Downe Bank Nature Reserve, St Peter and St Paul Church, and Hayes Common. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Blacksmith's Arms](http://www.theblacksmithsarms.co.uk/) in Cudham, the Queens Head in Downe, or the George & Dragon in Downe. 16 km.

0.0113494,50.8705975.name: Lewes
0.0113494,50.8705975.ramblerNote:
**[Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/)**: Rambler favourite! Three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. Sights: Mount Caburn and Lewes. Three steep climbs. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease, the Trevor Arms in Glynde, the Steamworks in Glynde, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms in Glynde. 23 km.

**[Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/)**: South Downs ridges, open downland, sweeping valley, remote hidden valley, coastal shingle beach, and recreation ground. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort, Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Lunch at the Ram Inn in West Firle. 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/)**: Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, bridleways, old racecourse, and town streets. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Lewes Old Racecourse, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Black Cap, Battle of Lewes viewpoint, Baxter's Field, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton, or the Swan Inn in Falmer. 23 km.

**[Lewes Circular via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/)**: Steep hills, chalk ridge, South Downs Way, secluded valley, flat river levee, and historic town. Sights: Lewes Castle, Mount Caburn, Firle Place, Monk's House, Harveys Brewery, Parish Church of St Thomas a Becket, and White Hart Hotel. Steep hills. Avoid Dec/Jan. Lunch at the Ram Inn in Firle. 23 km. 6 hours.

**Lewes to Southease via Glynde and Southease**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, long gradual descent to Rodmell, chalk paths, and open views. Sights: Mount Caburn. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease. 11 km.

**Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease & West Firle**: A longer variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Three downland ridges, extended South Downs Way, pretty village of West Firle, chalk paths, and panoramic views. Sights: Mount Caburn and West Firle. Very long walk. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, the Ram Inn in West Firle, or the Trevor Arms in Glynde. 29 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Lewes Castle**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, South Downs Way, and Ouse valley. Sights: Lewes Castle. 11 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Lewes Priory**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Saltdean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-saltdean/). Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs views, historic town, picturesque village, and return by train from Southease. Sights: Lewes Castle, Lewes Priory, Anne of Cleves House, and Monk's House. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 12 km.

**Lewes to Glynde via West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). Circular ridge path, open downland, and valley descent into Glynde. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort. Lunch at the [Trevor Arms](https://www.thetrevor.co.uk/) in Glynde, or the [Steamworks](https://steamworksbars.co.uk/about-us/glynde-steamworks-is-open-6472323) in Glynde. 6 km.

**Lewes to Glynde via West Firle Circular**: A shorter variant of [Lewes via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/). Steep hill with views, secluded valley, and chalk downland. Sights: Lewes Castle and Mount Caburn. Steep hill. 5 km.

**Lewes to Plumpton**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, and farmland paths. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Lewes Castle, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton. 17 km.

**Lewes to Seaford omitting West Firle**: A variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, remote valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort and St Andrew's Church Bishopstone. Steep climbs. Lunch at the [Trevor Arms](https://www.thetrevor.co.uk/) in Glynde, or the [Steamworks](https://steamworksbars.co.uk/about-us/glynde-steamworks-is-open-6472323) in Glynde. 18 km.

**Lewes to Brighton**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, and urban Brighton. Sights: Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, and [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Lunch at the Juggs in Kingston. 19 km.

**Lewes to Southease via Kipling Gardens**: A variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, heart of downs, and Ouse valley. Sights: Lewes Castle and Kipling Gardens. Lunch at the Juggs in Kingston. 20 km.

**Lewes to Cooksbridge via Swanborough and Black Cap & Old Hamsey Church**: A variant of [Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/). Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, River Ouse bank, farmland, and country lanes. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Black Cap, Old Hamsey Church, and Ashcombe Windmill. Track slippery when wet. Lunch at the Blacksmith's Arms in Offham. 24 km.

**Lewes to Seaford Walk via South Downs**: A longer variant of [Lewes to Saltdean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-saltdean/). Chalk grassland ridges, South Downs, Southease station midpoint, and extended coastal finish at Seaford. Sights: Lewes Castle and Monk's House. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 24 km.

**Lewes to Seaford via St Andrew's Church**: A longer variant of [Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/). River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, and seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 24 km. 6.5 hours.

**Lewes Circular via Northease Manor**: An alternative variant of [Lewes to Saltdean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-saltdean/). Lower-lying inland route, Northease Manor, South Downs villages, and chalk downland. Sights: Lewes Castle, Northease Manor, and Monk's House. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell.
0.0113494,50.8705975.publicRamblerNote:
**[Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/)**: Rambler favourite! Three distinct downland ridges, wide open chalk paths, panoramic views, historic town streets, and gentle valleys. Sights: Mount Caburn and Lewes. Three steep climbs. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease, the Trevor Arms in Glynde, the Steamworks in Glynde, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms in Glynde. 23 km.

**[Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/)**: South Downs ridges, open downland, sweeping valley, remote hidden valley, coastal shingle beach, and recreation ground. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort, Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Lunch at the Ram Inn in West Firle. 22 km. 6 hours.

**[Lewes Circular via Swanborough and Black Cap](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-swanborough-and-black-cap/)**: Open chalk downland, South Downs ridgeline, woods, wetland, bridleways, old racecourse, and town streets. Sights: Priory of St Pancras, Cockshut Stream Restoration Project, Lewes Old Racecourse, Lewes Castle, Barbican House Museum, Black Cap, Battle of Lewes viewpoint, Baxter's Field, and Ashcombe Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Half Moon in Plumpton, or the Swan Inn in Falmer. 23 km.

**[Lewes Circular via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/)**: Steep hills, chalk ridge, South Downs Way, secluded valley, flat river levee, and historic town. Sights: Lewes Castle, Mount Caburn, Firle Place, Monk's House, Harveys Brewery, Parish Church of St Thomas a Becket, and White Hart Hotel. Steep hills. Avoid Dec/Jan. Lunch at the Ram Inn in Firle. 23 km. 6 hours.

**Lewes to Southease via Glynde and Southease**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, long gradual descent to Rodmell, chalk paths, and open views. Sights: Mount Caburn. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease. 11 km.

0.0306729,50.83111.ramblerNote:
**[Southease to Seaford via Monk's House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/)**: River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland with coastal views, pretty villages, and seaside esplanade. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Southease Circular via King George V Memorial](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/)**: South Downs hills, chalk paths, river embankment, coastal clifftop, Undercliff sea-level promenade, open downland, arable fields, and quiet downland valleys. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, King George V Memorial, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, Piddinghoe Church, Harvey's Cross, Breaky Bottom Vineyard, and Rottingdean Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Papachino's Gateway Cafe in Peacehaven, the Dewdrop Inn in Peacehaven, the [Burger and Bird](https://www.burgerandbird.com/) in Peacehaven, the Big Mouths Burger Bar in Peacehaven, the [Stonehouse Pizza and Carvery](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Maloncho](https://www.maloncho-restaurant.co.uk/) in Peacehaven, the [Kappadokya](https://www.kappadokya.co.uk/) in Telscombe Cliffs, the Thai Elephant Two in Telscombe Cliffs, the Tavern at Telscombe Cliffs in Telscombe Cliffs, or the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/) in Telscombe Cliffs. 23 km.

**[Southease to Eastbourne via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/)**: Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, and Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch at the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop in Alfriston. 27 km.

**Southease to Seaford via St Andrew's Church**: A shorter variant of [Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/). Direct route across South Downs, open downland with coastal views, and seaside esplanade. Sights: St Andrew's Church. 12 km. 4 hours.

**Southease to Lewes**: A shorter variant of [Lewes via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/). Flat valley walk along River Ouse levee into historic Lewes. Sights: Lewes Castle and Monk's House. 6 km.

**Southease Circular via Monk's House**: A shorter variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, South Downs Way, and circular route back to station. Sights: Monk's House and Lewes Castle. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the Juggs in Kingston. 15 km.

**Southease to Bishopstone**: A shorter variant of [Southease to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-seaford/). River valley, ridge-top South Downs, open downland, and pretty village. Sights: Monk's House and St Andrew's Church. Lunch at the [Abergavenny Arms](http://www.abergavennyarms.com/) in Rodmell. 16 km.

**Southease Circular via Centenary Park & Breaky Bottom Vineyard**: A variant of [Southease Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/). South Downs hills, chalk paths, river embankment, coastal clifftop, open downland, and short section of concrete track. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, and Breaky Bottom Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Papachino's Gateway Cafe in Peacehaven, the Dewdrop Inn in Peacehaven, or the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/) in Telscombe Cliffs. 18 km.

**Southease to Brighton**: A longer variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, open escarpment, quaint village, seafront promenade, and urban Brighton. Sights: Monk's House, Lewes Castle, Kipling Gardens, Brighton Pier, and [Brighton Royal Pavilion](http://www.royalpavilion.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the Juggs in Kingston. 23 km.

**Southease Circular via Kipling Gardens**: A longer variant of [Southease to Rottingdean or Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-rottingdean-or-brighton/). Flat Ouse valley, riverbank, chalk downs, seafront, Saltdean, and circular route back over downs to station. Sights: Monk's House, Lewes Castle, and Kipling Gardens. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Abergavenny Arms in Rodmell, or the Juggs in Kingston. 23 km.

**Southease Circular via Balsdean deserted village**: A longer variant of [Southease Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-circular/). South Downs hills, chalk bridleways, open downland valleys, and wider views to both sides of the Downs. Sights: Centenary Park, Meridian Monument, Undercliff Walk, Telscombe Tye, St Peter's Church Southease, Balsdean deserted village, and Rottingdean Windmill. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Smugglers' Rest](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thesmugglersrestpeacehaven#/) in Telscombe Cliffs. 26 km.

**Southease to Eastbourne via Cuckmere Haven**: A longer variant of [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/). Open downland ridges, steep ascents, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters chalk cliffs, and coastal path. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Beachy Head, and Eastbourne Pier. Very hilly afternoon. Lunch at the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop in Alfriston. 33 km.

**[Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, open flats, commons, forest rides, gravel tracks, residential roads, and meadows. Sights: [Epping Forest](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Epping_Forest), [Queen Elizabeth's Hunting Lodge](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), [The View Epping Forest Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/chingford-visitor-centre), Ambresbury Banks Iron Age Hillfort, Wanstead Flats, Hollow Pond, Connaught Water, and Boating Lake in Highams Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Blakesley Arms in Manor Park, the Lakeside Diner in Whipps Cross, the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/) in Highams Park, the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Rangers Road, the Original Tea Hut in High Beach, the Oyster Shack & Seafood Bar in High Beach, the King's Oak Café in High Beach, the [King's Oak Hotel](https://www.kingsoakhotel.com/home) in High Beach, the [Forest Gate Inn](http://www.forestgateinnepping.co.uk/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Perky Blenders](https://perkyblenders.com/) in Ivy Chimneys, the [Duke of Wellington](https://dowepping.co.uk//) in Epping, the Dada Café in Epping, the [George & Dragon](http://www.georgeanddragonepping.co.uk/) in Epping, the Black Lion in Epping, or the [Funky Monk](https://funkymonk.co.uk/) in Epping. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**Manor Park to Chingford**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). Open flats, commons, woodland, forest rides, and gravel tracks. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Blakesley Arms in Manor Park, the Lakeside Diner in Whipps Cross, the [Humphry's Café](https://highamspark.london/cafes/humphrys-cafe/) in Highams Park, or the [Larder at Butler's Retreat](https://larderlondon.co.uk/the-larder-at-butlers-retreat/) in Rangers Road. 12 km.

**Manor Park to Buckhurst Hill**: A shorter variant of [Manor Park to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/manor-park-to-epping/). 0.0551243,50.7949162.name: Newhaven Town
0.0551243,50.7949162.ramblerNote:
**[Newhaven Town to Brighton](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/)**: Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, and busy seaside promenade and boardwalk. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, and Saltdean Lido. Exposed in high wind. Lunch at the Hope Inn in Newhaven, the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern) in Telescombe, the Smuggler's Rest in Peacehaven, the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/) in Saltdean, the Molly's at the Beach in Rottingdean, the White Horse in Rottingdean, or the West Quay in Brighton Marina. 18 km.

**[Newhaven Town to Newhaven Harbour via Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-circular/)**: Clifftop coast path, woods and fields, river bank, South Downs foothills, hills, shingle beach, and deserted village ruins. Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, Tide Mills, [Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](http://sailinginsussex.org/galley), Bishopstone Beach Halt station platform, and Chaley Heritage Marine Hospital excavations. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Bridge Inn in Newhaven, the Ark in Newhaven, the Mamoosh Riverside in Newhaven, the Abyss Beer and Pizza in Newhaven, the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/) in Denton, Newhaven, or the Hampden Arms in South Heighton, Newhaven. 24 km.

**Newhaven Town to Newhaven Harbour via Newhaven Fort**: A shorter variant of [Newhaven Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-circular/). Clifftop coast path, woods and fields, river bank, gentler hillside paths avoiding high Downs, shingle beach, and deserted village ruins. Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, and Tide Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Bridge Inn in Newhaven, the Ark in Newhaven, or the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/) in Denton, Newhaven. 20 km.

**Newhaven Town to Portslade (via Brighton and Hove)**: A longer variant of [Newhaven to Brighton and Portslade](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/). Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, busy promenade, beach huts, and wide seafront promenade. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, Saltdean Lido, i360 Tower, and Brighton West Pier. Exposed in high wind. Lunch at the Hope Inn in Newhaven, the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern) in Telescombe, the Smuggler's Rest in Peacehaven, the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/) in Saltdean, the Molly's at the Beach in Rottingdean, the White Horse in Rottingdean, the West Quay in Brighton Marina, or the [Big Beach Cafe](http://www.bigbeachcafe.com/) in Hove. 23 km.

Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, and Saltdean Lido. Exposed in high wind. Lunch at the Hope Inn in Newhaven, the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern) in Telescombe, the Smuggler's Rest in Peacehaven, the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/) in Saltdean, the Molly's at the Beach in Rottingdean, the White Horse in Rottingdean, or the West Quay in Brighton Marina. 18 km.

**[Newhaven Town to Newhaven Harbour via Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-circular/)**: Clifftop coast path, woods and fields, river bank, South Downs foothills, hills, shingle beach, and deserted village ruins. Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, Tide Mills, [Newhaven and Seaford Sailing Club](http://sailinginsussex.org/galley), Bishopstone Beach Halt station platform, and Chaley Heritage Marine Hospital excavations. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Bridge Inn in Newhaven, the Ark in Newhaven, the Mamoosh Riverside in Newhaven, the Abyss Beer and Pizza in Newhaven, the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/) in Denton, Newhaven, or the Hampden Arms in South Heighton, Newhaven. 24 km.

**Newhaven Town to Newhaven Harbour via Newhaven Fort**: A shorter variant of [Newhaven Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-circular/). Clifftop coast path, woods and fields, river bank, gentler hillside paths avoiding high Downs, shingle beach, and deserted village ruins. Sights: Newhaven Fort, St John the Evangelist Church, and Tide Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Bridge Inn in Newhaven, the Ark in Newhaven, or the [Flying Fish](https://www.flyingfishdenton.co.uk/) in Denton, Newhaven. 20 km.

**Newhaven Town to Portslade (via Brighton and Hove)**: A longer variant of [Newhaven to Brighton and Portslade](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/newhaven-to-brighton-and-portslade/). Harbourside path, white chalk cliffs, clifftop and undercliff concrete paths, shingle beach, busy promenade, beach huts, and wide seafront promenade. Sights: [Newhaven Fort](http://www.newhavenfort.org.uk), Brighton Pier, Brighton Marina, Brighton Pavilion, The Lanes, Saltdean Lido, i360 Tower, and Brighton West Pier. Exposed in high wind. Lunch at the Hope Inn in Newhaven, the [Peacehaven](https://www.stonehouserestaurants.co.uk/nationalsearch/southeast/thepeacehavenpeacehaven) in Peacehaven, the [Telescombe Tavern](http://telscombetavern.wix.com/thetelscombetavern) in Telescombe, the Smuggler's Rest in Peacehaven, the [Whitecliffs](https://www.facebook.com/whitecliffssaltdean/) in Saltdean, the Molly's at the Beach in Rottingdean, the White Horse in Rottingdean, the West Quay in Brighton Marina, or the [Big Beach Cafe](http://www.bigbeachcafe.com/) in Hove. 23 km.

0.0553234,51.6412266.name: Loughton

**[Loughton to Epping via Loughton Camp](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/)**: Ancient woodland, pollarded oaks, beeches and hornbeams, open countryside, farmland, parkland near a ruined country house, and bustling market town. Sights: Epping Forest, Loughton Camp, [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre), and [Copped Hall](http://www.coppedhalltrust.org.uk/). Can be muddy. Best in Dec–May. Lunch at the [High Beach Visitor Centre](https://www.cityoflondon.gov.uk/things-to-do/green-spaces/epping-forest/where-to-go-in-epping-forest/high-beach-visitor-centre) in High Beach, the [Kings Oak](http://www.kingsoakhotel.com/) in High Beach, or the [Horseshoes](https://www.instagram.com/thehorseshoesupshire/?hl=en) in Upshire. 13 km. 4 hours.

**Loughton to Epping via Epping Forest**: A shorter variant of [Loughton to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/loughton-to-epping/). Almost entirely woodland on the Three Forest Way, crossing roads, and with a shorter start through Loughton. Sights: Epping Forest. Good midsummer evening. 9 km.

Open fields, NE ascent to Toy's Hill, and easterly woodland walking after Ide Hill. Sights: Toy's Hill and Ide Hill. Lunch at the [Cock Inn](https://cockinnidehill.co.uk/) in Ide Hill. 17 km.

0.067215,51.2001369.ramblerNote:
**[Edenbridge Town Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/)**: Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com) in Edenbridge, or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk) in Edenbridge. 14 km. 3 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Edenbridge**: An alternative variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, and flat Eden Valley fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com) in Edenbridge, or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk) in Edenbridge. 12 km. 2.5 hours.

**Edenbridge Town to Hever via Great Stone Bridge Trust**: A longer variant of [Edenbridge Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/edenbridge-figure-of-8/). Gentle farmland, water meadows, riverside paths, farm airstrip, flat Eden Valley, and fields to Hever. Sights: [Edenbridge](https://www.visitedenbridge.com), [River Eden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Eden,_Kent), [Eden Valley Museum](https://evmt.org.uk), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), Stone Bridge, Great Stone Bridge Trust, Edenbridge Water Meadows, Gabriel's Fishery, and Devil's Den. Can be waterlogged. Lunch at the [Old Eden](https://www.theoldeden.com) in Edenbridge, or the [Ye Olde Crown](https://www.yeoldecrown.co.uk) in Edenbridge. 18 km. 4 hours.
