0.0703481,50.8591176.name: Glynde
0.0703481,50.8591176.privateNote: LON Trainline says: 1h25m — Victoria via Lewes
0.0703481,50.8591176.ramblerNote:
**[Glynde to Seaford via Firle Place](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/glynde-to-seaford/)**: Rambler favourite! South Downs ridge, chalk downland, Firle Park, river valley, ancient villages, forest, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and esplanade. Sights: Firle Place, Church of St Peter, Alfriston Clergy House, Parish Church of St Andrew, and Seaford Museum. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston, the Star Inn in Alfriston, the Ye Olde Smugglers Inn in Alfriston, the Wingrove Inn in Alfriston, the Chestnuts Tea Room in Alfriston, the Singing Kettle in Alfriston, the Poco Restaurant & Wine Bar in Alfriston, the Plough and Harrow pub in Litlington, or the Littlington Tea Gardens in Litlington. 23 km. 7 hours.

**Glynde to Southease via Glynde and Southease**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge, South Downs Way, open chalk paths, and panoramic views. Lunch at the YHA Courtyard Cafe in Southease. 6 km.

**Glynde to Lewes**: A shorter variant of [Lewes Circular via Glynde and Southease](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-circular-via-glynde-and-southease/). Rambler favourite! Downland ridge via Mount Caburn, steep descent into Lewes, and chalk paths. Sights: Mount Caburn. Steep descent. Lunch at the Trevor Arms in Glynde, the Steamworks in Glynde, or the Little Cottage Tea Rooms in Glynde. 6 km.

**Glynde to Southease via West Firle Circular**: A shorter variant of [Lewes via West Firle Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-via-west-firle-circular/). South Downs Way chalk ridge and extensive views inland and to Newhaven and the Channel. Sights: Firle Place. Lunch at the Ram Inn in Firle. 11 km.

**Glynde to Seaford omitting West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Mount Caburn Iron Age Hill Fort and St Andrew's Church Bishopstone. No lunch-time pub. 11 km.

**Glynde to Seaford via West Firle**: A shorter variant of [Lewes to Seaford via West Firle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/lewes-to-seaford-via-west-firle/). South Downs ridges, open downland, hidden valley, and coastal approach. Sights: Firle Park, St Peter's Church, St Andrew's Church Bishopstone, and Firle Tower. Steep climbs. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch at the Ram Inn in West Firle. 16 km.

**Glynde to Eastbourne**: An alternative variant of [Southease to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/southease-to-eastbourne/). Open treeless downland ridges, steep ascents, scenic Cuckmere Valley, coastal promenade, beach, and pier. Sights: Firle Beacon, Long Burgh Long Barrow, Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, Beachy Head, Wish Tower, Eastbourne Pier, and Italian Gardens. Steep ascents. Lunch at the Pubs, tea rooms and village shop in Alfriston. 27 km.

0.0745614,51.388626.name: Petts Wood
0.0745614,51.388626.ramblerNote: **[Petts Wood Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petts-wood-circular/)**: Suburban woodlands, wide woodland tracks, heathland, valleys, moated manor ruins, and open green spaces. Sights: [Scadbury Park](https://www.scadbury-park.org.uk/), [Chislehurst Caves](http://chislehurst-caves.co.uk/), William Willett Memorial Sundial, Scadbury Park Moated Manor House Ruins, and St Nicholas Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bull's Head](https://www.thebullsheadhotel.co.uk/) in Chislehurst. 12 km.
0.0745614,51.388626.publicRamblerNote: **[Petts Wood Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/petts-wood-circular/)**: Suburban woodlands, wide woodland tracks, heathland, valleys, moated manor ruins, and open green spaces. 0.0826201,50.7799859.name: Bishopstone
0.0826201,50.7799859.ramblerNote:
**[Bishopstone Circular via Saxon Church of St Andrew](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/)**: Steep repeated climbs and descents, chalk downland, grassy ridges, vineyard tracks, valley paths, quiet village lanes, and open hilltop views to sea and Downs. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, St Michael and All Angels church, [Cuckmere Pilgrim Path](http://cuckmerepilgrimpath.org.uk), Bishopstone station pillbox, and Alciston Court Farm dovecote. Can be overgrown. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch at the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/) in Berwick. 21 km.

**Bishopstone to Berwick**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way, and descent to Berwick via Vanguard Way. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow and St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch at the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/) in Berwick. 13 km.

**Bishopstone to Southease**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking, and descent to Southease. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Bishopstone to Glynde**: A shorter variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland climbs, South Downs Way ridge walking westward, and descent to Glynde. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. 16 km.

**Bishopstone Circular via Long Burgh Barrow**: A variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). Downland, South Downs Way, and alternative circular return via Walk 2/26. Sights: Long Burgh Barrow. Can be overgrown. 21 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford via Alfriston and Bishopstone**: An alternative variant of [Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/). Seafront, chalk downland, vineyard tracks, open ridgelines, and coastal cliff finish over Seaford Head. Sights: Seaford Head, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, and Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston. 22 km.

**Bishopstone to Seaford via Saxon Church of St Andrew**: A longer variant of [Bishopstone Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bishopstone-circular/). All main walk terrain plus seafront promenade into Seaford. Sights: Saxon Church of St Andrew, Long Burgh Barrow, and St Michael and All Angels church. Can be overgrown. Lunch at the [Cricketers' Arms](https://www.cricketersberwick.co.uk/) in Berwick. 23 km.

0.0948105,51.1813846.name: Hever
0.0948105,51.1813846.ramblerNote:
**[Hever to Leigh via Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/)**: Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, quaint villages, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, Chiddingstone Castle, St Mary's Church, Chiding Stone, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, and Luke Tapestry. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/) in Chiddingstone, the Tulip Tree Tearooms in Chiddingstone, or the [Leicester Arms Hotel](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/) in Penshurst. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Hever to Dormans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-dormans/)**: Undulating countryside, woods, farmland, fields, streams, historic manor houses, iron-age hillfort, remote border area of Kent, and Surrey and Sussex. Sights: Cowden, St Mary Magdalene Church, Sussex Border Path, Waystrode Manor, Crippenden Manor, Vanguard Way, Dry Hill Fort, and Greathed Manor. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaininncowden.com/) in Cowden. 16 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/)**: Low hills, wooded valleys, farm fields, bluebell woods, manor house grounds, golf course, orchards, and remote High Weald countryside. Sights: Crippenden Manor, Leighton Manor, Waystrode Manor, Sussex Border Path, Sussex House Farm, Heathersome's Wood, Bolebroke Castle, [Orchard Tea Room](https://www.perryhillorchards.co.uk/orchard-tea-room), St Mary Magdalene, and [Cowden](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cowden). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Fountain](https://www.thefountaincowden.co.uk) in Cowden. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Hever to Tonbridge via Tonbridge Castle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/)**: Gentle farmland, orchards, river valleys, parkland, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, Withers, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Hever to Cowden**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Ashurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-ashurst/). **Hever to Penshurst via Penshurst Place**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/). Farmland, river valley, parkland, and historic village. Sights: [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St Peter's Church Hever, St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Chippens Bank House, and Withers. **Hever to Leigh via Hever Castle**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Leigh](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/). Undulating hills, open fields, beech woods, rivers, ponds, and historic estates. Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), St Peter's Church, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), and St John the Baptist Church. Can be muddy. **Hever to Tonbridge via Chiddingstone Castle**: A longer variant of [Hever to Leigh](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-leigh/). Sights: [Hever Castle](http://www.hevercastle.co.uk/), Chiddingstone Castle, [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), and Haysden Country Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Castle Inn](http://www.thecastleinnchiddingstone.com/) in Chiddingstone.

**[Uckfield Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-via-van-hoogstraatens-path-circular/)**: Undulating low Weald countryside, fields, green lanes, woods, golf course, and suburban estates. Sights: [Hempstead Nature Reserve](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/enviro.html), [Sussex Horse Rescue Centre](http://sussexhorserescuetrust.org.uk/), [Framfield](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Framfield), [East Sussex National Golf Resort and Spa](http://www.eastsussexnational.co.uk/dine-drink/hotel-lounge-bar/), [Boothland Wood](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/enviro.html), [Uckfield](http://www.localauthoritypublishing.co.uk/councils/uckfield/index.html), and Framfield Church. Quite a few stiles. Lunch at the Hare and Hounds in Framfield, or the [Half Way House Tea Room](http://www.eastsussexnational.co.uk/dine-drink/hotel-lounge-bar/) in East Sussex National Golf Resort. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Uckfield to Lewes via Glyndebourne opera house](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/)**: River valleys, fields, woods, golf course, open chalk downland, South Downs escarpment, and historic town. Sights: Horsted Place, Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal), Glyndebourne opera house, Glyndebourne Wind Turbine, Southeram Nature Reserve, Lewes Castle, and Caburn Hillfort. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Anchor Inn in Barcombe Mills. 20 km.

**[Uckfield to Buxted via East Sussex National Golf Course](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/)**: Undulating Low Weald countryside, Uck Valley, golf course, stream valleys, old deer park, parkland, and bluebell woods. Sights: East Sussex National Golf Course, High Cross Estate, Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the [Blackboys Inn](http://www.theblackboys.co.uk/) in Blackboys. 21 km. 5 hours.

**Uckfield to Buxted via Tickerage Mill**: A shorter variant of [Uckfield to Buxted](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-buxted/). Undulating Low Weald countryside, stream valleys, parkland, bluebell woods, and routed through Framfield. Sights: Tickerage Mill, Buxted Park, and Buxted House. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the [Hare & Hounds](https://hareandhounds.net/) in Framfield. 18 km.

**Uckfield to Lewes via Barcombe Mills sluices and river pools**: An alternative variant of [Uckfield to Lewes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-lewes/). River valleys, fields, mill streams, riverside path, small villages, and historic town centre. Sights: Barcombe Mills sluices and river pools, St Mary the Virgin church, St Peter church, Hamsey, Lewes Castle, Lewes Barbican, Horsted Place, and Lavender Line (Dingley Dell terminal). Lunch at the Anchor Inn in Barcombe Mills. 18 km.

**Uckfield to Balcombe**: A shorter variant of [Uckfield to Pease Pottage](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/uckfield-to-pease-pottage/). Linear walk through East Sussex countryside, villages, and farmland ending at Balcombe station. Lunch at the [Griffin Inn](https://thegriffininn.co.uk/) in Fletching, the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrownhorstedkeynes.co.uk/) in Horsted Keynes, or the [Green Man](http://greenmanhorstedkeynes.co.uk/) in Horsted Keynes. 29 km.

0.1000064,50.7729203.name: Seaford
0.1000064,50.7729203.privateNote: LON Trainline says 1h25m - Victoria via Lewes
0.1000064,50.7729203.ramblerNote:
**[Seaford to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Dramatic clifftop chalk downs, undulating Seven Sisters cliffs, shingle beaches, river haven, and elegant seafront promenade. Sights: [Birling Gap](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/birling-gap-and-the-seven-sisters), Seven Sisters, Belle Tout, and Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Saltmarsh Kitchen in Exceat, or the Grab and Go in Exceat. 21 km.

**[Seaford Circular via Alfriston and Bishopstone](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/seaford-circular/)**: Steep coastal cliffs, chalk downland, open panoramic ridgelines, vineyard tracks, village greens, shingle beach, and seafront promenade. Sights: Seaford Head, Martello Tower, Hope Gap, Rathfinny Vineyard, Alfriston Church, George Inn, South Hill Barn, and Bishopstone Church. Steep coastal climbs. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston, the Brewster's Coffee Co in South Hill Barn, or the Papuchinos at the Galley in Seaford. 25 km.

**Seaford to Berwick**: An alternative variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). Coastal chalk cliffs, downland, river valley, open fields, and village lanes. Sights: Seaford Head, Seven Sisters, High and Over Hill, Cuckmere Haven, Berwick church, and Martello Tower. Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cricketer's Arms in Berwick, or the Berwick Arms in Berwick. 16 km.

0.1033213,51.6717759.ramblerNote: **[Theydon Bois to Epping](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/theydon-bois-to-epping/)**: Marshland, woods, gently rolling meadow-land, scenic hamlets, quiet tarmac roads with grassy verges, and motorway crossings. Sights: GreenAcres and [Theydon Oak pub](https://theydonoak.co.uk/). Boardwalks rotten in places. Lunch at the [Dog House](https://www.facebook.com/themoletrappubepping/) in Woodhatch, the [Kings Head](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thekingsheadnorthweald#/) in North Weald, or the [Garnon Bushes](https://www.garnonbushespub.com/) in Theydon Garnon. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), [Hever Castle](https://www.hevercastle.co.uk), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, St Mary, Chiddingstone, St Peter, Hever, Chiding Stone, Leicester Square, and Harden Vineyard. Sights: Chipping Ongar Motte-and-Double-Bailey, St. Andrew's Church, Greensted-juxta-Ongar, St. Laurence Church, Blackmore, and Gernon Bushes SSSI. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Theydon Oak in Coopersale. 26 km. 6 hours.

Sights: Chipping Ongar Motte-and-Double-Bailey, St. Andrew's Church, Greensted-juxta-Ongar, St. Laurence Church, Blackmore, and Gernon Bushes SSSI. Can be muddy. Sights: Magpie Bottom, Shoreham Aircraft Museum, and Mount Vineyard. Descents can be slippery. Best in Sep, Oct, Nov, Mar, Apr and May. Lunch at the Kings Arms in Shoreham, the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 12 km.

**[Knockholt Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-circular/)**: Rural fields with wildflowers, bluebell woods, and several village churches. Sights: [Down House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/home-of-charles-darwin-down-house/). Numerous stiles throughout. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the [Queens Head](https://www.queensheaddowne.com/) in Downe, the [George & Dragon](https://www.georgeanddragondowne.com/) in Downe, or the [Blacksmiths Arms](https://www.theblacksmithsarmscudham.com/) in Cudham. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**Knockholt to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Knockholt to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/knockholt-to-otford/). Wooded hills, steep ridge climb, and descent into village. **[Buxted Circular Walk](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/buxted-circular/)**: Gentle mix of fields and woods, Weald countryside, and views of the South Downs ridge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Crow and Gate in Poundgate. 16 km.

**Buxted to Ashurst**: An alternative variant of [Ashurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-circular/). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crow & Gate Inn](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/thecrowandgatecrowborough) in Poundgate. 24 km. 6 hours.

Sights: Church of St Mary the Virgin, All Saints Church Little Shelford, Church of St Edmunds Hauxton, All Saints Church Haslingfield, Travelling Telescope, Parish Church of St Andrew and St Mary Grantchester, Orchard Tea Rooms, Old Vicarage Grantchester, Rupert Brooke statue, Queens College, King's College, Clare College, Wren Library, Clare Bridge, Trinity Hall, Gate of Honour, Gonville and Caius College, Trinity College, St John's College, Sidney Sussex College, Great St Mary's Church, Church of St Edward, Saint Bene't's Church, Eagle pub, Corpus Christi College, Old Cavendish Lab, Pembroke College, Peterhouse, Little St Mary's Church, Fitzwilliam Museum, and Cambridge University Botanic Gardens. High tarmac count. Lunch at the Little Rose in Haslingfield. 19 km.

0.1526024,51.1284231.name: Ashurst

**[Ashurst to Eridge via Speldhurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/)**: Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, country lanes, wooded valleys, sandstone outcrops, heath, and meadows. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Ashurst Place, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com) in Speldhurst. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Ashurst Circular via St Michael and All Angels](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-circular/)**: Low hills, Medway valley, ancient woodland, Wealden farmland, Five Hundred Acre Wood, wildflower meadows, and quaint villages. Sights: [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), Pooh Sticks Bridge, Five Hundred Acre Wood, [Piglet's Tearoom](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk/tearoom), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, and Linkhorns Farmhouse. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk) in Withyham. 20 km. 5 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Spa Valley Railway & Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Eridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/). High Weald valleys, bluebell woods, Burrswood estate, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, and nature reserves. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), Burrswood, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com) in Groombridge, or the Junction Inn in Groombridge. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Groombridge Place & Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/). Low hills, wooded valleys, field edges, country lanes, sandstone outcrops, and village greens. Sights: [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Hare](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/hare) in Langton Green. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Groombridge Place & Broadwater Warren**: A longer variant of [Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/). Low hills, wooded valleys, field edges, RSPB heathland reserve, sandstone outcrops, and nature reserves. Sights: [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, and Birchden Vineyards. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Hare](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/hare) in Langton Green. 17 km.

**Ashurst to Eridge via Speldhurst & Broadwater Warren**: A longer variant of [Ashurst to Eridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-eridge/). Low hills, steep-sided valleys, field edges, wooded valleys, RSPB heathland reserve, sandstone outcrops, and nature reserves. Sights: [Speldhurst](https://www.speldhurst.org/history), [St Mary the Virgin](https://speldhurstchurch.org/about/building/history), [Groombridge Place](https://www.groombridgeplace.com), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), All Saints Church Langton Green, St John the Evangelist Groombridge, St Thomas the Apostle Groombridge, Birchden Vineyards, Ashurst Place, and Birchden Forge. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [George & Dragon](https://ganddspeldhurst.com) in Speldhurst. 20 km.

**Ashurst to East Grinstead**: A longer variant of [Ashurst to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/). Low hills, Wealden farmland, wooded bridleways, open Ashdown Forest heathland, golf course, disused railway trackbed (Forest Way), and historic town streets. Sights: [Ashdown Forest](https://ashdownforest.org/walking), Ashdown Forest Centre, [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml), Newbridge Mill, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, Pooh Corner, Gallipot Inn, Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun, Monument to Sir Archibald McIndoe, Freshfield Hall, and Brambletye Hotel. Can be muddy. Best in Oct–Nov. Lunch at the [Gallipot Inn](https://www.the-gallipot-inn.co.uk) in Gallipot Street, the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com) in Hartfield, or the [Hatch Inn](https://www.hatchinn.co.uk) in Colemans Hatch. 22 km. 5.5 hours.

**Ashurst Circular via Ashdown Forest**: A longer variant of [Ashurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ashurst-circular/). Low hills, Medway valley, ancient woodland, elevated heathland, Ashdown Forest ridges, Scots pine clumps, and Wealden villages. Sights: [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Ashdown Forest](https://ashdownforest.org/walking), Gills Lap Clump, Greenwood Gate Clump, King's Standing Clump, Heffalump Trap, Pooh Sticks Bridge, Five Hundred Acre Wood, [Piglet's Tearoom](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk/tearoom), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, and Linkhorns Farmhouse. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk) in Withyham. 25 km. 6.5 hours.

0.16054,51.8146205.name: Sawbridgeworth
0.16054,51.8146205.ramblerNote: **[Sawbridgeworth to Bishop's Stortford via St. Andrew's Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sawbridgeworth-to-bishops-stortford/)**: Gently rolling arable countryside, green lanes, quiet country roads, bluebell woods, river navigation towpath, and marshland nature reserves. Sights: Henry Moore Foundation, St. Andrew's Church, and Thorley Hall and Church. Lunch at the [Rosey Lea Café](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/plan-your-visit-studios-gardens/rosey-lea-cafe/) in Perry Green, the Bull Inn in Much Hadham, or the [Prince of Wales](http://www.thepow.co.uk/) in Green Tye. 23 km. 5 hours.

Lunch at the [Rosey Lea Café](https://henry-moore.org/studios-and-gardens/plan-your-visit-studios-gardens/rosey-lea-cafe/) in Perry Green, the Bull Inn in Much Hadham, or the [Prince of Wales](http://www.thepow.co.uk/) in Green Tye. 23 km. 5 hours.

0.1657244,50.8404862.name: Berwick
0.1657244,50.8404862.ramblerNote:
**[Berwick to Eastbourne via Filching Manor Motor Museum](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, and seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, and Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington, or the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Berwick Circular via Upper Dicker Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-via-upper-dicker-circular/)**: Gently undulating farmland, open fields, bluebell woods, reservoir shorepath, and quiet country lanes with fine downs and weald views. Sights: [Michelham Priory](https://sussexpast.co.uk/attraction/michelham-priory/), Abbot's Wood, Arlington Reservoir, Long Man of Wilmington, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be slippery. Lunch at the [Old Oak Inn](https://www.oldoakinnarlington.co.uk/) in Arlington, the [Blue Door Cafe](https://bluedoorcafe.co.uk/) in Michelham Priory, or the [Plough](https://www.plough-upperdicker.co.uk/) in Upper Dicker. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Berwick to Seaford via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Long Man Inn in Wilmington, the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Trawlers in Seaford, the Plough Inn in Seaford, or the Steamworks in Seaford. 20 km.

**Berwick to Polegate**: A shorter variant of [Berwick to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/). Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, Folkington village, and suburban roads at finish. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php). Suburban roads at end. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington. 11 km.

**Berwick to Eastbourne via Beachy Head**: A longer variant of [Berwick to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/). Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, ridge grassland, South Downs Way, clifftop, and highest chalk sea cliff in Britain. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Beachy Head, and Arlington Reservoir. Steep cliffs. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington, or the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington. 24 km.

**Berwick Circular via Windover Hill**: A shorter variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, village lanes, and fields. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, and Berwick church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Long Man Inn in Wilmington, the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cricketer's Arms in Berwick, or the Berwick Arms in Berwick. 13 km.

**Berwick to Seaford via Berwick church**: An alternative variant of [Berwick to Seaford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/). Fields, downland, village lanes, river valley, coastal cliffs, and shingle beach. Sights: Berwick church, High and Over Hill, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Cricketer's Arms in Berwick, the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Trawlers in Seaford, the Plough Inn in Seaford, or the Steamworks in Seaford. 18 km.
0.1657244,50.8404862.publicRamblerNote:
**[Berwick to Eastbourne via Filching Manor Motor Museum](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/)**: Rambler favourite! Farmland, reservoir, chalk downland, ridge grassland, chalk figure, smugglers villages, and seaside resort. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Arlington Reservoir, and Filching Manor Motor Museum. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington, or the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington. 20 km. 6.5 hours.

**[Berwick Circular via Upper Dicker Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-via-upper-dicker-circular/)**: Gently undulating farmland, open fields, bluebell woods, reservoir shorepath, and quiet country lanes with fine downs and weald views. Sights: [Michelham Priory](https://sussexpast.co.uk/attraction/michelham-priory/), Abbot's Wood, Arlington Reservoir, Long Man of Wilmington, and Holy Trinity Church. Can be slippery. Lunch at the [Old Oak Inn](https://www.oldoakinnarlington.co.uk/) in Arlington, the [Blue Door Cafe](https://bluedoorcafe.co.uk/) in Michelham Priory, or the [Plough](https://www.plough-upperdicker.co.uk/) in Upper Dicker. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Berwick to Seaford via Long Man of Wilmington](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-seaford/)**: South Downs chalk hills, open downland, river valley, coastal cliffs, shingle beach, and picturesque village. Sights: Long Man of Wilmington, Windover Hill, High and Over Hill, Berwick church, Cuckmere Haven, Seven Sisters, Seaford Head, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Long Man Inn in Wilmington, the George Inn in Alfriston, the Cuckmere Inn in Exceat, the Trawlers in Seaford, the Plough Inn in Seaford, or the Steamworks in Seaford. 20 km.

**Berwick to Polegate**: A shorter variant of [Berwick to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/). Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, Folkington village, and suburban roads at finish. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php). Suburban roads at end. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington. 11 km.

**Berwick to Eastbourne via Beachy Head**: A longer variant of [Berwick to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/berwick-to-eastbourne/). Rambler favourite! Farmland, chalk downland, ridge grassland, South Downs Way, clifftop, and highest chalk sea cliff in Britain. Sights: [Long Man of Wilmington](http://www.villagenet.co.uk/sevensisters/villages/wilmington.php), Beachy Head, and Arlington Reservoir. Steep cliffs. Lunch at the [Long Man Inn](https://longmaninn.co.uk/) in Wilmington, or the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington. 24 km.

0.1731809,51.1972733.ramblerNote:
**[Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/penshurst-circular/)**: Low-lying farmland, woodland, reservoir causeway, field edges, river meadows, and attractive Kent villages. Sights: [Bore Place](https://www.boreplace.org/visit/walks-at-bore-place), Bough Beech Reservoir, [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Chiding Stone, St Luke, Chiddingstone Causeway, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Larkin's Brewery, Penshurst Platform Gallery, Whistler's Farmhouse, and Penshurst Airfield. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Wheatsheaf](https://www.wheatsheafboughbeech.co.uk) in Bough Beech, or the [Castle Inn](https://thecastleinnchiddingstone.co.uk) in Chiddingstone. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Penshurst Circular via Penshurst Place**: A shorter variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying Eden valley farmland and village streets. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst. 9 km.

**Penshurst Circular via Chiddingstone Village**: An alternative variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying farmland, Eden valley, woods, vineyard, and picturesque village streets. Sights: [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 12 km. 3 hours.

**Penshurst to Leigh**: An alternative variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Low-lying Eden valley farmland, gentle ridges, woods, vineyard, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

**Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech & Hever Castle Golf Course**: A longer variant of [Penshurst Circular via Bough Beech](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/penshurst-circular/). Low-lying farmland, woodland, golf course, reservoir paths, river meadows, and Chiddingstone Nature Reserve. Sights: [Bore Place](https://www.boreplace.org/visit/walks-at-bore-place), Bough Beech Reservoir, Whistler's Farmhouse, Hever Castle Golf Course, St Paul, Four Elms, St Luke, Chiddingstone Causeway, Chiddingstone Nature Reserve, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Four Elms Inn in Four Elms, or the [Wheatsheaf](https://www.wheatsheafboughbeech.co.uk) in Bough Beech. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

0.1819438,51.2764117.name: Sevenoaks
0.1819438,51.2764117.publicNote: **[Borough Green to Sevenoaks, via Ightham Mote](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**: Nonstop beautiful landscape with [Ightham Mote](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/ightham-mote) — a small but charming 14th Century National Trust manor — in the middle, where you can have lunch and easily fit in a tour of the house. [Knole House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/knole) at the Sevenoaks end of the walk is well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself. No reason not to do it in the reverse direction. Adapted from *The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East*, 3rd edition.
0.1819438,51.2764117.ramblerNote: **[Sevenoaks Circular via Knole House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sevenoaks-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Deer park, fields, woods, coppiced woodland, escarpment ridge, bluebell and wild garlic woods, and Weald views. Sights: Knole House, Knole Park, Ightham Mote, and One Tree Hill. Some mud inevitable. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Brewhouse Cafe in Knole House, the Bucks Head in Godden Green, or the Ightham Mote restaurant in Ightham Mote. 16 km.
0.1819438,51.2764117.publicRamblerNote: **[Sevenoaks Circular via Knole House](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sevenoaks-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! **[Shoreham Circular via World Garden](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/)**: Steep-sided chalk hills, ancient woodland, chalk grassland, open countryside, valley floor, riverside paths, golf course, and lavender fields. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph, Lullingstone, World Garden, [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Pond, Shoreham Cross, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Broughton Manor, Pickmoss, Water House, and Holmesdale. Steep descents. Lunch at the Crown in Shoreham, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk) in Shoreham, the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk) in Shoreham, or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com) in Shoreham. 20 km. 5 hours.

**Shoreham Circular via Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/). Riverside paths, chalk woodland, chalk grassland, ancient trees, lavender fields, and valley floor. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph, Lullingstone, [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), and [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm). Lunch at the Crown in Shoreham, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk) in Shoreham, the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk) in Shoreham, or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com) in Shoreham. 10 km. 2.5 hours.

**Shoreham Circular via White Hill Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [Shoreham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/). Chalk hillside nature reserves, wildflower meadows, wooded hills, village streets, and riverside paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Pond, Shoreham Cross, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), White Hill Nature Reserve, and Fackenden Down. Steep descents. Lunch at the Crown in Shoreham, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk) in Shoreham, the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk) in Shoreham, or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com) in Shoreham. 9 km. 2.5 hours.

**Shoreham Circular via Samuel Palmer Trail**: An alternative variant of [Shoreham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/shoreham-circular/). Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, valley floor, riverside paths, hillside, lavender fields, and Samuel Palmer Trail landscapes. Sights: Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, [The Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Samuel Palmer Trail](https://darent-valley.org.uk/explore-the-darent-valley/samuel-palmer-trail), [Castle Farm](https://www.castlefarmkent.co.uk), [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, Shoreham Cross, and [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm). Steep descents. Lunch at the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk) in Shoreham, the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk) in Shoreham, the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com) in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 15 km. 4 hours.

0.1906219,51.5167202.name: Rainham
0.1906219,51.5167202.ramblerNote:
**[Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/)**: Completely flat, tarmac and hard surfaces, riverside sea wall, reed beds, marshes, and industrial-edge landscape with Thames views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Purfleet Heritage Museum, Purfleet Heritage Park, Coldharbour Point beacon, [London LOOP Section 24](https://tfl.gov.uk/modes/walking/loop-walk), and [NCN Route 13](https://www.sustrans.org.uk/find-a-route-on-the-national-cycle-network/route-13/). Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch at the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/) in Purfleet. 12 km. 3 hours.

**[Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/)**: Flat riverside path, marshland, industrial scenery, flood defences, graffiti art walls, former wharves, and Thames views throughout. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, Dartford Bridge, [St Clement's Church](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Clement%27s_Church,_West_Thurrock), and Concrete barges. Flood risk eastern half. Lunch at the RSPB Café in Purfleet, the Royal Hotel in Purfleet, or the Wharf in Grays. 16 km.

**Rainham to Purfleet via Rainham Hall**: A shorter variant of [Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/). Flat riverside path, marshland, RSPB nature reserve, and Thames views. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, and Concrete barges. Lunch at the RSPB Café in Purfleet, or the Royal Hotel in Purfleet. 8 km.

**Rainham Circular**: An alternative variant of [Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/). Completely flat, tarmac cycleway, sea wall, marshes, landfill edge, and riverside views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Wennington Marsh, and Rainham Silt Lagoons. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch at the [Phoenix](https://www.craftunionpubs.com/phoenix-rainham) in Rainham.

**Rainham to Purfleet via Rainham Hall**: A shorter variant of [Rainham to Grays](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-grays/). Flat riverside path, marshland, RSPB nature reserve, and Thames views. Sights: [Rainham Hall](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/rainham-hall/), [St Helen's and St Giles' Church](http://www.essexchurches.info/church.asp?p=Rainham), [Rainham Marshes RSPB Nature Reserve](http://www.rspb.org.uk/reserves/guide/r/rainhammarshes/), Purfleet Heritage Museum, Royal Hotel, and Concrete barges. Lunch at the RSPB Café in Purfleet, or the Royal Hotel in Purfleet. 8 km.

**Rainham Circular**: An alternative variant of [Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/). Completely flat, tarmac cycleway, sea wall, marshes, landfill edge, and riverside views. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, The Diver, Wennington Marsh, and Rainham Silt Lagoons. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch at the [Phoenix](https://www.craftunionpubs.com/phoenix-rainham) in Rainham.

Sights: All Saints Church, Great Chesterford, St Mary the Virgin, Little Chesterford, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, Market Place, [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, and Monk's Barn. 0.1971759,51.3129318.name: Otford
0.1971759,51.3129318.ramblerNote:
**[Otford Circular via Romney Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! North Downs hills, chalk woodland, bluebell woods, open commons, grassy fields, disused golf course, Darent Valley riverbank, and quaint village. Sights: [The Ancients](http://www.tate.org.uk/collections/glossary/definition.jsp?entryId=332), [Pre-Raphaelite](http://www.tate.org.uk/collections/glossary/definition.jsp?entryId=231), Church of St Peter and St Paul, Otford Palace, and Otford Solar System. Can be muddy. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Rising Sun in Cotman's Ash, the King's Arms in Shoreham, the Crown in Shoreham, the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham, the Shoreham Aircraft Museum Tea Room in Shoreham, the Honey Pot in Shoreham, or the Mount Vineyard in Shoreham. 12 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Otford Circular via Shoreham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-circular-via-shoreham/)**: Steep-sided hills, secluded chalk valleys, mixed woodland, open countryside, chalk grassland nature reserves, and attractive villages. Sights: Otford Chalk Pit, North Downs Way, Magpie Bottom, Ss Peter and Paul, [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk), War Memorial, Water House, [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk), Shoreham Aircraft Museum, Polhill Bank, Shoreham Cross, [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), Broughton Manor, Pickmoss, Otford Pond, St Bartholomew, and Otford Palace. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com) in Shoreham, the [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk) in Shoreham, the [Kings Arms](https://www.thekingsarms-shoreham.foodndrink.uk) in Shoreham, or the Crown in Shoreham. 14 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Otford to Eynsford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-eynsford/)**: River valley, open fields, orchards, beech woods, golf course, ridge path, and historic villages. Sights: St Bartholomew's Church, Otford Palace, Otford Solar System, Church of St Peter and St Paul, Shoreham Memorial Cross, Lullingstone Park Visitor Centre, [Lullingstone Castle](http://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk/), [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa/), Eagle Heights Wildlife Foundation, [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle/), St Martin's Church, and Eynsford Viaduct. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [King's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/thekingsarmsshoreham/) in Shoreham, the [Crown](https://www.thecrownshoreham.co.uk/) in Shoreham, or the [Samuel Palmer](https://thesamuelpalmer.com/) in Shoreham. 14 km. 4 hours.

**[Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/)**: North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, open countryside, and urban streets. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch at the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/) in Wrotham, or the Village in Vigo Village. 21 km.

**Otford to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Otford Circular via Romney Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-circular/). Rambler favourite! North Downs hills, chalk woodland, bluebell woods, open commons, grassy fields, and Shoreham village. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul and Shoreham Memorial Cross. Can be muddy. Lunch at the King's Arms in Shoreham, the Crown in Shoreham, or the Samuel Palmer in Shoreham. 8 km.

**Otford to Halling**: A longer variant of [Otford to Snodland](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/otford-to-snodland/). North Downs ridge, southern escarpment, fields, woods, open views south, forest, country park, and open countryside. Sights: Trosley Country Park. Lunch at the [Rose and Crown](https://www.roseandcrownwrotham.co.uk/) in Wrotham, or the Village in Vigo Village. 23 km.

0.2009551,51.0892394.name: Eridge
0.2009551,51.0892394.ramblerNote:
**[Eridge Circular via Harrison's Rocks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/)**: Undulating High Weald, streams, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, conifer-to-heathland nature reserve, and woods. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), St Thomas the Apostle, St John the Evangelist, Groombridge station, Birchden Forge, and Wildwood Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com) in Groombridge, or the Junction Inn in Groombridge. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Eridge Circular via Spa Valley Railway**: A shorter variant of [Eridge Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-circular/). Undulating High Weald, streams, meadows, sandstone outcrops, heathland, and woods; omits Harrison's Rocks loop. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), St Thomas the Apostle, St John the Evangelist, and Groombridge station. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Crown Inn](https://www.thecrowngroombridge.com) in Groombridge, or the Junction Inn in Groombridge. 14 km.

**Eridge to East Grinstead**: A longer variant of [Eridge to Forest Row and East Grinstead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eridge-to-forest-row-and-east-grinstead/). Undulating High Weald countryside, clay paths, woods, fields, estate grounds, river valley, disused railway cycleway, and hilltop town streets. Sights: [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [High Weald Landscape Trail](https://highweald.org/things-to-do/high-weald-landscape-trail), Buckhurst Park, Penns in the Rocks, [St Michael and All Angels](https://www.withyhamchurch.org/history), Old Buckhurst, St Mary the Virgin, Lych Gate Cottage, [Pooh Corner](https://www.poohcorner.co.uk), [Tablehurst Farm](https://www.tablehurstfarm.org.uk), [Forest Way](https://www.eastsussex.gov.uk/leisureandtourism/countryside/walks/forestway), Brambletye House, Sackville College, St Swithun's Church, and [East Grinstead](http://www.westsussex.info/east-grinstead.shtml). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Dorset Arms](https://www.dorset-arms.co.uk) in Withyham, or the [Bear Inn](https://www.thebearhartfield.com) in Hartfield. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

0.2042149,51.3623288.ramblerNote:
**[Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/)**: Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, ancient woodland, golf course, chalk grassland nature reserves, riverside paths, and farmland. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/find-a-kent-country-park/lullingstone-country-park), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), and Otford Palace. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk) in Otford, or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros) in Otford. 21 km. 5.5 hours.

**[Eynsford Circular via Farningham](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-farningham/)**: Ancient woodlands, low hills, chalk grassland, riverside paths, open hillside, farm fields, and quiet villages in the Darent valley. Sights: [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), Eynsford Viaduct, Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph Lullingstone, St Martin of Tours, Franks Hall, Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, and Ss Peter and Paul Farningham. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford, the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford, the Five Bells in Eynsford, the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com) in Eynsford, the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk) in Farningham, the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham) in Farningham, or the [Fighting Cocks](https://fightingcockshortonkirby.co.uk) in Horton Kirby. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Eynsford to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/). Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, woodland, chalk grassland nature reserves, and riverside village paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), and [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk) in Otford, or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros) in Otford. 16 km. 4 hours.

Sights: [Lullingstone Roman Villa](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/lullingstone-roman-villa), [Lullingstone Castle](https://www.lullingstonecastle.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), Eynsford Viaduct, Tudor Gatehouse, St Botolph Lullingstone, St Martin of Tours, Franks Hall, Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, and Ss Peter and Paul Farningham. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford, the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford, the Five Bells in Eynsford, the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com) in Eynsford, the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk) in Farningham, the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham) in Farningham, or the [Fighting Cocks](https://fightingcockshortonkirby.co.uk) in Horton Kirby. 17 km. 4 hours.

**Eynsford to Shoreham**: A shorter variant of [Eynsford Circular via Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eynsford-circular-via-otford/). Rambler favourite! Secluded valleys, chalk downland ridges, woodland, chalk grassland nature reserves, and riverside village paths. Sights: [Otford Solar System](https://www.otford.org/solarsystem), Otford Palace, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), and [Mount Vineyard](https://www.themountvineyard.co.uk). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bull](https://thebull-otford.co.uk) in Otford, or the [Olive Tree](https://hospicesofhope.co.uk/our-shops/#our-bistros) in Otford. 16 km. 4 hours.

**[Audley End Circular via The Cottage (pargeted house, Wendens Ambo)](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendens-ambo-circular/)**: Gently rolling chalky uplands, farmland, woods and copses, green lanes, field boundaries, and pretty villages with thatched cottages and pargeted houses. Sights: St. Mary the Virgin, Arkesden and The Cottage (pargeted house, Wendens Ambo). Lunch at the [Axe & Compasses](http://www.axeandcompasses.co.uk/) in Arkesden, or the [Red Cow](http://www.theredcow.com/) in Chrishall. 23 km. 5.5 hours.

**Audley End to Newport**: A shorter variant of [Great Chesterford to Newport](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/great-chesterford-to-newport/). Road sections, Audley End parkland, farmland, river valleys, historic market town, and woods. Sights: [Audley End House](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/audley-end-house-and-gardens), Audley End Miniature Railway, Bridge End Garden, [Fry Art Gallery](https://fryartgallery.org), [St Mary the Virgin, Saffron Walden](https://www.stmaryssaffronwalden.org/Groups/313211/History_and_Guides.aspx), Walden Castle, [Saffron Walden Museum](https://www.saffronwaldenmuseum.org), Turf Maze, Old Sun Inn, King Edward VI Almshouses, St Mary the Virgin, Newport, and Monk's Barn. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://www.theeightbellssaffronwalden.com) in Saffron Walden, the Kings Arms in Saffron Walden, the [Cross Keys Hotel](https://www.theoldcrosskeys.co.uk) in Saffron Walden, or the [Temeraire](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/the-temeraire-saffron-walden) in Saffron Walden. 15 km. 3.5 hours.

0.2112588,51.1939929.name: Leigh
0.2112588,51.1939929.privateNote: Trainline says 52m — London Bridge via Tonbridge
0.2112588,51.1939929.ramblerNote:
**[Leigh to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/)**: Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, gentle farmland slopes, vineyard, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 13 km. 3 hours.

**[Leigh to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rolling Kentish farmland, fields, oasthouses, bluebell woods, steep greensand hillside, and deer park. Sights: St Mary's Church, Hall Place, Knole Park, Knole House, and Brewhouse Tearoom. Can be muddy. Lunch at the White Rock in Underriver. 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Leigh to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-to-tunbridge-wells/)**: Rolling Kent hills, pastoral river valleys, parkland, woods, lakes, and commons extending into town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](http://www.penshurstplace.com/), St John the Baptist Church, Leicester Square (Penshurst), River Medway, All Souls Chapel, Poundsbridge, Pantiles, King Charles the Martyr Church, and [The Opera House](http://www.jdwetherspoon.co.uk/pub/theoperahouse). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Spotted Dog](http://www.spotteddogpub.co.uk/) in Smart's Hill, the [Bottle House Inn](http://www.thebottlehouseinnpenshurst.co.uk/) in Smart's Hill, or the [Leicester Arms](http://www.leicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk/) in Penshurst. 18 km. 5.5 hours.

**Leigh Circular via Penshurst Place & Chiddingstone Village**: A variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, farmland, woods, and hamlet lanes. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), and St John the Baptist. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 16 km.

**Leigh Circular via Chiddingstone Castle**: A longer variant of [Leigh and Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/leigh-and-penshurst/). Rambler favourite! Parkland, river valley, farmland, woods, vineyard, and picturesque village streets. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Village](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/chiddingstone-village), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), St John the Baptist, and Harden Vineyard. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Leigh to Tonbridge**: A shorter variant of [Hever to Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hever-to-tonbridge/). Parkland estate, river valley, country park lakes, and riverside paths into a market town. Sights: [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Tonbridge Castle](https://www.tonbridgecastle.org), St John the Baptist Church Penshurst, Leicester Square Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Straight Mile, and Powder Mills. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 12 km. 3 hours.

0.2181092,51.5538747.name: Hornchurch
0.2181092,51.5538747.ramblerNote: **Hornchurch to Rainham**: A longer variant of [Rainham to Purfleet via RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rainham-to-purfleet-via-rspb-rainham-marshes/). Flat, tarmac, marshes, riverside, reed beds, and with an Underground-to-station start via the Ingrebourne Valley. Sights: [RSPB Rainham Marshes](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/rainham-marshes), Concrete Barges, and The Diver. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Feb. Lunch at the [Royal Hotel Purfleet](http://theroyalpurfleet.co.uk/) in Purfleet. 36 km.

0.2191164,51.4474203.name: Dartford
0.2191164,51.4474203.ramblerNote: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. Sights: Queen Elizabeth Bridge, Dartford Marshes, Littlebrook Power Station, and [Thames Path](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Thames_Path). Flood risk — check warnings. Lunch at the Asda café in Greenhithe. 11 km.
0.2191164,51.4474203.publicRamblerNote: **[Dartford to Greenhithe](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dartford-to-greenhithe/)**: Flat riverside paths, flood defences, marshland, industrial edge-land, and wide Thames estuary views. 0.2278823,51.2145812.name: Hildenborough
0.2278823,51.2145812.ramblerNote:
**Hildenborough to Penshurst**: An alternative variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Roadside walking, fields, parkland, riverside paths, low hills, vineyards, and farmland. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Harden Vineyard, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 18 km. 4.5 hours.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge via Hall Place**: An alternative variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Roadside walking, fields, parkland, riverside paths, parkland, playing fields, and town centre. Sights: Hall Place, St Mary, Leigh, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), St John the Baptist, Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Penshurst Park, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge School. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Fleur De Lis](https://fleurdelisleigh.co.uk) in Leigh. 18 km. 4 hours.

**Hildenborough to Tonbridge via Hadlow Tower**: A longer variant of [Hildenborough to Hadlow or Tonbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hildenborough-to-hadlow-or-tonbridge/). Wealden countryside, meadows, farm fields, woods, and long riverside path along the River Medway into Tonbridge. Sights: [Hadlow Tower](http://www.thehadlowtower.co.uk/), [Broadview Gardens](https://www.hadlow.ac.uk/about-us/broadview-gardens-and-garden-centre), St Giles, Shipbourne, Hadlow College, St Mary, Hadlow, Hadlow Castle, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Chaser Inn](https://www.thechaser.co.uk/) in Shipbourne, or the [Kentish Rifleman](https://www.thekentishrifleman.co.uk/) in Dunk's Green. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

0.235341,51.4016801.name: Farningham Road
0.235341,51.4016801.ramblerNote:
**[Farningham Road to Sole Street](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-sole-street/)**: Urban sprawl, housing developments, busy roads, arable fields, and occasional glimpses of historic rural Kent. Sights: Wheatsheaf pub and Chapter House Farm. Lunch at the [Ship Inn](https://www.shipatsouthfleet.co.uk/) in Southfleet, the [Manor Farm Barn](https://www.manorfarmbarnsouthfleet.co.uk/) in Southfleet, or the [Railway Inn](http://www.railwayinnsolestreet.co.uk/) in Sole Street. 17 km.

**[Farningham Road to Otford via South Darenth Viaduct](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/)**: Ancient woodland, field edges, riverside Darent Valley Path, low hills, chalk downland, quaint villages, golf course, and nature reserves. Sights: [Farningham Woods Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/farningham-woods), [Darent Valley Path](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpdarentvalleypath.html), [Eynsford Castle](https://www.english-heritage.org.uk/visit/places/eynsford-castle), [Eagle Heights](https://www.eagleheights.co.uk), [Lullingstone Country Park](https://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/lullingstone-country-park), South Darenth Viaduct, Franks Hall, Farningham Cattle Screen, Farningham Mill, Ss Peter and Paul, Farningham, St Martin of Tours, Lullingstone Castle, [Shoreham Aircraft Museum](https://www.shoreham-aircraft-museum.co.uk/teas.htm), Ss Peter and Paul, Shoreham, The Mount Vineyard, White Hill Nature Reserve, Fackenden Down, Otford Solar System, Otford Pond, St Bartholomew, Otford, Otford Palace, Kings Arms, and Water House. Motocross path closures possible. Lunch at the [Lion Hotel](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/east/thelionfarningham) in Farningham, the [Pied Bull](https://piedbullfarningham.co.uk) in Farningham, the [Castle Hotel](https://www.castlehotelkent.com) in Eynsford, the [Malt Shovel](https://themaltshoveleynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford, or the [Plough Inn](https://www.theploughinneynsford.co.uk) in Eynsford. 20 km. 5 hours.

**Farningham Road Circular via Nine Hole Wood**: An alternative variant of [Farningham Road to Otford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/farningham-road-to-otford/). Ancient woodland, riverside Darent Valley Path, Farningham village, Sparepenny Lane hillside, and Horton Kirby. Sights: Haysden Country Park, Brookhurst Field, St Lawrence Church, Wealdway, and Eden Valley Walk. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Kentish Hare](http://www.thekentishhare.com/) in Bidborough, or the [Plough at Leigh](http://www.theploughatleigh.com/) in Hildenborough. 17 km.

Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), [Tunbridge Wells](https://visittunbridgewells.com), The Pantiles, Calverley Grounds, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, Eridge Old Park, Eridge Park, Birchden Vineyards, and St Thomas the Apostle. 0.2664989,52.3906885.ramblerNote: **[Ely Circular via Ely Cathedral](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ely-circular/)**: Open fenland, flat drained farmland, long straight tree-lined farm tracks, hedge-lined meadows, river banks, dykes and catchwater drains, quayside, and historic city. Sights: [Ely Cathedral](http://www.elycathedral.org/) and [Cromwell's House](http://www.olivercromwellshouse.co.uk/). Exposed river banks. Lunch at the [Red Lion](https://www.theredlionstretham.co.uk/) in Stretham. 18 km. 4 hours.

**[Tonbridge to Penshurst via Harden Vineyard](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/)**: Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, low hills, orchards, vineyards, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 18 km. 4 hours.

**[Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/)**: Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, country park, riverside paths, residential streets, and fishing and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, Heusenstamm Wood, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Lunch at the [Poacher & Partridge](https://elitepubs.com/venue/the-poacher-and-partridge) in Tudeley Hale. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 15 km.

**Tonbridge Circular via Eden Valley Walk**: A variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, parkland, playing fields, and town centre. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St John the Baptist, Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Penshurst Park, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge School. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Fleur De Lis](https://fleurdelisleigh.co.uk) in Leigh. 18 km. 4 hours.

**Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley & St Thomas à Becket**: A longer variant of [Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/). Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, low wooded hills, country lanes, country park, riverside paths, and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), St Thomas à Becket, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, and Heusenstamm Wood. Tricky navigation, limited waymarking. Lunch at the [George & Dragon](https://www.gd-tudeley.com) in Tudeley, or the [Dovecote Inn](https://www.dovecote-capel.co.uk) in Capel. 25 km. 6 hours.
