Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Harden Vineyard, Larkin's Brewery, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Rock Inn](https://www.therockchiddingstone.com) in Hoath Corner. 18 km. 4 hours.

**[Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/)**: Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, country park, riverside paths, residential streets, and fishing and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, Heusenstamm Wood, and Tonbridge Memorial Garden. Lunch at the [Poacher & Partridge](https://elitepubs.com/venue/the-poacher-and-partridge) in Tudeley Hale. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

**Tonbridge to Penshurst via Haysden Country Park**: A shorter variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, Eden Valley Walk, farmland, and picturesque villages. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), [Chiddingstone Castle](https://www.chiddingstonecastle.org.uk), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St Mary, Chiddingstone, Chiding Stone, and Penshurst Platform Gallery. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, or the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst. 15 km.

**Tonbridge Circular via Eden Valley Walk**: A variant of [Tonbridge to Penshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-to-penshurst/). Lakes, water meadows, riverside paths, parkland, playing fields, and town centre. Sights: [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), [Eden Valley Walk](https://www.walkingpages.co.uk/trails_paths/ldpedenvalleywalk.html), [Penshurst Place](https://www.penshurstplace.com), Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, St John the Baptist, Penshurst, Sidney Oak, Penshurst Park, Tonbridge Castle, and Tonbridge School. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Leicester Arms Hotel](https://www.theleicesterarmspenshurst.co.uk) in Penshurst, the [Porcupine Pantry](https://www.penshurstplace.com/explore/eatshop/porcupine-pantry) in Penshurst, or the [Fleur De Lis](https://fleurdelisleigh.co.uk) in Leigh. 18 km. 4 hours.

**Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley & St Thomas à Becket**: A longer variant of [Tonbridge Circular via Tudeley](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tonbridge-circular-via-tudeley/). Parkland, farm fields, paddocks, orchards, low wooded hills, country lanes, country park, riverside paths, and sailing lakes. Sights: [All Saints Church, Tudeley](https://www.tudeley.org/Groups/363846/Chagall_Windows.aspx), St Thomas à Becket, [Haysden Country Park](https://www.tmbc.gov.uk/leisure-parks/haysden-country-park), Tonbridge Castle, Somerhill House, Hadlow Tower, Leigh Flood Relief Barrier, Stone Lock, and Heusenstamm Wood. Tricky navigation, limited waymarking. Lunch at the [George & Dragon](https://www.gd-tudeley.com) in Tudeley, or the [Dovecote Inn](https://www.dovecote-capel.co.uk) in Capel. 25 km. 6 hours.

0.2796796,50.7959254.name: Hampden Park
0.2796796,50.7959254.ramblerNote: **[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, and Little Chelsea. Steep descents. Lunch at the [Eight Bells](https://eightbellsjevington.com/) in Jevington, the [Tiger Inn](https://www.beachyhead.org.uk/the-tiger-inn/) in East Dean, the Hikers' Rest Coffee Shop in East Dean, the Thai Terre in East Dean, or the Beehive on the Green in East Dean. 26 km.
0.2796796,50.7959254.publicRamblerNote: **[Hampden Park to Eastbourne](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hampden-park-to-eastbourne/)**: Parks, woods, chalk downland ridges, open grassland, chalk heath, dense beech forest, coastal farmland, golf course, and Victorian seaside town. Sights: Hampden Park, Lullington Heath National Nature Reserve, Friston Forest, St Andrew's Church, Jevington, Friston Place, Belle Tout Lighthouse, Birling Gap, Eastbourne cricket ground, Eastbourne Town Hall, Gildredge Park, and Little Chelsea. 0.2815355,50.7696435.name: Eastbourne
0.2815355,50.7696435.publicNote: For strong walkers I highly recommend walking all the way from Eastbourne to Seaford Station. This starts out following the charming Eastbourne seafront and then into the absolutely spectacular Seven Sisters cliff walk. You can have lunch at the National Trust village of Birling Gap, before completing the trek through the marshes of Cuckmere Haven. It's long but impossible to mess up since you're just following the sea, and everything is signposted. The cliffs are best appreciated when sparkling on a bright summer's day. Alas, everyone else knows this so the Seven Sisters part is extremely busy on Summer weekends — although it does create a jolly festival atmosphere if you can tolerate that. To me this is the greatest walk in England.
0.2815355,50.7696435.ramblerNote:
**[Eastbourne to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-hastings-via-bexhill/)**: Flat coastal seafront promenades, shingle beach, sandy beach at low tide, sea wall paths, quiet coast roads, and low cliffs. Sights: [Eastbourne Pier](http://www.eastbournepier.com/), [Eastbourne Harbour](http://www.eastbourneharbour.com/walks.php), [Martello Towers](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Martello_tower), [Pevensey Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/daysout/properties/pevensey-castle/), [De La Warr Pavilion](http://www.dlwp.com/), and Hastings Pier. Shingle heavy going. Lunch at the Aqua Bar in Pevensey Bay, the Moorings in Pevensey Bay, the [Castle Inn](http://castleinnpevenseybay.co.uk/) in Pevensey Bay, the [Star Inn](https://www.thestarinnnormansbay.co.uk/) in Norman's Bay, the [Relais Cooden Beach](https://www.therelaisretreats.com/coodenbeach/) in Cooden Beach, the [De La Warr Pavilion Cafe, Bar & Kitchen](http://www.dlwp.com/) in Bexhill, the [Farmyard](https://farmyardwine.com/) in St Leonard's, or the [Royal](http://theroyalstleonards.co.uk/) in St Leonard's. 26 km.

**Eastbourne Circular**: An alternative variant of [Eastbourne to East Dean](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/eastbourne-to-east-dean/). Seafront promenade, chalk cliffs, open downs, quiet downland valley, ridge descent, and town streets. Sights: Beachy Head, Belle Tout, Birling Gap, and Seven Sisters. Mile on Eastbourne streets. Lunch at the Beachy Head pub in Beachy Head, the Birling Gap National Trust Tea Room in Birling Gap, the Tiger Inn in East Dean, or the Hiker's Rest in East Dean. 17 km.

0.2943374,51.1040897.ramblerNote:
**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Eridge Rocks**: A shorter variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), The Pantiles, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun) in Eridge Green. 17 km.

**Frant to Tunbridge Wells via Harrison's Rocks**: An alternative variant of [Tunbridge Wells Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/tunbridge-wells-circular/). High Weald hills, landscaped parkland, bluebell woods, restored heathland, sandstone outcrops, river valley, wooded common, and colonnaded town walkway. Sights: [Eridge Rocks](https://sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/visit/eridge-rocks), [Broadwater Warren](https://www.rspb.org.uk/days-out/reserves/broadwater-warren), [Harrison's Rocks](https://www.thebmc.co.uk/modules/rad/view.aspx?id=119), [Spa Valley Railway](https://www.spavalleyrailway.co.uk), [Groombridge Place](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Groombridge_Place), The Pantiles, St Alban Frant, High Rocks, and Eridge Old Park. Busy road at start. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Nevill Crest and Gun](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/nevillcrestandgun) in Eridge Green. 21 km. 5.5 hours.

0.3130406,51.0733883.name: Wadhurst
0.3130406,51.0733883.ramblerNote:
**[Wadhurst Circular via Bewl Water Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-via-bewl-water-circular/)**: Rambler favourite! Hidden valleys, ancient woods, quaint old farms, reservoir shoreline, hilltop village, and gentle but rolling Wealden landscape. Sights: Bewl Water Reservoir. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Old Vine in Cousley Wood. 17 km.

**[Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/)**: High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, landscaped parkland, and back streets and alleyways of a spa town. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), [Tunbridge Wells](https://www.visittunbridgewells.com/explore), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Juliets](https://www.julietsandmore.com), and [Opera House](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/opera-house-royal-tunbridge-wells). Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the George Inn in Frant, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk) in Frant. 13 km. 3.5 hours.

**[Wadhurst Circular via Bewl Water](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-circular/)**: High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, hedgerows, quiet farmland, and historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul, [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk), and Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/) in Mayfield, the [Middle House](http://www.themiddlehousemayfield.co.uk/) in Mayfield, or the [Greyhound](http://www.thegreyhoundwadhurst.co.uk/) in Wadhurst. 19 km. 6 hours.

**Wadhurst Circular via Church of St Peter and St Paul**: A shorter variant of [Wadhurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-circular/). High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, and quiet farmland. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [White Hart](http://www.whitehartwadhurst.co.uk/) in Wadhurst. 10 km. 3 hours.

**Wadhurst to Frant**: A shorter variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, open fields, descent through woodland, and brief roadside stretch. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, and [Brecknock Arms](https://www.thebrecknockarmspub.com). No pavement on final road. Lunch at the George Inn in Frant, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk) in Frant. 11 km. 3 hours.

**Wadhurst to Stonegate**: An alternative variant of [Wadhurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-circular/). High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, quiet farmland, and historic villages. Sights: Church of St Peter and St Paul and Church of Saint Dunstans. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Rose & Crown Inn](http://www.roseandcrownmayfield.co.uk/) in Mayfield. 16 km.

**Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells via Dunorlan Park**: A longer variant of [Wadhurst to Tunbridge Wells](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-to-tunbridge-wells/). High Weald ridges, wooded valleys, parkland, recreation grounds, suburban roads, and Victorian park gardens. Sights: [Sussex Border Path](https://www.sussexborderpath.co.uk), Eridge Old Park, Saxonbury Hill Tower, The Mount, St Alban, Frant, Nevill Crest, High Weald Landscape Trail, Tunbridge Wells Circular Walk, [Dunorlan Park](https://tunbridgewells.gov.uk/community-and-leisure/parks-and-play-areas/dunorlan-park), [Calverley Grounds](https://tunbridgewells.gov.uk/community-and-leisure/parks-and-play-areas/calverley-grounds), and Calverley Park. Can be boggy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the George Inn in Frant, or the [Abergavenny Arms](https://www.abergavennyarms.co.uk) in Frant. 16 km. 4 hours.

**Wadhurst to Robertsbridge**: A longer variant of [Wadhurst Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wadhurst-circular/). High Weald AONB, rolling hills, woods, Bewl Water lakeside, Sussex Border Path, and quiet farmland. Sights: [Bewl Water](http://www.bewl.co.uk) and Church of St Peter and St Paul. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Old Vine](http://www.theoldvineinn.co.uk/) in Cousley Wood, or the [Look Out restaurant](http://www.bewl.co.uk) in Bewl Water. 34 km. 7 hours.

0.3566218,51.3864534.ramblerNote:
**[Meopham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/meopham-to-rochester/)**: Chalk grassland, ancient woodland, community woodland, landscaped parkland, wildflower meadows, farm fields, country park, and busy road stretches. Sights: [Jeskyns Community Woodland](https://www.forestryengland.uk/jeskyns), [Ashenbank Wood](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/ashenbank-wood), [Cobham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cobham,_Kent), [Cobham Park (Cobham Wood and Mausoleum)](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/cobham-wood-and-mausoleum), Darnley Mausoleum, [Ranscombe Farm Nature Reserve](https://www.plantlife.org.uk/our-work/ranscombe-farm-nature-reserve), [Camer Park](https://explorekent.org/activities/camer-park), St Mary Magdalene Church, New College of Cobham, Nurstead Court, Bronze Age Barrow, Cobham Hall, and Luddesdown Vineyard. Can be muddy. **[Stonegate to Robertsbridge via Batemans](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-to-robertsbridge/)**: Hilly High Weald, valleys, ridgetop village, hornbeam woods, country estate, and panoramic ridge views. Sights: Batemans. Can be muddy. Best in Oct, Nov and Apr. Lunch at the Rose & Crown in Burwash, the Bear Inn in Burwash, the Blacksmiths Tea and Coffee Shop in Burwash, the Ostrich in Robertsbridge, or the George Inn in Robertsbridge. 14 km.

**[Stonegate Circular via Bateman's](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-circular/)**: Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, classic English woodland, pastureland, and unspoiled village. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch at the Rose and Crown in Burwash, the [Bear Inn](http://www.bear-inn-hotel-burwash.co.uk/) in Burwash, or the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/) in Burwash Weald. 15 km. 5 hours.

**Stonegate to Wadhurst**: An alternative variant of [Stonegate Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/stonegate-circular/). Rolling hills, tranquil valleys, woodland, pastureland, and remote wilderness sections. Sights: [Bateman's](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk) and Church of St. Bartholomew. Nettles and brambles. Lunch at the [Wheel Inn](http://www.thewheelburwashweald.co.uk/) in Burwash Weald. 32 km.

**[Ingatestone to Battlesbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingatestone-to-battlesbridge/)**: Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, open fields with far views, and Crouch Valley finish. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bakers Arms](http://www.bakersarms.co.uk/) in Stock, the [Hoop](http://www.thehoop.co.uk/) in Stock, the [Dandelion & Burdock Kitchen & Lounge](https://www.dandelion-burdock.co.uk/) in Stock, the [Bear Inn](http://www.thebearstock.com/) in Stock, the [Harvard Inn](https://theharvardinn.co.uk/) in Stock, or the [Old Windmill](https://www.brunningandprice.co.uk/oldwindmill/) in South Hanningfield. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

Wid Valley fields, riverside paths, cattle farm estate, bluebell woods, tree-lined tracks, and Crowsheath Community Woodland. Sights: Ingatestone Hall, St. Mary's Church Buttsbury, Stock Tower Mill, and Hanningfield Reservoir Nature Discovery Centre. Can be muddy. Sights: [River Medway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/River_Medway), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), Twyford Bridge, Yalding Lees, Ss Peter and Paul, Yalding, Yalding Town Bridge, Warde's Moat, St Mary, Hunton, Greensand Way, Medway Valley Walk, Cheveney Mill, and Yalding Fen. Lunch at the [Boathouse](https://www.boathouseyalding.co.uk) in Twyford Bridge. 18 km. 4 hours.

**Paddock Wood to Yalding via River Medway**: A shorter variant of [Paddock Wood to Yalding](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/paddock-wood-to-yalding/). 0.4121616,51.2266654.ramblerNote:
**[Yalding to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rambler favourite! Undulating Kent farmland, orchards, bluebell woods, greensand escarpment, and deer park. Sights: Ightham Mote, Knole House, and One Tree Hill. Waymarking can disappear. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Swan in West Peckham, the Kentish Rifleman in Dunks Green, or the Chaser Inn in Shipbourne. 21 km.

**[Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/)**: Flat riverside paths, farmland fields, orchards, quiet lanes, and a floodplain. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), [Almshouses](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page272.html), and [Twyford Bridge](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page17.html). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/) in West Farleigh. 12 km.

**[Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Church of St Lawrence](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-borough-green/)**: River Medway start, open fields, deer-fenced woodland, Kentish Weald countryside, vast managed woods, bluebell woodland valleys, orchard edges, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, Church of St Lawrence, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Mereworth Wines in Mereworth. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Roydon Hall**: An alternative variant of [Yalding to Borough Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-borough-green/). Open fields, country lanes, managed Mereworth Woods, bluebell woodland valleys, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Moody Mare Country Pub in Mereworth. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Beltring**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, woods, and a pretty stretch of river Medway. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Busy A-road crossing. Lunch at the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/) in West Farleigh, or the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/) in East Peckham. 20 km.

**Yalding to Harrietsham**: A longer variant of [Yalding to Sutton Valence](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sutton-vallance/). Flat Medway Valley start, gentle Greensand Ridge climb, arable fields, apple orchards, scenic ridge walking, coppiced woodland with wood anemone, and then descent to Harrietsham. Sights: Boughton Monchelsea church, Buttercup Goat Sanctuary, and Pepper Box pub. Best in Mar–Apr. Lunch at the Bull in Linton. 24 km.

**Yalding to Paddock Wood**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, meadows, woods, and quiet lanes. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Lunch at the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/) in West Farleigh, or the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/) in East Peckham.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via St Dunstan's Church**: An alternative variant of [Yalding to Borough Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-borough-green/). Sunken bridleways, open fields, Greensand Way, Gower Hill woodland, Mereworth Woods, bluebell valley, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and St Dunstan's Church. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Swan on the Green in West Peckham.
0.4121616,51.2266654.publicRamblerNote:
**[Yalding to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-sevenoaks/)**: Rambler favourite! Undulating Kent farmland, orchards, bluebell woods, greensand escarpment, and deer park. Sights: Ightham Mote, Knole House, and One Tree Hill. Waymarking can disappear. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Swan in West Peckham, the Kentish Rifleman in Dunks Green, or the Chaser Inn in Shipbourne. 21 km.

**[Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/)**: Flat riverside paths, farmland fields, orchards, quiet lanes, and a floodplain. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/), [Yalding](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yalding), [Almshouses](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page272.html), and [Twyford Bridge](http://yaldinghistory.webplus.net/page17.html). Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/) in West Farleigh. 12 km.

**[Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Church of St Lawrence](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-borough-green/)**: River Medway start, open fields, deer-fenced woodland, Kentish Weald countryside, vast managed woods, bluebell woodland valleys, orchard edges, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, Church of St Lawrence, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Mereworth Wines in Mereworth. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Borough Green & Wrotham via Roydon Hall**: An alternative variant of [Yalding to Borough Green](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-to-borough-green/). Open fields, country lanes, managed Mereworth Woods, bluebell woodland valleys, and small villages. Sights: Roydon Hall, St Michaels Church, and Mereworth Wines. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Moody Mare Country Pub in Mereworth. 16 km. 5 hours.

**Yalding to Beltring**: A longer variant of [Yalding Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/yalding-circular/). Flat riverside paths, farmland, woods, and a pretty stretch of river Medway. Sights: [Teston Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Teston_Bridge), [Nettlestead Church](http://nettlesteadchurch.co.uk/), [Teston Bridge Country Park](http://www.kent.gov.uk/leisure-and-community/kent-country-parks/teston-bridge-country-park), and [Tea Pot Island](http://www.teapotisland.co.uk/). Busy A-road crossing. Lunch at the [Tickled Trout](http://www.thetickledtrout.com/) in West Farleigh, or the [Man of Kent](http://themanofkentpub.com/) in East Peckham. 20 km.

**[Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/)**: Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, open estuary waterscape, wide flat creek-side track, and gentrifying seaside town. Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Two Tree Island, and Benfleet Creek. Can be muddy. Best in May–Oct. Lunch at the Crooked Billet in Leigh-on-Sea, or the Osborne Bros in Leigh-on-Sea. 15 km.

**[Benfleet Circular via Canvey Island](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-canvey-island/)**: Flat seawall path, tidal mudflats, salt marshes, grassy marshes, sandy beaches, industrial waterfront, tidal creeks, and oil terminals. Sights: Hadleigh Castle, Labworth Beach Café, [Lobster Smack](https://www.lobstersmackcanveyisland.co.uk/), Concord Beach Paddling Pool, Dr. Feelgood seawall murals, and Canvey Point. Check tide timings. Lunch at the Labworth Beach Café in Canvey Island, the Street & Co Coffee Shop in Canvey Island, or the [Lobster Smack](https://www.lobstersmackcanveyisland.co.uk/) in Canvey Island. 23 km. 5 hours.

**Benfleet to Leigh-on-Sea via Leigh on Sea & Hadleigh Castle**: A shorter variant of [Benfleet Circular via Leigh on Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/benfleet-circular-via-leigh-on-sea/). Hilly woodland, narrow grassy paths, Hadleigh Country Park, and estuary views. Sights: Hadleigh Castle. Can be muddy. Best in May–Oct. Lunch at the Crooked Billet in Leigh-on-Sea, or the Osborne Bros in Leigh-on-Sea. 8 km.

0.5768255,50.8583028.name: Hastings
0.5768255,50.8583028.ramblerNote:
**[Hastings to Rye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-to-rye/)**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, remote naturist beach, flat marshland, ancient hilltop towns, and panoramic viewpoints. Sights: Hastings Castle, St Clements Caves, New Gate, St. Thomas' Church, The Look Out, and [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk) in Fairlight, the [Coastguard Café](https://www.coastguardcafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, or the [Eater's](https://www.facebook.com/eatersatpett/) in Pett Level. 19 km. 6 hours.

**[Hastings Circular via Fairlight Glen Beach](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-circular-via-fairlight-glen-beach/)**: Hilly clifftop path, steep glens, shingle and sandy beach, forested inland valleys, coastal headlands, bracken, and eroding cliffs. Sights: Hastings Castle, Jerwood Art Gallery, Fairlight Glen Beach, [St Andrew's Church](http://www.sussexparishchurches.org/content/view/170/34/), and [Hastings County Park](http://www.1066online.com/HASTINGS-MAIN/Places-to-Visit/visit-hastings/country-park.html). Crumbly cliff edges. Lunch at the [Coastguards Tea Rooms](http://www.coastguardstearooms.co.uk/) in Fairlight. 13 km.

**Hastings Circular via St Clements Caves**: A shorter variant of [Hastings to Rye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-to-rye/). Rambler favourite! Clifftop coastal path, steep climbs, Hastings Old Town, caves, and castle. Sights: Hastings Castle and St Clements Caves. Crumbly cliff edges. 9 km. 3 hours.

**Hastings to Winchelsea**: A shorter variant of [Hastings to Rye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hastings-to-rye/). Rambler favourite! Lunch at the [Cove](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk) in Fairlight. 16 km.

Sights: [Hucking Estate](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/hucking-estate), [Hollingbourne Meadows](http://www.hollingbournemeadowstrust.co.uk), [Leeds Castle](https://leeds-castle.com), The Shepherd, Hollingbourne Manor, and All Saints Hollingbourne. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Park Gate Inn](https://www.vintageinn.co.uk/restaurants/south-east/theparkgateinnhollingbourne#) in Hollingbourne. 15 km. 4 hours.

**Hollingbourne Circular via Hucking Estate**: A longer variant of [Hollingbourne Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/hollingbourne-circular/). Open farm fields, North Downs ridge, chalk grassland, grassy rides, woodland paths, open valley, castle grounds, and meadows. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Hook & Hatchet Inn](https://www.hookandhatchetpub.co.uk) in Hucking. 20 km. 5 hours.

Sights: Thurnham Castle, White Horse Wood Country Park, [Hucking Estate](https://www.woodlandtrust.org.uk/visiting-woods/woods/hucking-estate), St Margaret Hucking, The Shepherd, The Tranchet Axe, The Wild Boar Family, [Leeds Castle](https://leeds-castle.com), Hollingbourne Manor, and All Saints Hollingbourne. 0.7025193,50.9339128.name: Winchelsea
0.7025193,50.9339128.ramblerNote:
**[Winchelsea to Hastings via All Saints Church](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/)**: Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, and historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, and Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in Mar–Aug. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, the Royal Oak Inn in Pett, the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/) in Pett Level, or the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/) in Fairlight Cove. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/)**: Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, hilly territory with fine views, coastal cliff path with steep ascents and descents, and historic walled town. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, Icklesham Church, Doleham station, Fairlight Hall, Fairlight Glen, Ecclesbourne Glen, East Hill Lift, and Hastings Old Town. Steep ascents and descents. Lunch at the Three Oaks pub in Three Oaks, the Coastguard Cafe in Fairlight, or the Grumpy Cook Cafe in Fairlight. 26 km.

**Winchelsea Circular**: A variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/). Rambler favourite! Flat river levels, farmland, coastal path to Pett Level, and return via connecting walk. Sights: All Saints Church and Hog Hill windmill. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, or the Royal Oak Inn in Pett. 16 km.

**Winchelsea to Three Oaks**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, and hilly territory with fine views over the Brede valley. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, and Icklesham Church. Lunch at the Queen's Head in Icklesham, or the Three Oaks pub in Three Oaks. 12 km.

Flat river levels, farmland, coastal path to Pett Level, and return via connecting walk. Sights: All Saints Church and Hog Hill windmill. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, or the Royal Oak Inn in Pett. 16 km.

**Winchelsea to Three Oaks**: A shorter variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings via Three Oaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings-via-three-oaks/). Undulating ridge, pastureland, farms, woods, and hilly territory with fine views over the Brede valley. Sights: Winchelsea, New Gate, and Icklesham Church. Lunch at the Queen's Head in Icklesham, or the Three Oaks pub in Three Oaks. 12 km.

0.7310208,50.952632.name: Rye
0.7310208,50.952632.ramblerNote:
**[Rye Circular via St Mary the Virgin](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-figure-of-8/)**: Medieval hilltop town, reclaimed marshland, shingle beach, saline lagoons, freshwater gravel pits, reedbeds, riverside embankments, and coastal nature reserve. Sights: [St Mary the Virgin](https://www.ryeparishchurch.org.uk/history.htm), [Rye Castle Museum](http://ryemuseum.co.uk), [Lamb House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/lamb-house), [Rye Heritage Centre](https://www.ryeheritage.co.uk), [Rye Harbour Nature Reserve](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk), Camber Castle, Castle Water, Mary Stanford Lifeboat House, Ypres Tower, Landgate, Mermaid Inn, Rye Windmill, Saltcote Place, Church of the Holy Spirit, and Martello Tower. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [William the Conqueror](https://www.williamtheconqueror.co.uk) in Rye Harbour, the Bosun's Bite in Rye Harbour, or the [Lime Kiln Cafe](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/discovery-centre/cafe) in Rye Harbour. 16 km. 3.5 hours.

**Rye Circular via All Saints Church**: A variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/). Rambler favourite! Flat levels, farmland, coastal path to Pett Level, and return via connecting walk. Sights: All Saints Church and Hog Hill windmill. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, or the Royal Oak Inn in Pett. 21 km.

**Rye to Hastings**: A longer variant of [Winchelsea to Hastings](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/winchelsea-to-hastings/). Rambler favourite! Flat levels, farmland, hilly coastal cliffs, wooded glens, naturist beach, and historic old town. Sights: All Saints Church, Hog Hill windmill, [Hastings Country Park](https://www.hastingscountryparkhistory.com/index.php), Fairlight Glen nudist beach, and Hastings Castle. Steep cliff climbs. Best in Mar–Aug. Lunch at the Queens Head in Icklesham, the Royal Oak Inn in Pett, the [C-Side cafe](https://www.csidecafe.co.uk/) in Pett Level, the [Cockles and Dreams](https://www.facebook.com/cocklesanddreams/) in Pett Level, or the [Cove Inn](https://www.thecovefairlight.co.uk/) in Fairlight Cove. 24 km.

**Rye Circular via Camber Sands**: A shorter variant of [Rye, Dungeness and Lydd-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-dungeness-lydd-on-sea/). River estuary, sand dunes, beach, and grassy marshland. Sights: Camber Sands and Romney Marsh Windfarm. Check tide timings. Lunch at the [Owl](http://theowlcamber.co.uk/) in Camber, the [Gallivant](https://thegallivant.co.uk/) in Camber, the Rye Bay Café/Bistro in Camber, or the Dunes Bar & Restaurant in Camber. 17 km.

**Rye Circular via Scots Float Sluice**: A longer variant of [Rye Figure of 8](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/rye-figure-of-8/). Medieval hilltop town, reclaimed marshland, shingle beach, riverside embankments, agricultural fields, wooded hillsides, and village lanes via Playden. Sights: [St Mary the Virgin](https://www.ryeparishchurch.org.uk/history.htm), [Rye Castle Museum](http://ryemuseum.co.uk), [Lamb House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/sussex/lamb-house), [Rye Heritage Centre](https://www.ryeheritage.co.uk), [Rye Harbour Nature Reserve](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk), Camber Castle, Castle Water, Mary Stanford Lifeboat House, Ypres Tower, Landgate, Mermaid Inn, Rye Windmill, Saltcote Place, Scots Float Sluice, St Michael Playden, Church of the Holy Spirit, and Martello Tower. Embankment path closed until 2026/27. Lunch at the [William the Conqueror](https://www.williamtheconqueror.co.uk) in Rye Harbour, the Bosun's Bite in Rye Harbour, or the [Lime Kiln Cafe](https://rye.sussexwildlifetrust.org.uk/discovery-centre/cafe) in Rye Harbour. 21 km. 4.5 hours.

0.7465822,51.1564849.ramblerNote:
**[Pluckley Circular via Dering windows](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-circular/)**: Flat Low Weald farmland, orchards, quiet lanes, ancient pubs, quaint Kent villages, and a fine viewpoint near Pluckley. Sights: Dering windows, St Nicholas Church, St Mary the Virgin, Pluckley Station, and H E Bates' home in Little Chart Forstal. Best in Apr–May. Lunch at the Swan Inn in Little Chart, the Black Horse in Pluckley, or the Dering Arms in Pluckley. 11 km.

**[Pluckley to Ashford International](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-to-ashford/)**: Pastures, orchards, woods, commons, bogs, parkland estate, and riverside parks into a town centre. Sights: Godinton Park, Hothfield Bogs, and Sheerland House. Lunch at the [Dering Arms](http://www.deringarms.com/) in Pluckley, the [Black Horse](https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/) in Pluckley, the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswaninnlittlechart.co.uk/index) in Little Chart, the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/) in Great Chart, the [Curious Brewery Taproom & Restaurant](https://curiousbrewery.com/taproom-restaurant/) in Ashford, or the [Everest Inn](https://everestinnkent.co.uk/) in Ashford. 16 km.

**Pluckley to Ham Street**: A longer variant of [Pluckley to Ashford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pluckley-to-ashford/). Pastures, orchards, woods, commons, bogs, parkland estate, and continuing Greensand Way to Ham Street. Sights: Godinton Park and Hothfield Bogs. Lunch at the [Dering Arms](http://www.deringarms.com/) in Pluckley, the [Black Horse](https://theblackhorseatpluckley.co.uk/) in Pluckley, the [Swan Inn](https://www.theswaninnlittlechart.co.uk/index) in Little Chart, or the [Swan and Dog](https://www.swananddog.co.uk/) in Great Chart. 25 km.

0.8545851,51.0681986.name: Ham Street
0.8545851,51.0681986.ramblerNote:
**[Ham Street to Appledore via Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/)**: Woodland National Nature Reserve, Royal Military Canal towpath, farm fields, vineyard, Romney Marsh flatlands, and a historic village main street. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), [Gusbourne Vineyard](https://www.gusbourne.com), St Matthew's Church, St Mary's Church, Ss Peter and Paul Church, and Miss Mollett's High Class Tea Room. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the [Woolpack Inn](https://www.woolpackwarehorne.co.uk) in Warehorne. 14 km. 3 hours.

**Ham Street Circular via Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve & Saxon Shore Way**: A shorter variant of [Ham Street to Appledore](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ham-street-to-appledore/). Woodland, canal towpath, farm fields, isolated church, and Romney Marsh flatlands. Sights: [Ham Street Woods National Nature Reserve](https://explorekent.org/activities/ham-street-woods-national-nature-reserve), [Royal Military Canal](http://www.rmcp.co.uk/the-royal-military-canal), [Saxon Shore Way](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saxon_Shore_Way), St Matthew's Church, and St Mary's Church. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Woolpack Inn](https://www.woolpackwarehorne.co.uk) in Warehorne. 12 km. 3 hours.

Lunch at the [Sportsman](http://www.thesportsmanseasalter.co.uk/) in Seasalter, or the [Poppet's Café & Tea Room](http://www.poppetscafeseasalter.com/) in Seasalter. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Faversham Circular via Uplees Marshes](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/faversham-circular/)**: Flat marshland, creek embankments, farm fields, quiet country lanes, woodland, and tidal waterways. Sights: Faversham Guildhall, Shepherd Neame Brewery, St Mary of Charity, England Coast Path, Saxon Shore Way, TS Hazard, [Oare Gunpowder Works Country Park](https://oaregunpowderworks.co.uk), [St Mary Luddenham](https://www.visitchurches.org.uk/visit/church-listing/st-mary-luddenham.html), [Oare Marshes Nature Reserve](https://www.kentwildlifetrust.org.uk/nature-reserves/oare-marshes), Uplees Marshes, Harty Ferry, Oare Mill, St Mary Magdalene and St Lawrence, and Stonebridge Pond. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Shipwright's Arms](http://www.theshipwrightsathollowshore.co.uk) in Hollowshore, the [Castle](https://www.castleoarefaversham.co.uk) in Oare, the [Three Mariners](https://www.threemarinersoare.co.uk) in Oare, or the [Cafe by the Creek](https://www.thecafebythecreek.co.uk/cafe) in Oare. 19 km. 4.5 hours.

0.9296702,51.1855902.name: Wye
0.9296702,51.1855902.ramblerNote:
**[Wye Circular via Crown Memorial](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wye-circular/)**: North Downs escarpments, chalk downland, beech and chestnut woods, open fields, riverbank along Great Stour, churchyard, and farmland. Sights: Crown Memorial, Crown Millennium Stone, Church of St Mary, Crundale, Crundale House, Chapel and Manor of Olantigh, Church of St Gregory and St Martin, and Wye College. Can be muddy. Lunch at the Timber Batts at the Compasses in Sole Street, Crundale. 17 km. 5 hours.

**Wye to Chilham**: A variant of [Chilham Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/chilham-circular/). Downland escarpment, upland fields, woods, country estate, and a hilltop village. Sights: Godmersham Park and [Chilham Castle](http://www.chilham-castle.co.uk). Lunch at the Timber Batts @ The Compasses in Sole Street. 17 km.

**Wye to Sandling**: An alternative variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Tiger Inn in Stowting, or the Five Bells Inn in Brabourne. 20 km.

**Wye Circular via Wye Nature Reserve**: A longer variant of [Wye Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wye-circular/). North Downs escarpments, chalk downland, light woodland, open arable fields, farmland, villages, and riverbank along Great Stour. Sights: Crown Memorial, Crown Millennium Stone, Wye Nature Reserve, Devils Kneading Trough, Church of St Mary, Crundale, Crundale House, Chapel and Manor of Olantigh, Church of St Gregory and St Martin, and Wye College. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Bowl Inn](http://thebowlonline.co.uk/) in Hastingleigh, or the Timber Batts at the Compasses in Sole Street, Crundale. 22 km.

**Wye to Folkestone West via NDW**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/). North Downs ridge, valley, disused railway, spectacular Channel views, and road walking at end. Sights: Channel Tunnel Terminal. Road walking at end.

0.9562415,51.8565976.name: Wivenhoe
0.9562415,51.8565976.ramblerNote:
**[Wivenhoe Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivenhoe-circular/)**: River estuary mudflats, sea wall paths, woodland, water meadows, saltmarshes, farmland, and quaint riverside villages. Sights: Nottage Maritime Institute, St Mary's Church, River Colne Flood Surge Barrier, Church of St Lawrence, Church of St Andrew, Fingringhoe Mill, and John Brunning Walk. Check ferry timetable. Lunch at the Rose & Crown in Wivenhoe, the Greyhound in Wivenhoe, the Anchor in Rowhedge, or the Ye Olde Albion in Rowhedge. 14 km. 4.5 hours.

**Wivenhoe to Clacton-on-Sea (via ferry)**: A longer variant of [Wivenhoe to West Mersea or Clacton-on-Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wivenhoe-to-west-mersea-or-clacton-on-sea/). Tidal river estuary, saltings, mud flats, disused railway trackbed, harbourfront, ferry crossing, coastal sea wall, caravan park frontages, sandy beach, and seaside town promenade. Sights: Jaywick Martello Tower and [Colne Point National Nature Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/colne-point). Check ferry times. Lunch at the No. 1 Harbour Square in Brightlingsea, the [Yachtsman's Arms](https://www.facebook.com/theyachtsmansarms) in Brightlingsea, or the Ferry Boat Inn in Point Clear. 24 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland with Channel views, cliff top promenade, shingle beach, and historic harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), [Folkestone Warren Halt railway station](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone_Warren_Halt_railway_station), Folkestone Harbour Arm, and Battle of Britain Memorial. Steep climbs near start. Lunch at the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/) in Etchinghill. 17 km.

**[Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/)**: Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, ridge with sea views, shingle beach, traffic-free seafront promenade, and cliff gardens. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch at the Earl of Clarendon in Sandgate, the Famous Ship Inn in Sandgate, the Boat House in Sandgate, the Mermaids Cafe Bar in Folkestone, or the HatHats Coffee in Folkestone. 11 km.

**[Sandling to Wye](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-wye/)**: North Downs escarpment ridge, open downland, woods, sloping fields, and fine views over Kent coast. Sights: Tickled Trout. Can be muddy. Best in Mar–May. Lunch at the Tiger Inn in Stowting, or the Five Bells Inn in Brabourne. 20 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone West**: A shorter variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Dismantled railway, chalky escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, and long pavement finish. Sights: [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch) and [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway). Long pavement to station. Lunch at the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/) in Etchinghill. 11 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via White Cliffs of Dover**: A variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, open downland, White Cliffs cliff-top path, Channel views, and beach and harbour. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Folkestone Harbour Arm. Steep climbs near start. Lunch at the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/) in Etchinghill. 20 km.

**Sandling to Dover Priory**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone or Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-or-dover/). Rambler favourite! Escarpment ridge, steep climbs, White Cliffs cliff-top path, remote chalk downland, housing estate outskirts, and Western Heights fortifications. Sights: [Folkestone](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folkestone), [Sandgate Branch](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sandgate_Branch), [Elham Valley Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Elham_Valley_Railway), Battle of Britain Memorial, White Cliffs of Dover, and Western Heights. Steep climbs near start. Lunch at the [Gatekeeper Inn](http://www.gatekeeperinn.co.uk/) in Etchinghill, or the Clifftop Cafe in White Cliffs. 21 km.

**Sandling to Folkestone Central via the Sea & Harbour Arm**: A longer variant of [Sandling to Folkestone via the Sea](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/sandling-to-folkestone-via-the-sea/). Disused railway trackbed, orchards, fields, open downland, shingle beach, seafront promenade, refurbished harbour area, historic old streets, and clifftop esplanade. Sights: Saltwood Castle, Sandgate Castle, Folkestone Harbour, Harbour Arm, Mermaid Beach, and Sunny Sands. Two steep hill climbs. Best in Jul–Sep. Lunch at the Earl of Clarendon in Sandgate, the Famous Ship Inn in Sandgate, the Boat House in Sandgate, the Mermaids Cafe Bar in Folkestone, the HatHats Coffee in Folkestone, the Harbour Arm in Folkestone, or the Steep Street Coffee House in Folkestone. 13 km.

1.1683853,51.0828121.name: Folkestone Central
1.1683853,51.0828121.ramblerNote:
**[Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Martello Towers](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/)**: Rambler favourite! Coastal cliffs, chalk downland, sea defences, wild landslip area, wartime installations, ridge between valley and sea, and town crossing. Sights: Martello Towers, Folkestone Warren, World War Two sound mirror, and Western Heights. Cracked sea defences. Lunch at the Royal Oak in Folkestone Warren. 14 km.

**Folkestone Central Circular**: A shorter variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Coastal seafront, Folkestone Warren sea defences, steep cliff climb, and cliff-top return with Channel views. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and Martello Towers. Cracked sea defences. Lunch at the Royal Oak in Folkestone Warren, or the Cliff Top Cafe in Folkestone. 13 km.

**Folkestone Central to Dover Priory via Battle of Britain Memorial**: An alternative variant of [Folkestone to Dover](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/folkestone-to-dover/). Rambler favourite! Cliff-top path, panoramic Channel views, wartime installations, and chalk downland. Sights: Battle of Britain Memorial, Cliff Top Cafe, and World War Two sound mirror. Lunch at the Cliff Top Cafe in Folkestone, or the Royal Oak in Folkestone Warren. 15 km.

1.2678629,51.8462158.name: Walton-on-the-Naze
1.2678629,51.8462158.ramblerNote:
**[Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/)**: Flat coastal walk, Victorian seaside town, red London Clay eroding cliffs, fossil-rich headland, sandy spit, salt marsh sea wall, and backwater views. Sights: [Walton Pier](https://waltonpier.co.uk/), [Naze Tower](http://www.nazetower.co.uk/), Stone Point, Hamford Water National Nature Reserve, and [John Weston Reserve](http://www.essexwt.org.uk/reserves/john-weston). Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently. Lunch at the [Naze Tower Tea Room](http://www.nazetower.co.uk/) in The Naze. 12 km.

**Walton-on-the-Naze to Frinton-on-Sea**: A longer variant of [Walton-on-the-Naze Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/walton-on-the-naze-circular/). Flat coastal seafront, beach huts, and sandy shore southward to Frinton. Check tide timings. Stone Point spit section may not be reliable — hasn't been walk-checked recently.

**[Birchington-on-Sea to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/)**: Entirely flat coastal walk; seafront promenade, raised sea wall, remote shingle beach, grassy clifftop slopes, and a seaside resort. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Herne Bay Pier, Kentish Flats offshore windfarm, and Maunsell Forts. Lunch at the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/) in Reculver, or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver) in Reculver. 14 km. 5 hours.

**Birchington-on-Sea to Whitstable**: A longer variant of [Birchington to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/). Flat coastal promenade and sea wall; shingle beach, beach huts, caravan-park front, residential clifftops, and seaside towns. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Long Rock nature reserve, and Herne Bay Pier. Lunch at the [King Ethelbert Inn](https://www.kingethelbertinn.co.uk/) in Reculver, or the [HatHats Coffee Company](https://hathats.co.uk/pages/reculver) in Reculver. 21 km.

1.3048692,51.1259357.name: Dover Priory
1.3048692,51.1259357.ramblerNote: **[Dover Priory to Deal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dover-to-deal/)**: Rambler favourite! Clifftop chalk paths, two significant climbs, level pebble beach coastpath, open sea views, and historic town. Sights: Dover Castle, South Foreland Lighthouse, [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), and [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/). Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch at the White Cliffs Visitor Centre in Dover, the Mrs Knott's Tea Room in South Foreland Lighthouse, the Pines Garden Tea Room and Museum in St Margaret's Bay, the Coastguard in St Margaret's Bay, the Zetland Arms in Kingsdown, the Deal Pier Kitchen in Deal, the Port Arms in Deal, the Kings Arm in Deal, or the Ship Inn in Deal. 17 km.
1.3048692,51.1259357.publicRamblerNote: **[Dover Priory to Deal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dover-to-deal/)**: Rambler favourite! 1.3381527,52.1043512.name: Melton
1.3381527,52.1043512.ramblerNote:
**[Melton to Woodbridge](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/)**: Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, poplar rows, quiet lanes, heathland golf course, and compact riverside town. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo), [Woodbridge Tide Mill](https://www.riverdeben.org/), Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve, and Tranmer House. Café in ticketed area. Lunch at the [Unruly Pig Pub & Kitchen](https://theunrulypig.co.uk/) in Bromeswell, or the [Ufford White Lion](https://uffordwhitelion.co.uk/) in Lower Ufford. 20 km. 4.5 hours.

**Melton Circular**: A shorter variant of [Melton to Woodbridge via Sutton Hoo](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/melton-to-woodbridge-via-sutton-hoo/). Lowland heathland, river marshes, estuary walls, reedbeds, and return via England Coast Path. Sights: [Sutton Hoo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sutton_Hoo) and Bromeswell Green Nature Reserve. Low chalk cliffs, sandy bays and coves, cliff-top grassland, seaside promenades, and historic coastal towns. Sights: Turner Contemporary Gallery, Kingsgate Castle, North Foreland Lighthouse, and Dickens House Museum. Check tide timings. Lunch at the [Captain Digby](http://www.captaindigby.co.uk/) in Kingsgate Bay, the [Botany Bay](https://www.botanybayhotel.co.uk/) in Botany Bay, the [Louisa Bay Cafe](https://www.facebook.com/Louisa-Bay-Cafe-260058987367161/) in Louisa Bay, the [Sams](https://twitter.com/dumptoncafe) in Dumpton Gap, the [Royal Victoria Pavillion](https://www.jdwetherspoon.com/pubs/all-pubs/england/kent/the-royal-victoria-pavilion-ramsgate) in Ramsgate, or the [Royal Harbour Brasserie](http://www.royalharbourbrasserie.co.uk/) in Ramsgate. 15 km.

**Margate to Herne Bay**: A longer variant of [Birchington to Herne Bay](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/birchington-to-herne-bay/). Flat seafront promenades, quiet residential clifftops, small headlands, and sandy beaches linking coastal towns. Sights: [Reculver Towers and Roman Fort](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Reculver), Turner Contemporary Gallery, and Herne Bay Pier. Sights: Sandlings Walk, Suffolk Coast Path, Havergate Island RSPB Reserve, Shingle Street, Bawdsey Manor, Orford Ness, Martello Towers, Hollesley Marshes Nature Reserve, and Butley Ferry. Long shingle sections. Lunch at the [Green Man](http://www.greenmantunstall.co.uk/) in Tunstall, or the [Froize Freehouse](http://froize.co.uk/) in Chillesford. 35 km.

1.3986457,51.2231068.name: Deal
1.3986457,51.2231068.ramblerNote: **Deal to Dover Priory**: An alternative variant of [Dover to Deal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/dover-to-deal/). Rambler favourite! Pebble beach coastpath, clifftop chalk paths, dramatic cliffs near Dover, and seaside town finish. Sights: [Deal Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), [Walmer Castle](http://www.english-heritage.org.uk/), South Foreland Lighthouse, and Dover Castle. Mobile may switch networks. Best in May–Jun. Lunch at the Zetland Arms in Kingsdown, or the Coastguard in St Margaret's Bay. 17 km.

**[Bath Spa Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bath-circular-via-2-tunnels-and-a-canal/)**: Disused railway path, tunnels, viaducts, canal towpath, river, and historic city centre. Sights: [Bath](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bath,_Somerset), [Somerset and Dorset Joint Railway](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Somerset_and_Dorset_Joint_Railway), [Combe Down Tunnel](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Combe_Down_Tunnel), [2 Tunnels Greenway](http://www.twotunnels.org.uk/), [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal), [Dundas Aqueduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dundas_Aqueduct), Prior Park, Devonshire Tunnel, and Tucking Mill Viaduct. Lunch at the [Hope and Anchor](http://www.hopeandanchormidford.co.uk/) in Midford, or the [Wheelwrights Arms Country Inn](http://www.wheelwrightsarms.co.uk/) in Monkton Combe. 19 km.

**Bath Spa to Bradford-on-Avon**: An alternative variant of [Bath Circular via 2 Tunnels and a Canal](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/bath-circular-via-2-tunnels-and-a-canal/). Disused railway path, tunnels, viaducts, and canal towpath including Dundas Aqueduct crossing. Sights: [Dundas Aqueduct](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dundas_Aqueduct) and [Kennet and Avon Canal](http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kennet_and_Avon_Canal). Lunch at the [Hope and Anchor](http://www.hopeandanchormidford.co.uk/) in Midford, or the [Wheelwrights Arms Country Inn](http://www.wheelwrightsarms.co.uk/) in Monkton Combe.

0.3061936,51.2931422.name: Borough Green & Wrotham
0.3061936,51.2931422.publicNote: **[Borough Green to Sevenoaks, via Ightham Mote](https://www.komoot.com/tour/2902569123)**: Nonstop beautiful landscape with [Ightham Mote](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/ightham-mote) — a small but charming 14th Century National Trust manor — in the middle, where you can have lunch and easily fit in a tour of the house. [Knole House](https://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/visit/kent/knole) at the Sevenoaks end of the walk is well worth a visit but requires an entire day in itself. Adapted from *The Rough Guide to Walks in London & the South East*, 3rd edition.
0.3061936,51.2931422.ramblerNote: **[Borough Green & Wrotham to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/borough-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand hills, apple orchards, woodland paths, streams, deer park, open parkland, and historic manor grounds. Sights: Plaxtol Parish Church, Fairlawne House, [Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote), [Knole House](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-knole), and Knole Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [National Trust restaurant at Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote) in Ightham Mote, or the Chaser Inn in Shipbourne. 15 km. 4.5 hours.
0.3061936,51.2931422.publicRamblerNote: **[Borough Green & Wrotham to Sevenoaks](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/borough-green-to-sevenoaks/)**: Greensand hills, apple orchards, woodland paths, streams, deer park, open parkland, and historic manor grounds. Sights: Plaxtol Parish Church, Fairlawne House, [Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote), [Knole House](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-knole), and Knole Park. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [National Trust restaurant at Ightham Mote](http://www.nationaltrust.org.uk/main/w-ighthammote) in Ightham Mote, or the Chaser Inn in Shipbourne. 15 km. 4.5 hours.

-2.3020982,54.149217.name: Horton-in-Ribblesdale
-2.3020982,54.149217.ramblerNote:
**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/pen-y-ghent-and-plover-hill/)**: Open moorland, remote valleys, engineered hill paths, ancient trading routes, rocky scrambles, boggy plateau, and sweeping Dales views. Sights: Pen-y-ghent, Plover Hill, Hull Pot, and Pendle Hill. Can be boggy. 17 km. 4.5 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Ribblehead](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/ingleborough-via-moughton-scars-and-gaping-gill/)**: Limestone pavements, caves, dramatic gorge, moorland fell, high exposed plateau, steep engineered paths, quiet valley, and Iron Age hillfort remains. Sights: Moughton Scars, Trow Gill, Gaping Gill, Ingleborough, and Ribblehead Viaduct. Steep ascent/descent. 20 km. 6 hours.

**[Horton-in-Ribblesdale to Settle](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/fountains-fell-and-catrigg-force/)**: Wild remote fell, moorland plateau, Pennine Way paths, lonely pastures, limestone country, waterfall, elevated bridleways above valley, and views of green dales and tarn. Sights: Fountains Fell, Pen-y-ghent, Malham Tarn, Catrigg Force, and Winskill Stones National Nature Reserve. Can be boggy/rough. 24 km. 6 hours.

-0.3638616,51.0508094.name: Christ's Hospital
-0.3638616,51.0508094.ramblerNote: **[Christ's Hospital to Billingshurst](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/christs-hospital-to-billingshurst/)**: Very flat farmland, re-wilding estate, woods, streams, fields, and quiet lanes. -0.9420743,51.7709259.name: Haddenham and Thame Parkway
-0.9420743,51.7709259.ramblerNote:
**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Gibraltar and Ford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-gibraltar-and-ford/)**: Flat Thame Valley farmland, tranquil hamlets, pastures, community woodland, and stately mansion grounds. Sights: Notley Abbey, Dinton Hall, [Hartwell House](http://www.hartwell-house.com/spa-cafe-and-bar/), and [King's Head](http://www.kingsheadaylesbury.co.uk/). Lunch at the [Crown at Cuddington](https://www.thecrowncuddington.com/) in Cuddington, the [Bottle & Glass](http://www.thebottleandglass.com/) in Gibraltar, the [Seven Stars](http://www.sevenstarsdinton.com/) in Dinton, or the [Dinton Hermit Inn](https://www.thedintonhermit.co.uk/) in Ford. 23 km. 5 hours.

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/)**: Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, tree-lined avenue, and river and brook paths into a market town. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch at the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/) in Waddesdon, the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/) in Waddesdon, the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/) in Waddesdon, the 94 Coffee Shop in Waddesdon, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/) in Waddesdon. 25 km. 6 hours.

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway Circular via Brill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-circular-via-brill/)**: Rolling arable farmland, hilltop villages, hayfields, meadows, pastures, and far-reaching panoramic views. Sights: Brill Windmill, Brill Common, and Chilton House. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Pointer](http://www.thepointerbrill.co.uk/) in Brill, the [Pheasant](https://www.thepheasantbrill.co.uk/) in Brill, or the [Ash Tree](https://www.theashtree.co.uk/) in Ashendon. 27 km. 6.5 hours.

**Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury Vale Parkway**: An alternative variant of [Haddenham to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/). Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, and tree-lined avenue. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch at the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/) in Waddesdon, the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/) in Waddesdon, the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/) in Waddesdon, the 94 Coffee Shop in Waddesdon, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/) in Waddesdon. 19 km.

**Haddenham and Thame Parkway Circular via Gibraltar and Ford**: A shorter variant of [Haddenham to Aylesbury via Gibraltar and Ford](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-gibraltar-and-ford/). Flat Thame Valley farmland, tranquil hamlets, pastures, and bus return along the A418. Sights: Notley Abbey and Dinton Hall. Lunch at the [Crown at Cuddington](https://www.thecrowncuddington.com/) in Cuddington, the [Bottle & Glass](http://www.thebottleandglass.com/) in Gibraltar, the [Seven Stars](http://www.sevenstarsdinton.com/) in Dinton, or the [Dinton Hermit Inn](https://www.thedintonhermit.co.uk/) in Ford. 23 km. 5 hours.

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway to Aylesbury via Waddesdon](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-to-aylesbury-via-waddesdon/)**: Thame Valley floodplain, energetic climbs, open vistas, Rothschild estate parkland, tree-lined avenue, and river and brook paths into a market town. Sights: [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/), Eythrope Park, and Upper Winchendon. Lunch at the [Waddesdon Manor](http://www.waddesdon.org.uk/) in Waddesdon, the [Five Arrows Hotel and Restaurant](https://fivearrowshotel.co.uk/) in Waddesdon, the [Bow](https://www.thebowwaddesdon.co.uk/) in Waddesdon, the 94 Coffee Shop in Waddesdon, or the [Lion](http://www.thelionwaddesdon.co.uk/) in Waddesdon. 25 km. 6 hours.

**[Haddenham and Thame Parkway Circular via Brill](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/haddenham-circular-via-brill/)**: Rolling arable farmland, hilltop villages, hayfields, meadows, pastures, and far-reaching panoramic views. Sights: Brill Windmill, Brill Common, and Chilton House. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Pointer](http://www.thepointerbrill.co.uk/) in Brill, the [Pheasant](https://www.thepheasantbrill.co.uk/) in Brill, or the [Ash Tree](https://www.theashtree.co.uk/) in Ashendon. 27 km. 6.5 hours.

-0.8293176,51.7358193.name: Monks Risborough
-0.8293176,51.7358193.ramblerNote: **Monks Risborough to Wendover**: A shorter variant of [Wendover Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wendover-circular/). Wooded hills, valleys, Chiltern escarpment, open ridges, and returning to Wendover. Sights: Pulpit Hill fort and Ellesborough Church. Can be muddy. Best in Sep–Nov. Lunch at the [Plough](http://plough-at-cadsden.co.uk/) in Lower Cadsden, or the [Russell Arms](http://therussellarms.co.uk/) in Butler's Cross. 9 km. 3 hours.

0.7586045,51.9258987.name: Chappel and Wakes Colne
0.7586045,51.9258987.ramblerNote: **[Chappel and Wakes Colne to Bures](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/wakes-colne-to-bures/)**: Undulating hills, brooks, farms, mixed woodland, fishing lakes, thatched cottages and barns, river valley, and golf course diversion. Sights: East Anglian Railway Museum, Chappel Viaduct, Chalkney Wood, Colne Priory, and St Andrew's Church. Can be very muddy. Lunch at the Five Bells in Colne Engaine. 17 km. 5.5 hours.

**[King's Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/)**: Open fields, Cotswold villages, long views, and gentle countryside. Sights: Aynho Park, Aynho Grammar School, Kings Sutton Church, Croughton Church, and Souldern House. Lunch at the [Cartwright Hotel and Restaurant](http://www.cartwright-hotel.co.uk/) in Aynho, the [Fox Inn](http://www.thefoxatsouldern.co.uk/) in Souldern, the [Blackbird Inn](https://whatpub.com/pubs/NTH/153/blackbird-croughton) in Croughton, or the [Rose & Crown](http://theroseandcrowncharlton.co.uk/) in Charlton. 17 km.

**King's Sutton to Banbury**: A longer variant of [Kings Sutton Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/kings-sutton-circular/). 0.0009329,51.7881973.name: St Margarets
0.0009329,51.7881973.ramblerNote:
**[St Margarets Circular via Hollycross Lake](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/st-margarets-circular/)**: Rolling East Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, ancient tracks, green lanes, river valleys along Ash, Rib and Lea rivers, and scenic nature reserve. Sights: Thundridge Old Church, Amwell Nature Reserve, and Hollycross Lake. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Ermines Café & Stores](https://erminesnum39.wixsite.com/ermines-cafe) in Thundridge, or the [Feathers Inn](https://www.chefandbrewer.com/pubs/hertfordshire/feathers) in Wadesmill. 24 km. 5.5 hours.

**St Margarets Circular via Amwell Nature Reserve**: A shorter variant of [St Margarets Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/st-margarets-circular/). Rolling East Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, river valleys, and figure-of-eight loop. Sights: Amwell Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequerswareside.com/) in Wareside. 12 km.

**St Margarets Circular via Thundridge Old Church**: A shorter variant of [St Margarets Circular](https://www.walkingclub.org.uk/walk/st-margarets-circular/). Rolling East Hertfordshire countryside, wheat fields, woods, river valleys, and hourglass-shaped loop. Sights: Thundridge Old Church and Amwell Nature Reserve. Can be muddy. Lunch at the [Chequers Inn](http://www.chequerswareside.com/) in Wareside. 19 km.
